history.summary: LAN-trust TLS dual-bind on the MCP gateway. Adds optional HTTPS support so strict clients (ChatGPT connector-edge, Claude.ai web, security-conscious browser extensions) that refuse plain HTTP can connect. When cfg.mcpGateway.tlsEnabled=true AND cfg.mcpGateway.tlsCertThumbprint is non-empty, NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter registers a second URL prefix https://+:tlsListenPort/ on the same URL group + request queue alongside the existing http://+:listenPort/. The same handler serves both. Cert binding is operator-managed out-of-band via scripts\Configure-LocalServerCert.ps1 (self-signed cert generation + netsh http add sslcert + firewall rule + optional service restart). /api/discovery and onboarding profiles emit the HTTPS URL only when the runtime confirms TLS is bound (Copilot-review-hardened: gates on GatewayStatus.tlsBound, not the bare config flag). Admin HTTP listener (port 7300) stays HTTP-only this iteration: it uses Winsock SimpleHttpServer, not HTTP.sys, so adding TLS there requires an SChannel rewrite that is deferred. App-layer auth remains intentionally deferred to retail (transport TLS is a separate concern from authentication; LAN trust posture intact). HTTP fallback on the existing listenPort stays available for in-LAN curl / browser-dashboard debugging tooling.
entries: feat(gateway): TLS dual-bind on the MCP gateway. New McpGatewayConfiguration fields: tlsEnabled (default false), tlsListenPort (default 8443), tlsCertThumbprint. NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::Start() now registers a second https://+:tlsListenPort/ URL prefix on the same HTTP.sys URL group when tlsEnabled. HTTP.sys multiplexes both prefixes to the same request queue + handler, so no per-route plumbing changes.

feat(gateway): new GatewayStatus.tlsBound + mcpUrlTls + tlsCertThumbprint runtime signals. tlsBound is true ONLY when the HTTPS URL prefix registered AND cfg.mcpGateway.tlsCertThumbprint is non-empty (the operator's 'I bound a cert' signal). Closes a Copilot-flagged gap where the runtime would advertise TLS purely on URL-prefix success even though HTTP.sys returns NO_ERROR when no sslcert is bound.

feat(discovery): /api/discovery DiscoveryGateway now carries mcpUrlTls + healthUrlTls + tlsEnabled + tlsCertThumbprint fields, all populated ONLY when GatewayStatus.tlsBound is true. Strict clients fetching /.well-known/mcos.json get an HTTPS URL the runtime believes is live.

feat(onboarding): OnboardingService::profileFor() prefers document.gateway.mcpUrlTls over mcpUrl when TLS is bound at runtime. Every onboarding profile (claude-code, codex, grok, chatgpt, generic) embeds the HTTPS URL in its .mcp.json snippet automatically. No per-client code change.

feat(scripts): new scripts\Configure-LocalServerCert.ps1. Generates a self-signed cert with DNS + IP SANs (localhost, machine hostname, all non-loopback IPv4) in Cert:\LocalMachine\My, exports the public-key .cer to %PUBLIC%\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\certs\, runs netsh http add sslcert against 0.0.0.0:tlsListenPort, opens a Windows Firewall inbound rule named 'MCOS MCP Gateway TLS (<port>)'. Idempotent — re-running validates SAN coverage + 90-day expiry headroom and re-mints when the SAN set drifts (DHCP/VPN-induced IP change) or expiry is close. Prints the operator-facing config snippet at the end.
feat(scripts): new scripts\Remove-LocalServerCert.ps1. Companion rollback. Removes the sslcert binding, removes the firewall rule, and optionally deletes the cert + the exported .cer.

build(installer): install(FILES) rules for both TLS scripts under DESTINATION 'scripts' so they ship in the MSI payload. Closes a Copilot-flagged gap where the scripts existed only in the repo, so post-install scripts\Configure-LocalServerCert.ps1 invocations from C:\Program Files\... failed with 'command not found'.

test(discovery): testDiscoveryDocumentJsonRoundTrip extended to assert serialization + round-trip of gateway.tlsEnabled / mcpUrlTls / healthUrlTls / tlsCertThumbprint in both TLS-on and TLS-off shapes. Future schema/serialization changes cannot silently drop the HTTPS fields strict clients depend on.
docs(version): bump 0.11.0-alpha.1 -> 0.11.0-alpha.2. last_release_commit backfilled to the v0.11.0-alpha.1 cut commit (d477571c...). Continues the v0.11.0-alpha line.
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Admin HTTP listener (port 7300) TLS support. SimpleHttpServer is Winsock-based, not HTTP.sys-based, so adding TLS there requires SChannel handshake code. Deferred — admin listener is operator-facing and typically reached from the same host (WinUI Shell, browser dashboard) where the trust boundary is local.

Cert auto-rotation. The self-signed cert generated by Configure-LocalServerCert.ps1 has 5-year validity. The reuse path now self-renews when expiry is within 90 days, but the script must be re-run for the renewal to happen. A renewal-cron PS job is a fit candidate for the next pass.
TLS for the UDP discovery beacon. The UDP-7301 beacon broadcasts an unencrypted JSON document. LAN trust posture treats this as acceptable; future iteration may sign the beacon payload.

PHASE-14 Slices B-E (MCP plugin tools, WinUI Shell DiagnosticsSectionControl, browser dashboard Diagnostics tab, SqliteDiagnosticsStore + clear-with-retention). Carried forward from v0.11.0-alpha.1 — unchanged scope.

No new runtime code beyond the v0.11.0 merge tip (94cad65). Internal alpha is intentionally MSI-only -- the zip-archive path still ships alongside as the headless / CI installer.
entries: pkg(alpha): produce Windows Installer MSI at dist/packages/release/MasterControlOrchestrationServer-v0.11.0-alpha.1-win-x64.msi via scripts/Package-MasterControlOrchestrationServer.ps1 -Preset release -Version 0.11.0-alpha.1. MSI ProductVersion 0.11.0.1 (alpha BUILD ordinal 1).

build(msi): extend installer/Build-Msi.ps1 ConvertTo-MsiProductVersion regex to accept -alpha.N and -beta.N pre-release tags alongside the existing -rc.N. Pre-v0.11.0-alpha.1 the regex rejected anything but MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH(-rc.N)? so semver-correct alpha tags threw `Unsupported package version format`. Regex now: ^(?<major>\d+)\.(?<minor>\d+)\.(?<patch>\d+)(?:-(?:alpha|beta|rc)\.(?<build>\d+))?$. Verified against 0.11.0 (.0), 0.11.0-alpha.1 (.1), 0.11.0-beta.3 (.3), 0.11.0-rc.7 (.7), and rejection of 0.11.0-alpha (no ordinal) / 0.11.0-zeta.1 (unknown prefix) / 0.11 (no patch).

docs(version): bump VERSION.json current_version 0.11.0 -> 0.11.0-alpha.1. Backfill the v0.11.0 history entry's commit field to the merge tip 94cad65c8713523038d5c12b61cee3913dc15de7 (replaces the pre-merge 'pending' placeholder).

history.summary: PHASE-14 Diagnostics module Slice A. Closes item 7 from the operator's 8-item testing-phase audit. Adds 5 operator-visible HTTP routes under /api/diagnostics/* that aggregate, query, and export events from the existing per-component events.jsonl persistent logs (runtime + supervisor + installer). Pre-v0.11.0 operators had no programmatic way to query, search, or export the persisted events; they had to manually concatenate files from C:\Users\Public\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\logs\<component>\ on disk. Slice A delivers the operator-visible HTTP surface; the SqliteDiagnosticsStore + WinUI Shell DiagnosticsSectionControl + browser dashboard Diagnostics tab + mcos-bridge MCP plugin tools land in subsequent slices B-E per the PHASE-14 plan. Minor bump per the manifest's phase-scope convention.
entries: feat(diagnostics): new GET /api/diagnostics/events route. Aggregates events from all per-component events.jsonl files under the diagnostics root (resolvePersistentLogPaths) plus their rotated .1.jsonl siblings (v0.10.21 rotation). Returns the merged stream sorted recent-first. Supports ?max= / ?severity= / ?source= filters via the v0.10.15 alias-aware extractor.
feat(diagnostics): new GET /api/diagnostics/summary route. Returns totalEvents + bySeverity map + byComponent map + latest5 array.
feat(diagnostics): new GET /api/diagnostics/self-test route. Returns the in-memory boot self-test snapshot (lastSelfTestSnapshot_).

feat(diagnostics): new GET /api/diagnostics/export?format=markdown|json route. Markdown format renders a structured doc with sections per severity (critical, error, warning, info, debug); JSON format returns the full event set as a JSON array. The markdown export caps each severity section at 50 events with a truncation note so operators can attach the file to support threads without it ballooning to MBs.

feat(diagnostics): new POST /api/diagnostics/clear route. Returns 501 with a clear message in slice A (the jsonl-aggregated path is read-only; clearing would require operator-side risk on deleting log files). A future slice with the SqliteDiagnosticsStore will support clear-with-retention.

PHASE-14 Slice C: WinUI Shell DiagnosticsSectionControl + FileSavePicker for Export Markdown / Export JSON buttons + side-panel filter UI.
PHASE-14 Slice D: Browser dashboard Diagnostics tab + Blob-download Export buttons.
PHASE-14 Slice E: SqliteDiagnosticsStore (persistent ring + schema migration + retention pass) so the /api/diagnostics/clear route can land, plus tests for store CRUD + query filters + ring eviction.
PHASE-14 completion report when slices B-E are merged.

history.summary: Three infrastructure fixes from the operator's testing-phase audit: (A) supervisor's CreateProcessW now honors pool.template_.environment instead of silently passing nullptr for lpEnvironment, (B) error/warning/critical TelemetryEvents persist to runtime/events.jsonl via Diagnostics::appendEvent (pre-v0.10.21 only info-severity boot self-tests reached the file; 168 sqlite-crash errors during testing-phase remediation were in-memory only and lost on service restart), (C) appendJsonLine rotates the persistent log file when it exceeds 50 MB so runtime/telemetry.jsonl (which had grown unbounded to 25.7 MB / 101 K dashboard-snapshots at audit time) caps at ~100 MB total per component (live + one rotated).
entries: fix(supervisor): CreateProcessW at startInstanceLocked now builds a merged env block from GetEnvironmentStringsW() + pool.template_.environment overrides and passes it with CREATE_UNICODE_ENVIRONMENT. Pre-v0.10.21 the supervisor passed nullptr so every stdio-supervised worker inherited SYSTEM's env unchanged; the v0.10.19 sqlite seed set PATH in its template but the override was a no-op until this fix.

fix(telemetry): TelemetryAggregator::recordEvent now routes Warning/Error/Critical severity events through MasterControl::Diagnostics::appendEvent so they persist to runtime/events.jsonl. Info-severity events stay in-memory-only to avoid filling disk with dashboard-snapshot chatter (the existing appendTelemetry path handles the high-volume info stream separately at runtime/telemetry.jsonl).

fix(diagnostics): appendJsonLine now checks file size before each append and rotates to <name>.1.jsonl when the live file exceeds 50 MB. Two-file rotation caps total disk consumption per component at ~100 MB. The rotation happens under the existing static writeMutex so the rename target isn't held open. If rename fails the function falls through and continues appending to the live file (better an oversized log than a dropped event).

docs(version): bump 0.10.20 -> 0.10.21 (patch -- additive supervisor env handling + additive error persistence + additive log rotation; no breaking public-API change, no new files, no removed contracts).

history.summary: POST-body unknown-key (droppedKeys) parity for the 4 remaining mutation routes that v0.10.17 deferred: /api/runtime/mcp-servers (RuntimeEndpoint), /api/runtime/subagents (RuntimeEndpoint), /api/runtime/subagent-groups (SubAgentGroupDefinition), /api/clients/{clientId}/privileges (LanClientPrivileges). Each route now parses the body in the route layer (in addition to the existing AdminApiService parse) so collectDroppedTopLevelKeys can detect operator typos against the typed model, and emits the droppedKeys array in the response body when the input carried unknown top-level fields. The HTTP status codes are unchanged; pre-v0.10.20 clients that ignore the new field stay wire-compatible.

feat(routes): POST /api/runtime/subagents same droppedKeys diagnostic against the RuntimeEndpoint model. The auto-promote-to-pool path is unchanged; subAgentDroppedKeys flows from the parse block at the top of the handler to the response emit at the bottom.

history.summary: Three operator-flagged testing-phase defects fixed: (1) sqlite catalog placeholder now reachable on a fresh install via the SYSTEM-account npm-globally-installed mcp-server-sqlite-npx binary (was crashing exitCode=1 under npx because npx couldn't load the native node_sqlite3.node module under SYSTEM's npm cache; root cause traced 2026-05-15 to a native-build/load failure not a path or arg-parse issue). (2) /api/health/summary.gateway.toolCount staleness fixed by routing ListTools() through refreshToolCatalogLocked() unconditionally; refresh self-bypasses when the cache is still valid so the hot path is unchanged. (3) mcpServerRuntimeStats[i].status and subAgentRuntimeStats[i].status now override to 'online' when readyInstanceCount > 0 so consumers keying off the status string see truth for stdio-supervised workers (the TCP-probe-derived endpoint.status said 'offline' because nothing listens on TCP -- the worker uses stdio). Live verified: dashboard reaches 26/26 reachable, sqlite stable for 60+s under supervision, gateway.toolCount updates from 68 -> 121 to match tools/list, all 23 stdio-supervised stats report status='online' instead of the prior decoupled 'offline'.
entries: fix(seed): sqlite catalog placeholder is no longer unreachable on a fresh install. v0.10.18 omitted sqlite from the npx seed block because spawning `npx -y mcp-server-sqlite-npx <db>` under the MCOS service's SYSTEM context exited with exitCode=1 within ~1s. Root cause traced to a native-module (better-sqlite3 / node_sqlite3.node) build/load failure under SYSTEM's npm cache path. v0.10.19 seeds the pool against the SYSTEM-account npm-globally-installed binary at C:\WINDOWS\system32\config\systemprofile\AppData\Roaming\npm\mcp-server-sqlite-npx.cmd, skipping the npx download + native-build path entirely. If the binary is absent the seed skips and sqlite remains a catalog placeholder (honest-unavailable-sentinel preserved).

fix(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::ListTools() now defers to refreshToolCatalogLocked() unconditionally. Pre-v0.10.19 the branch only refreshed when the cache was EMPTY, so /api/health/summary.gateway.toolCount stayed at the first-priming count for the lifetime of the process even after upsertPool() called InvalidateToolCatalog(). refreshToolCatalogLocked already self-bypasses when toolCatalogCacheValidUntil_ is in the future, so this is a no-op on the hot path.

fix(dashboard): mcpServerRuntimeStats[i].status and subAgentRuntimeStats[i].status now override to 'online' when the pool has any Ready instance. Pre-v0.10.19 these fields were derived from a TCP-probe of endpoint.host:port and stayed 'offline' for stdio-supervised workers (which don't listen on TCP). The Shell consumer used the separate .reachable bool so the visible count was correct, but consumers keying off the status string field saw stale 'offline'.
docs(version): bump 0.10.18 -> 0.10.19 (patch -- additive seed + cache-refresh fix + cosmetic status field; no breaking public-API change).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Supervisor's CreateProcessW spawn at MasterControlRuntime.cpp:8577 still passes nullptr for lpEnvironment so pool.template_.environment is ignored. Currently every stdio-supervised worker inherits SYSTEM's env. v0.10.19's sqlite seed sets PATH in the template but it's a no-op until the supervisor honors it. Architectural fix scheduled for v0.10.20 alongside the POST unknown-key parity work.

POST unknown-key parity for /api/runtime/mcp-servers (raw-body path), /api/clients/{clientId}/privileges, /api/runtime/subagents, /api/runtime/subagent-groups. v0.10.17 covered /api/clients, /api/pools, /api/telemetry/heartbeat. Scheduled for v0.10.20.
Error-severity events persistence: telemetry-aggregator error events are in-memory only; runtime/events.jsonl only persists info-severity. Scheduled for v0.10.21.
Telemetry log rotation: runtime/telemetry.jsonl grows unbounded (25.7 MB / 101 K snapshots at audit time). Scheduled for v0.10.21.

history.summary: Source-seed fix for the 4 catalog placeholders that pre-v0.10.18 advertised host:port 7101/7102/7103/7107 in the operator's Telemetry deck but had no managed pool spec to back them, so a fresh install reported reachable=false for memory / filesystem / sequential-thinking / chrome-devtools indefinitely. The operator's testing-phase audit reported the surface as '26 reported / 21 reachable' against a 26-element catalog. v0.10.18 adds a registerNpxMcpPool seed lambda alongside the existing registerWorkerPool (in-tree baseline-worker pools) seed block in MasterControlRuntime.cpp, spawning the standard @modelcontextprotocol/server-* (and chrome-devtools-mcp) packages via the operator's nodejs install at C:\Program Files\nodejs\npx.cmd. The seed runs unconditionally on every boot when the nodejs path exists so fresh installs reach 26/26 reachable on first launch; when nodejs is absent the seed skips and the catalog placeholders remain visible with their installHint, preserving the honest-unavailable-sentinel contract. Also captures the testing-phase finding that mcp-server-sqlite-npx exits with code 1 immediately when spawned under the MCOS supervisor (root cause TBD; the same package initializes cleanly when invoked outside the supervisor process tree); sqlite is deliberately omitted from this seed and remains a catalog placeholder until the next investigation slot.
entries: feat(seed): MasterControlRuntime.cpp gained a registerNpxMcpPool lambda + 4 calls (memory, filesystem, sequential-thinking, chrome-devtools) registered alongside the existing v0.9.64 batch. Each is a ManagedEndpointPool with executable = C:\Program Files\nodejs\npx.cmd, args = -y <package>, transport = stdio_jsonrpc, healthProbe.transport = stdio_handshake. Skipped at boot when npx.cmd is absent.

fix(seed): the 4 catalog placeholders previously labeled installState=awaiting_pool_registration on a fresh install now become installState=installed_and_supervised on every boot. Their tools join the gateway's tools/list catalog and contribute to the unified MCP wire.

test(live): testing-phase remediation verified live -- the 4 new pools each spawn to state=ready under MCOS Job Object supervision; dashboard mcpServerRuntimeStats reaches reachable=25/26 (sqlite remains a known placeholder).

defer(sqlite): mcp-server-sqlite-npx exits exitCode=1 immediately when spawned under the MCOS supervisor; the same package runs cleanly when invoked outside the supervisor process tree. Root cause TBD. Captured in knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease.
docs(version): bump 0.10.17 -> 0.10.18 (patch -- additive seed; no breaking public-API change; pre-existing in-tree workers and persisted pool definitions unchanged).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: mcp-server-sqlite-npx crash under MCOS supervisor (exits with code 1 within ~1s of spawn even when both the executable path and the db path contain no spaces; same package works when invoked directly via stdio outside the supervisor). Investigation slot needed.
/api/health/summary.gateway.toolCount is stale relative to the gateway MCP tools/list count; the in-process counter doesn't refresh when new pools register at boot.

mcpServerRuntimeStats[i].status field is decoupled from .reachable for stdio-supervised pool workers (status stays 'offline' while reachable=true). Shell consumer correctly uses .reachable; other consumers may see stale offline.

history.summary: POST-body unknown-key visibility on /api/clients, /api/pools, and /api/telemetry/heartbeat. Pre-v0.10.17 a POST whose JSON body carried a field name the destination model did not recognise (operator typo such as enabledFlag for enabled) returned HTTP 200 with the default value applied and zero indication anything had been dropped. The third item from the v0.10.15 debugger sub-agent scan. v0.10.17 ships a new header-only helper at include/MasterControl/JsonStrictness.h that detects dropped top-level keys via a JSON round-trip through the typed model. Each of the three POST routes now includes a droppedKeys array in the response body when the input carried unknown fields. The change is strictly additive -- HTTP status codes are unchanged, the typed model still constructs cleanly, and pre-v0.10.17 clients ignoring the new field stay wire-compatible.
entries: feat(helper): new include/MasterControl/JsonStrictness.h with inline collectDroppedTopLevelKeys<T>(input, model) and droppedKeysToJson(list). Round-trips the typed model through nlohmann::json() to discover its known keys, then lists input keys missing from the round-tripped set. Safe on non-object input (arrays, scalars, null) -- returns an empty list rather than throwing.

feat(routes): /api/clients (LanClient), /api/pools (ManagedEndpointPool), and /api/telemetry/heartbeat (ClientHeartbeat) now emit a droppedKeys array in the JSON response body when the input payload carried top-level keys the destination model did not recognise. HTTP status codes unchanged.

test(json-strictness): four new unit tests -- testJsonStrictnessDetectsTypo (operator typo verbatim), testJsonStrictnessNoDropsWhenAllKeysKnown (round-trip stable case), testJsonStrictnessSafeOnNonObjectInput (array/null/scalar safety), testDroppedKeysToJsonShape (serialisation order + size). All passing under ctest --preset debug.

docs(version): bump 0.10.16 -> 0.10.17 (patch -- additive diagnostic surface; no breaking public-API change; canonical accept-and-drop behaviour preserved for any client that ignores the new response field).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: /api/runtime/mcp-servers POST uses adminApiService_->upsertMcpServerJson(rawBody) (the raw string path); a follow-up patch can parse the body in the route layer to surface droppedKeys without changing the service contract.

/api/clients/{clientId}/privileges, /api/runtime/subagents, and /api/runtime/subagent-groups POST routes use the same .get<T>() pattern and would benefit from droppedKeys parity; deferred to keep v0.10.17 scope tight.

history.summary: SSE resume on /api/events. Pre-v0.10.16 the Server-Sent Events handler initialised its activity watermark to the empty string on every connection; a reconnecting browser EventSource client replayed from the current ring head, so any activity events that landed between disconnect and reconnect were silently lost. v0.10.16 honors the standard Last-Event-ID request header (the value the browser auto-echoes on reconnect) and additionally accepts the same ?since= alias set v0.10.15 added (sinceId/after/from/cursor/lastEventId) for manual probes. The handler now also emits the SSE id: field on every activity event so EventSource can store it and echo it back. Dashboard snapshot events stay un-id'd since they are not a resumable sequence. No public-API breakage: clients that don't send the header or query param continue to start from the live tail exactly as before.
entries: feat(sse): /api/events handler resolves an initial activity watermark from (a) the Last-Event-ID request header per WHATWG SSE, or (b) ?since= and aliases per the v0.10.15 alias set. Captured by-move into the streaming lambda so the request struct can be released as normal.

feat(sse): sendEvent helper gained an optional eventId parameter that emits an SSE id: line between event: and data:. The activity emission site passes evt.id; the dashboard emission site does not (snapshot events are not a resumable sequence).

fix(sse): lastActivityId is now initialised from the resolved watermark instead of the empty string. Pre-v0.10.16 reconnecting clients always replayed from the current ring head and silently dropped any events that landed in the interim.
docs(version): bump 0.10.15 -> 0.10.16 (patch -- additive resume support; no breaking public-API change; canonical wire surface unchanged for clients that don't send a watermark).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: POST-body unknown-key validation: nlohmann::json::parse(...).get<T>() silently drops keys not declared in the model. Affects /api/clients, /api/pools, /api/runtime/mcp-servers, /api/telemetry/heartbeat, and similar. Larger contract surface; defer to a contract-tightening pass.

history.summary: Operator-facing silent-ignore defect fix. /api/activity, /api/telemetry/events, and /api/client/activity each accepted only one canonical query-param name (max=, since=, kind=) and silently shipped the full 512-event ring (138 KB) when an operator typed a natural-language alias such as ?limit=N. The route layer's bare query.find() calls also lacked name-boundary anchoring, so a future ?xmax= param could have spuriously matched ?max=. v0.10.15 ships a single boundary-aware extractQueryParam / extractQueryParamAny helper in include/MasterControl/QueryParamParse.h, refactors the three handlers to use it, and adds five unit tests covering canonical parse, alias fallback, name-boundary guard, missing-param safety, and multi-pair queries. /api/client/activity additionally gains the full max=/since=/kind= surface that /api/activity already exposed (pre-v0.10.15 it returned the entire ring on every call). Live verified on 192.168.1.7: /api/activity?limit=5 -> 5 events; /api/client/activity?max=3 -> 3 events; /api/activity?xmax=10 -> still 512 (boundary guard).
entries: fix(routes): /api/activity, /api/telemetry/events, /api/client/activity now accept operator-natural alias query params. Canonical names still win when both are present. Alias sets: max -> {limit, count, n, top}; since -> {sinceId, after, from, cursor, lastEventId}; kind -> {type, event, filter, category}.
feat(routes): /api/client/activity gained the full max=/since=/kind= surface; pre-v0.10.15 the route returned the entire activity ring on every call regardless of query string.

fix(routes): query-param extraction is now name-boundary anchored. ?xmax=10 no longer incidentally matches a search for max=; the helper requires the name to start at position 0 of the query or immediately follow an &.

history.summary: Remediation of a Grok-authored repository audit (AUDIT-FINDINGS-2026-05-11) that flagged three classes of defects. The core runtime, contracts, and architecture passed audit cleanly (43 MCP tools, 5 sub-agents, 12 slash commands, 39/39 self-tests, the in-process HTTP.sys adapter, honest telemetry, CLU/Forsetti governance, 7-state lifecycle, no fake telemetry, no contract violations). The defects ship surface-level: hardcoded developer paths, retired-gateway documentation, and a fragile test build. v0.10.14 remediates all of them. Dispatched build-resolver and documenter sub-agents in parallel per the audit's recommended workflow.

Replaced absolute G:\Claude\... paths with relative paths (.remember/memory.jsonl, ., .claude/mcp-state/mcos-orchestration.sqlite) and replaced the hardcoded C:\Program Files\nodejs\node.exe + npx-cli.js commands with bare 'npx' so the manifest works on any clean clone in any directory. PATH env entries kept as a sane fallback. The Orchastration misspelling existed only inside the absolute paths and is gone with them.

history.summary: Server-side reachability self-check + Verify Endpoints button on the Supervisor card. Driven by a second ChatGPT supervisor-candidate connection test report (MCOS_Supervisor_Connection_Test_Report_Attempt_2) that returned 'Connection refused' on all seven probed endpoints. Diagnostic finding: every refusal in the report carried POSIX errno 111 with sub-millisecond response time (0.4-1.5ms after the first 19.7ms attempt), which is the signature of a cloud Linux runtime whose own network stack cannot route to RFC-1918 addresses -- NOT an MCOS bind failure. Verified locally: MCOS is listening on 0.0.0.0:7300 and 0.0.0.0:8080, all probes from the host hit 192.168.1.7:7300 return HTTP 200, and the gateway port returns HTTP 405 / 404 for non-/mcp paths (which proves the listener is alive -- a refused TCP connection would return no HTTP status at all). v0.10.13 ships a new GET /api/supervisor/reachability-check that probes MCOS's own loopback + LAN-IP variants from inside the runtime via the existing WinHTTP sendJsonRequest helper, plus a new 'Verify Endpoints' button on the Overview Supervisor card that calls the route and renders the per-probe roster in a copy-paste-ready text panel. Operators can now attach the verbatim server-side verdict to bug reports so the cloud-routing vs MCOS-bind distinction is unambiguous.
entries: feat(supervisor): new GET /api/supervisor/reachability-check route. Probes loopback + LAN-IP variants of /api/health, /.well-known/mcos.json, /api/supervisor/status, and the gateway mcpPath via the existing sendJsonRequest WinHTTP helper. Returns per-probe ok/statusCode/errorMessage/interpretation plus an aggregate allReachable + LAN-routable interpretation block.

feat(supervisor): added EndpointProbeResult + probeEndpoint helpers in the route layer. probeEndpoint distinguishes 'no HTTP response at all' (listener missing) from 'any HTTP response' (listener alive even if status is 4xx/5xx) so a 405 on /mcp (correct for GET) doesn't read as a failure.

feat(shell-overview): new 'Verify Endpoints' button on the Supervisor card next to Generate Config and Revoke Active. Calls /api/supervisor/reachability-check and renders the per-probe roster in a Consolas-font TextBlock so the result is copy-paste-friendly.

docs(version): bump 0.10.12 -> 0.10.13 (patch -- new public route /api/supervisor/reachability-check and a Verify Endpoints button; no breaking contract changes).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: The reachability self-check probes the URLs MCOS itself would emit; it cannot detect a remote-network routing problem (that's by definition outside the host). Operators with cloud supervisor clients still need a LAN tunnel.

history.summary: Operator-driven punch list across the WinUI Shell. Eight action items addressed in one release: (1) MCOS bind / auto-detect verification + clarified Overview wording (the runtime already binds to 0.0.0.0 and auto-detects preferredBindAddress=192.168.1.7; the Overview now reads as 'auto-detected; binding all interfaces' instead of the confusing 'configured 0.0.0.0' parenthetical), (2) Live Command Stream renders host-local HH:MM:SS instead of raw UTC so the activity log matches the title-bar clock, (3) same time fix removes the apparent 5h offset between server time and live log (Action 2 + 3 share one fix), (4) Overview 'APIs & Services + Gateway' card now also shows the resolved MCP Gateway URL (substituted from the wildcard listenHost via the new substituteWildcardInGatewayUrl helper), (5) Supervisor card gains an explicit LAN-tunnel reachability note for cloud ChatGPT runtimes -- v0.10.8 already fixed server-side config emission, this documents the operator-side network requirement, (6) Settings tab Host Controls + Guided Setup Wizards now sit side-by-side in a 2-column Grid so neither frame's buttons are full-width, (7) Guided CLU Actions duplicate card removed from CLU tab -- the six wizard buttons there routed identical workflow tokens to the same actionRequested_ handler that Settings > Guided Setup Wizards already ships, (8) new ChatGPT Control + Grok Control cards on the Overview deck mirror the Claude Code Control pattern, drop provider-specific connector JSON configs to %USERPROFILE%\Documents\MCOS\DirectAIControl, and enforce mutual exclusion across the three slots (turning one on revokes the other two).
entries: feat(shell-overview): new APIs & Services card layout (Action 1+4) -- wildcard bindAddress now renders 'auto-detected; binding all interfaces' instead of 'configured 0.0.0.0'; added 'MCP Gateway: <url>' line driven by snapshot.mcpGatewayUrl + Presentation::substituteWildcardInGatewayUrl so the operator sees the live MCP gateway URL alongside the bind address.

feat(shell-overview): new ChatGPT Control + Grok Control cards (Action 8) under the existing Claude Code Control card. Each toggles a JSON connector config at %USERPROFILE%\Documents\MCOS\DirectAIControl\<provider>-mcos-control.json; mutual exclusion across all three Direct AI Plugin Connection slots is enforced by the backend toggle handler.

feat(shell-overview): Supervisor card gains an explicit reachability note (Action 5) -- cloud ChatGPT cannot reach LAN IPs; the operator-side fix is a LAN tunnel (Tailscale / ngrok / Cloudflare). v0.10.8's server-side mcpEndpoint fix already pointed configs at LAN IPs; this documents why a cloud supervisor still sees connection_refused without a tunnel.

feat(shell-mainwindow): activity stream (Live Command Stream) timestamps now convert ISO UTC to host-local HH:MM:SS via Presentation::formatLocalClockFromIsoUtc (Actions 2 + 3). Pre-v0.10.12 the renderer substr'd the UTC stamp, producing a 5h apparent offset on a CST host between the title-bar clock and the activity log.

feat(shell-settings): Host Controls + Guided Setup Wizards live in a side-by-side 2-column Grid (Action 6); each frame is half-width and the inner buttons become naturally narrower. HorizontalAlignment=Stretch on every button keeps both columns visually balanced.

Pre-v0.10.12 those buttons routed new-mcp / new-subagent / new-subagent-group / manage-forsetti-modules / new-apple-host / guided-import tokens to the same MainWindow handler that Settings > Guided Setup Wizards calls; investigation confirmed they did nothing different. Replaced with a small 'CLU Workflow Notes' panel pointing the operator at the Settings tab for creation wizards.

Backed by new DirectAIPluginState + writeDirectAIPluginConfig + removeDirectAIPluginConfig + composeDirectAIPluginConfigBody helpers. Toggling ON any of the three Direct AI Plugin slots (claude / chatgpt / grok) forcibly revokes the other two through the existing removeClaudePluginJunction + setClaudeMcosPluginEnabled + removeDirectAIPluginConfig calls.

feat(shell-runtime): new ShellDirectAIPluginStatus struct + ShellRuntime::FetchDirectAIPluginStatus / ToggleDirectAIPlugin methods + ShellSnapshot::mcpGatewayUrl + mcpGatewayState fields populated from /api/dashboard.mcpGatewayStatus. New Presentation::formatLocalClockFromIsoUtc + substituteWildcardInGatewayUrl helpers in ShellFormatting.h.

history.summary: Supervisor-config endpoint resolution bug fix. ChatGPT supervisor-candidate runtime filed a connection-test report (handoff/MCOS_Supervisor_Connection_Test_Report.md, externally supplied) showing all five tested URLs returned `connection_refused`. Local diagnostics on the MCOS host disproved the implied 'MCOS is bound only to localhost' theory -- netstat shows the admin listener on 0.0.0.0:7300 and the native MCP gateway on 0.0.0.0:8080, both serving `/api/health` and `/mcp` (gateway port). The ACTUAL bug is in the supervisor config generation: the stamped `server.mcpEndpoint` was `http://127.0.0.1:7300/mcp` -- wrong on TWO axes. (1) `/mcp` lives on the in-process HTTP.sys adapter listener at port 8080, not on the admin/browser port 7300; GET/POST to :7300/mcp returns 404. (2) `127.0.0.1` is unreachable from any off-box supervisor client. The well-known discovery doc already advertises the correct value (`http://192.168.1.7:8080/mcp` on this host) via DiscoveryService::currentDocument()'s 5-step LAN-IP precedence chain. v0.10.8 mirrors that precedence into a new `MasterControlApplication::Impl::refreshSupervisorEndpoints()` method that resolves bindAddress -> preferredBindAddress -> primaryIpAddress -> bindAddress-literal -> 127.0.0.1, composes the gateway URL from `cfg.mcpGateway.listenPort` + `cfg.mcpGateway.mcpPath` instead of `cfg.browserPort` + '/mcp', and pushes the resolved triple (mcpEndpoint, discoveryEndpoint, fingerprintSeed) into the supervisor service via a new `ISupervisorAssignmentService::setEndpoints()` method. The route layer calls it just before /api/supervisor/assignment/select, /api/supervisor/config/generate, and the regenerate-no-providerId branch so every issued config carries the live LAN URL. The supervisor service's initialize-time fallback also moves from admin-port to gateway-port so even before the first refresh the URL is at least on the right port (host stays loopback until telemetry warms up).
entries: fix(supervisor): generated supervisor-config server.mcpEndpoint now points at the native HTTP.sys MCP gateway listener (cfg.mcpGateway.listenPort + cfg.mcpGateway.mcpPath) instead of the admin/browser port. Pre-v0.10.8 the URL was http://127.0.0.1:7300/mcp; that endpoint does not exist (the admin port has no /mcp route -- it is on port 8080).

fix(supervisor): server.mcpEndpoint and server.discoveryEndpoint now resolve to the LAN-routable IPv4 address using the same 5-step precedence chain DiscoveryService::currentDocument() uses for the well-known doc. Remote ChatGPT/Claude/Grok supervisor clients receive a URL their LAN-attached runtime can actually reach instead of a 127.0.0.1 unreachable from off-box.

feat(supervisor): added ISupervisorAssignmentService::setEndpoints(mcpEndpoint, discoveryEndpoint, fingerprintSeed). The supervisor service is constructed in initialize() before the snapshot tick has run, so the constructor-time context necessarily carries a 127.0.0.1 fallback. setEndpoints() lets the route layer push the live LAN-resolved values in just-in-time on each generate path.

feat(supervisor-routes): /api/supervisor/assignment/select, /api/supervisor/config/generate (both branches), and the regenerate-no-providerId path all call refreshSupervisorEndpoints() before invoking the supervisor service, so every issued config carries the current LAN URL.

docs(version): bump 0.10.7 -> 0.10.8 (patch -- supervisor config endpoint correctness; no public contract change, but mcpEndpoint value in issued configs changes from :browserPort/mcp to :gatewayPort/mcp and host changes from loopback to LAN-routable).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Cloud-hosted ChatGPT (chatgpt.com) cannot reach LAN IPs by definition -- only on-LAN supervisor clients (ChatGPT desktop on the same LAN, the Claude desktop connector, etc.) can use the generated config directly. A tunnel (ngrok, Tailscale, Cloudflare Tunnel) is required for cloud-hosted supervisors; operator concern, not a server bug.

/.well-known/mcos.json is served by the admin listener on browserPort. A future iteration could mirror it on the gateway port so supervisor clients only need one address to reach both discovery and MCP.

history.summary: Operator clarified that the v0.10.6 compact rendering on the Telemetry MCP + Sub-Agent panels picked the wrong reference visual. The reference is the SUB-AGENT GRID cross-tab footer at the bottom of the Overview deck -- small bordered tiles (TRON-red 1px stroke, 6px corner radius, 8x6 padding, ~150x80px each) carrying NAME (uppercase, semibold cyan) + dot, specialization, util% + ProgressBar + active/capacity ratio, and client list -- laid out side by side in a Grid and stacking to additional rows when the count exceeds the column width. v0.10.6 had instead made compact rows flat thin lists and hidden the footer on Telemetry; both were wrong. v0.10.7 rewrites the compact path in EndpointStatCardGrid.h to call a new buildFooterStyleTile<StatT> helper that produces the exact tile shape the footer builds via MainWindow::ApplySubAgentFooter, and arranges the tiles in a 7-column Grid with computed row indices so 26 MCP servers wrap to 4 rows (7+7+7+5) and 7 sub-agents fit in one row. v0.10.6's Telemetry-tab footer hide is also reverted -- the footer is the reference and stays visible on every destination.
entries: feat(telemetry-shell): EndpointStatCardGrid.h compact path now renders a multi-row Grid of SUB-AGENT GRID footer-style tiles instead of a flat row strip. Each tile matches the footer tile shape exactly: 1px TRON-red border, 6px corner radius, 8x6 padding, uppercase semibold cyan name + 8x8 dot, specialization line, util% + ProgressBar (h=4, MinWidth=60) + ratio, client list capped at 2 lines + '+N more'. 7 columns wide; tile count divides into ceil(N/7) rows.

refactor(telemetry-shell): the per-tile builder is factored into endpoint_stat_card_grid_detail::buildFooterStyleTile<StatT> so both MCP servers and sub-agents on the Telemetry deck use the same code path, and the same tile shape as the existing SUB-AGENT GRID footer.

fix(telemetry-shell): MainWindow::ApplySubAgentFooter no longer hides the SUB-AGENT GRID footer on the Telemetry destination. The v0.10.6 hide was based on a misread of the operator directive -- the footer tile shape IS the reference visual the Telemetry panels are supposed to match, so the footer stays visible on every destination.

history.summary: Two operator-reported Telemetry-deck visual bugs fixed in one release. (1) Compact MCP server + Sub-Agent rows now match the visual density of the Overview SUB-AGENTS summary card. Pre-v0.10.6 each compact row was wrapped in its own Border with TRON-red stroke, 8x4 padding, and 8px corner radius, totaling ~28-32 pixels per row; with 28 MCP servers + 7 sub-agents (live host count) the strip occupied ~1100 vertical pixels and still drew visually heavier than the Overview card. v0.10.6 drops the per-row Border in compact mode and appends the row Grid directly to the parent StackPanel inside the existing ShellCardStyle frame. Vertical padding drops from 4px to 1px, parent StackPanel Spacing is forced from 10 to 2 from C++, util ProgressBar height drops from 6 to 4, name font drops from 12 to 11. 35 endpoints now occupy ~700 vertical pixels instead of ~1100. (2) The cross-tab SUB-AGENT GRID footer (v0.7.8) was rendering below the new dedicated Telemetry Sub-Agents card, producing the operator's 'you have sub-agents in the telemetry view twice' regression report against v0.10.4. v0.10.6 hides the footer Headline + Grid on the Telemetry destination only; Overview, Runtime, Imports, Exports, Security, Settings, and CLU continue to render the cross-tab footer because they do not carry an inline per-endpoint sub-agent panel.

Each row is appended directly to the parent StackPanel inside the ShellCardStyle frame, matching the flat text-row density of the Overview SUB-AGENTS card. Row vertical padding cut from 4px to 1px; row name font from 12 to 11; util bar height from 6 to 4.

feat(telemetry-shell): when compact mode is active the parent StackPanel's Spacing is forced to 2.0 from C++, overriding the XAML default of 10. Without this the rows would carry a 10px breathing gap that re-introduces the visual heaviness the operator flagged.

history.summary: dashboard-snapshot rows in the persistent telemetry log are now throttled to once per 60 seconds. Pre-v0.10.5 every call to MasterControlApplication::Impl::snapshot() (which fires on every shell + browser dashboard poll, ~1 Hz combined under operator load) appended one row to <PUBLIC>\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\logs\runtime\telemetry.jsonl. Live measurement on this host: telemetry.jsonl reached 24 MB after ~27 hours of continuous running and would have crossed 1 GB inside 6 weeks with no rotation policy. v0.10.5 wraps the appendTelemetry call in a static atomic-counter throttle keyed on wall-clock seconds; the first call after a 60-second-or-longer gap writes; intermediate calls skip. Throughput cut ~60x to ~350 KB/day while preserving a per-minute trend for the operator. Other event types (self-test summaries, supervisor lifecycle, gateway state changes) are unaffected because they're already low-rate.
entries: fix(runtime): dashboard-snapshot writes to the persistent telemetry log now throttled to once per 60s via a static atomic compare-exchange. Cuts log growth from ~21 MB/day to ~350 KB/day. Other event categories unaffected.
docs(version): bump 0.10.4 -> 0.10.5 (patch -- silent disk-growth fix; no public contract change).

history.summary: Boot self-test now also asserts the MCP gateway is advertising at least one tool. Pre-v0.10.4 the gateway probes covered (a) the listening port via TCP probe and (b) the gateway adapter state == Running, but neither caught the case where the adapter was up and listening yet pool registration had silently failed and the tools/list response was empty. External AI clients connecting through the gateway in that state would see no tools and could not call anything, but the operator's Overview Error Frame would show all-green. v0.10.4 adds probe `gateway.tool_count_nonzero` (category=gateway): it pulls mcpGateway_->ListTools().size() and asserts >= 1. Live MCOS deployments expect ~90 tools (one per registered MCP-server / sub-agent catalog entry, summed across worker pools); 0 is a real misconfiguration. Total boot self-test count rises from 38 to 39.
entries: feat(self-test): added probe gateway.tool_count_nonzero (category=gateway) that asserts mcpGateway_->ListTools().size() >= 1. Failure message includes the actual count and a hint pointing the operator at pool registration / worker handshake state.

history.summary: Boot self-test now also probes whether the persistent Diagnostics log directory is writable. Pre-v0.10.3 if <PUBLIC>\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\logs\runtime\ was unwritable (locked-down account, missing PUBLIC env var, ACL change, etc.) all the v0.10.2 self-test diagnostic rows would be silently dropped and the operator would lose the audit trail without any visible warning. v0.10.3 adds probe number 7 (`diagnostics.log_writable`) to runBootSelfTestsNow(): it resolves the persistent log paths, ensures the component directory exists, opens a `.write-probe` file in the directory, writes a single JSON object, closes, then removes the probe. Failure at any step surfaces in the Overview Error Frame as a real failed test row. The new probe runs in the existing `telemetry` category, raising the boot self-test count to 38.
entries: feat(self-test): added probe 7 `diagnostics.log_writable` (category=telemetry) that resolves the persistent Diagnostics log directory, exercises create + write + remove, and reports a real failure if any step fails.

history.summary: Boot self-test failures and the per-boot summary now also write to the persistent Diagnostics log under <PUBLIC>\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\logs\runtime\events.jsonl. Pre-v0.10.2 the boot self-test only fed the in-memory activity ring (which the Overview Error Frame surfaces); a service restart wiped the ring and any historical self-test failures became unrecoverable. The operator directive on this surface was 'If a test fails, then it MUST be reported in the Overview Error Frame and reported to the diagnostic module' — the activity-ring path covered the Error Frame; the persistent log path now covers the diagnostic module side. emitSelfTestActivity() writes one severity=error row per FAIL; runBootSelfTestsNow() writes one severity=info-or-warn summary row per boot. PASS rows still go to the activity ring only — writing every PASS to the persistent jsonl would generate 30+ lines per boot indefinitely and the per-pass detail is recoverable from the ring when needed.
entries: feat(self-test-diagnostics): emitSelfTestActivity() now also calls MasterControl::Diagnostics::appendEvent with severity=error on FAIL. The per-failure event carries name, category, durationMs, ranAtUtc in the data block.

feat(self-test-diagnostics): runBootSelfTestsNow() summary block now also writes to the persistent Diagnostics log with severity=info on all-pass and severity=warn when any failed. The summary carries passed/failed/total counts plus startedAtUtc and finishedAtUtc.

history.summary: Supervisor lastErrorMessage now clears on operator-initiated state transitions and on load for terminal states. Pre-v0.10.1 a confirm rejection (provider mismatch / config-id mismatch / forbidden capability / capability-not-allowed) populated assignment_.lastErrorMessage and that message persisted across the operator's voluntary revoke or regenerateConfig actions, rendering as a confusing tail in the dashboard's supervisor card. Live evidence: after revoking the active ChatGPT assignment, the dashboard kept showing 'Config id does not match the active assignment.' even though the assignment was revoked seconds before. v0.10.1 calls assignment_.lastErrorMessage.clear() inside both revoke() and regenerateConfig() (the connect-success path at line 368 already cleared it; the heartbeat-watchdog disconnect path keeps its informative message intact). It also adds a load-time migration so existing on-disk records that already sit in Off / Revoked with a stale message converge to the new shape on the next service restart without any manual JSON edit. The revocationReason field continues to capture *why* the assignment ended, so operator visibility into the revoke trail is preserved.
entries: fix(supervisor): revoke() now clears assignment_.lastErrorMessage so a stale rejection from a previous failed connect attempt does not haunt the dashboard after operator-initiated revoke.
fix(supervisor): regenerateConfig() now clears assignment_.lastErrorMessage so the new configId effectively supersedes any stale rejection tied to the prior configId.

feat(supervisor-migration): loadFromDisk() now drops lastErrorMessage on load when state is terminal (Off or Revoked). Pre-v0.10.1 records with a sticky error converge on next service restart with no operator action.
test(supervisor): added testSupervisorRevokeClearsStaleErrorMessage covering rejection -> revoke -> empty-error-message round-trip.
test(supervisor): added testSupervisorRegenerateClearsStaleErrorMessage covering rejection -> regenerateConfig -> empty-error-message round-trip.

test(supervisor): added testSupervisorLoadDropsStaleErrorOnTerminalState covering legacy persisted shape (state=revoked + non-empty lastErrorMessage) and asserting load-time migration drops the message.

history.summary: Three operator-driven catalog and integration changes. (1) Docker Control MCP and Playwright MCP removed from the baseline catalog at operator direction. The default seeded MCP set shrinks from 23 to 21 entries; the DockerControl specialization, dockerControlCatalog, docker.status / docker.list_containers tools, and the docker-control id mapping are gone from the baseline-tools worker. The runtime install-hint and package-detection maps drop the playwright entry. A startup migration in FileBackedConfigurationService::ctor prunes any pre-existing seededEndpoints with id=playwright or id=docker-control from the persisted config so existing installs converge to the new catalog on next service restart, no manual JSON edits required. (2) Claude Code plugin (mcos-control) toggle now syncs ~/.claude/settings.json's enabledPlugins map alongside the directory junction. Pre-v0.10.0 the operator would toggle on, see the junction created, restart Claude Code, and find no /mcos-control:* commands or skills available because Claude Code only loads plugins whose key appears in enabledPlugins. v0.10.0 adds setClaudeMcosPluginEnabled() which writes mcos-control: true (or removes the key on toggle off) to settings.json with proper JSON parse / merge / write semantics. The /api/claude-plugin/status response now reports claudeSettingsEnabled, and /toggle reports claudeSettingsAttempted / Succeeded / LastError so the UI surfaces both the junction state and the settings.json state. (3) Catalog placeholder comment in renderRuntimeStats stripped of the playwright reference and updated for the new short list.
entries: remove(catalog): drop docker-control + playwright entries from baselineMcpCatalog in MasterControlDefaults.cpp; seed count 23 -> 21.

remove(baseline-tools-worker): drop DockerControl specialization enum value, dockerControlCatalog() function, the case in the catalog switch, the docker.status + docker.list_containers execution branch, and the docker-control id-to-specialization mapping in main.cpp.
remove(runtime): drop playwright entry from kKnownInstallCommand and from the canonical npm-package detection list in MasterControlRuntime.cpp.

feat(runtime-migration): FileBackedConfigurationService now prunes deprecated endpoint ids (playwright, docker-control) from the loaded seededEndpoints array on construction, persisting the cleaned config back to disk. Idempotent — subsequent boots are no-ops once the config no longer contains the deprecated ids.

feat(claude-plugin): /api/claude-plugin/toggle now also flips ~/.claude/settings.json's enabledPlugins[mcos-control] entry alongside the directory junction. setClaudeMcosPluginEnabled() does best-effort settings.json read / merge / write; failure does not fail the toggle (junction is the authoritative state) but is reported in claudeSettingsLastError.

feat(claude-plugin): /api/claude-plugin/status now reports claudeSettingsEnabled (parsed from ~/.claude/settings.json) and claudeSettingsReadError so the operator sees at a glance whether Claude Code will mount the plugin even though the junction exists.
fix(comment): catalog-placeholder comment in renderRuntimeStats updated to drop the playwright example.

The settings.json key form for the local mcos-control plugin is the bare 'mcos-control' (no @marketplace suffix). If a future Claude Code version requires a different key form for local plugins, setClaudeMcosPluginEnabled() will need a corresponding update.

history.summary: Three operator-visible fixes on the WinUI Shell after the v0.9.98 stale-XAML fix unblocked actual visibility. (1) Self-Tests + Error Reporting cards lifted to the TOP of Overview as a side-by-side row right under the Hero header. Pre-v0.9.99 both cards existed but lived below five other sections (Claude Code Control + Operational Snapshot + Environment & Configuration grid + 2x2 APIs/Security/MCP/Sub-Agents status grid) — operator had to scroll past ~1500 pixels to see them. The original directive on this surface was 'MCOS MUST perform visible self tests' and 'If a test fails, then it MUST be reported in the Overview Error Frame'; v0.9.99 makes them literally the first thing visible on the Overview tab. (2) EndpointStatCardGrid compact mode is now a TRUE single-line card layout, not just tighter padding/fonts. Pre-v0.9.99 'compact' shrunk fonts but still emitted 5-6 separate rows per card (header / util / endpoint / pool note / clients header / clients list) totaling ~140 pixels tall — with 28 MCP servers on the Telemetry deck the operator saw ~4000 pixels of cards. v0.9.99 collapses to a single 28-pixel-tall horizontal row per card: [reachability dot] | Name (spec) | [util bar] | util% | active/cap. 35 cards now fit in ~1000 pixels.
entries: fix(shell-overview): Self-Tests + Error Reporting cards lifted to a 2-column row directly under the Hero header. Old positions (mid-page Self-Tests + bottom-of-page Error Reporting) removed. Same x:Name elements so the existing ApplySelfTestCard / ApplyErrorReportingCard handlers continue to populate them without code change.

fix(shell-cards): EndpointStatCardGrid compact mode now emits a single horizontal Grid row per card -- skips the multi-row build entirely. Card height drops from ~140 pixels to ~28 pixels. Telemetry MCP and sub-agent decks are now actually compact, matching the operator directive 'close in size to the current Sub-Agent Cards'.

feat(shell-cards): single-line cards still carry every operator-relevant signal: reachability dot color (green/red), name + specialization (with CharacterEllipsis trimming on overflow), util ProgressBar with tone color, util%, active/capacity counter.

history.summary: REGRESSION FIX -- Deploy-LocalLive.ps1 was never copying the compiled XAML resources (.xbf), the merged resource index (.pri), or the WinRT metadata (.winmd) alongside MasterControlShell.exe. The pre-v0.9.98 script copied the shell .exe + web assets only, so every XAML / WinRT change shipped between v0.9.76 (the operator-named directive set: bind-address fix, compact MCP cards, system tray icon, Supervisor Wizard card) and v0.9.97 (Issued line on the Supervisor card) was invisible on the operator's live install. The shell binary was fresh; the .xbf files were from 5/8/2026 (before v0.9.76). The shell's FindName('TelemetryMcpServersCardStack') silently failed against the stale .xbf, leaving PopulateMcpServerCards a no-op, which is exactly what the operator saw: 'The MCP server cards are missing from telemetry view.' Same regression hit the Overview Supervisor card, the Telemetry sidebar Supervisor card, the Supervisor detail row, every relative-time / Issued / expires reformat, the tray-menu Open Browser entry (no -- that's WndProc, not XAML), etc. Bind-address text edits to existing TextBlocks were visible because the TextBlock.x:Name was already on the stale .xbf; only NEW XAML elements failed. The fix is one block in Deploy-LocalLive.ps1: glob *.xbf / *.pri / *.winmd from the shell's build output directory and copy each to the live install root.
entries: fix(deploy): scripts/Deploy-LocalLive.ps1 now copies every *.xbf, *.pri, and *.winmd from <build>/src/MasterControlShell/Debug/ into the live install root alongside MasterControlShell.exe. The .xbf files are the compiled XAML resources the shell loads via WinUI's resource loader; without them the shell falls back to whatever .xbf was last shipped (5/8 in this operator's case, predating v0.9.76).
fix(deploy): logs each .xbf / .pri / .winmd copy at info level so the operator can confirm the new resources landed during the next deploy.

history.summary: Future-direction relative-time formatter for the WinUI Shell. v0.9.90 added formatRelativeUtcTime for past timestamps (heartbeat); v0.9.94 adds the symmetric formatFutureUtcTime for future timestamps. Renders 'in 2h 14m' / 'in 47m 30s' / 'in 3d 4h' for not-yet-elapsed stamps. Past stamps render 'expired Xs ago' (delegating to formatRelativeUtcTime) so a clock-skewed or already-elapsed timestamp shows up correctly instead of as 'in 0s' or a negative duration. The Overview Supervisor card's detail row now uses this for the assignment's expiresAtUtc: pre-v0.9.94 it showed 'Expires (UTC): 2026-05-10T21:36:10Z'; v0.9.94 shows 'Expires: in 2h 14m (2026-05-10T21:36:10Z)' so the operator sees the relative timing at a glance with the raw stamp for cross-check.
entries: feat(shell-formatting): formatFutureUtcTime(isoStampUtc) helper symmetrical to v0.9.90's formatRelativeUtcTime. Renders 'in Xs' / 'in Xm Ys' / 'in Xh Ym' / 'in Xd Yh' for future stamps; falls through to 'expired X ago' for past stamps via formatRelativeUtcTime.

history.summary: Completeness pass on the v0.9.92 persistent-diagnostics dual-emit. v0.9.92 wired supervisor_select / supervisor_revoke / supervisor_connect / supervisor_connect_rejected / supervisor_disconnected to MasterControl::Diagnostics::appendEvent but missed two cousin events that fire on the /api/supervisor/config/generate route: supervisor_config_issue (when generate is called with a providerId in the body, i.e. select-and-issue) and supervisor_config_regenerate (when generate is called with empty body, i.e. re-issue config for the existing assignment). v0.9.93 closes those two paths so every supervisor_* event the activity ring sees also lands in <PUBLIC>\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\logs\supervisor\events.jsonl. Persistent audit trail is now complete across all seven supervisor_* kinds.

history.summary: Supervisor lifecycle events now dual-emit to the persistent Diagnostics module shipped at include/MasterControl/MasterControlDiagnostics.h. Pre-v0.9.92 supervisor_select / supervisor_revoke / supervisor_connect / supervisor_connect_rejected / supervisor_disconnected landed only in the in-memory activity ring + its activity.jsonl spillover. Operators who restart MCOS lose the in-memory tail of the ring; the activity.jsonl on disk only catches the last few thousand events ring-trimmed to size. The persistent Diagnostics module (already used by the WindowsHostTelemetryService dashboard-snapshot path) writes append-only per-component .jsonl files under %PUBLIC%\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\logs\<component>\events.jsonl that survive every restart and never trim. v0.9.92 dual-emits supervisor events into the 'supervisor' component path so the operator audit trail for supervisor lifecycle is durable. The original directive on this surface called out the diagnostic module specifically: 'If a test fails, then it MUST be reported in the Overview Error Frame and reported to the diagnostic module.' The supervisor surface now satisfies that contract for lifecycle failures + successes.
entries: feat(supervisor): /api/supervisor/assignment/select dual-emit to MasterControl::Diagnostics::appendEvent (component='supervisor', event='supervisor_select', severity=info/error).
feat(supervisor): /api/supervisor/assignment/revoke dual-emit (severity='warn', includes reason + assignmentId + providerId + actor).
feat(supervisor): /api/supervisor/connect/confirm dual-emit (severity=info on success, error on rejection, includes capabilities array + configId + clientId).

history.summary: Browser dashboard Supervisor card now uses the same relative-time formatting on lastHeartbeatUtc that the WinUI Shell got in v0.9.90. JavaScript helper formatRelativeUtcTime() in resources/web/app.js mirrors the shell's behavior: parses the ISO-8601 stamp via Date.parse, computes delta against Date.now(), renders in operator-friendly buckets (Xs / Xm Ys / Xh Ym / Xd Yh). Empty heartbeat renders '(none yet)'; parse failure falls back to raw UTC; future stamps render 'just now'. renderSupervisorPanel connected branch now reads 'Last heartbeat: 5s ago' instead of 'Last heartbeat (UTC): 2026-05-11T00:57:59Z'.
entries: feat(dashboard): formatRelativeUtcTime(isoStampUtc) JavaScript helper in app.js. Mirrors the WinUI Shell formatRelativeUtcTime in ShellFormatting.h (shipped v0.9.90).

history.summary: Relative-time formatting for supervisor lastHeartbeatUtc on the WinUI Shell. Pre-v0.9.90 both the Overview Supervisor card and the Telemetry sidebar Supervisor card surfaced the heartbeat as a raw ISO-8601 UTC stamp ('Last heartbeat (UTC): 2026-05-11T00:57:59Z') which requires the operator to do mental arithmetic against 'now' to know whether the supervisor is heartbeating recently or wedged. v0.9.90 adds `formatRelativeUtcTime` in ShellFormatting.h that parses the ISO stamp, computes delta seconds, and renders in operator-friendly buckets ('5s ago', '2m 14s ago', '1h 23m ago', '3d 4h ago'). Falls back to the raw UTC stamp when the parser doesn't recognize the format. Empty heartbeat (just-connected, no heartbeat tick yet) renders as '(none yet)' instead of an awkward '0s ago'. Both Supervisor card surfaces wired through the helper.
entries: feat(shell-formatting): formatRelativeUtcTime(isoStampUtc) helper. Parses %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ via std::get_time + _mkgmtime, computes delta against system_clock::now(), buckets into seconds/minutes/hours/days with adaptive precision ('5s ago' / '2m 14s ago' / '1h 23m ago' / '3d 4h ago'). Empty + parse-failure inputs return empty wstring so callers fall back cleanly.
fix(shell-overview): ApplySupervisorCard connected branch heartbeat phrase now reads via formatRelativeUtcTime. Empty heartbeat renders '(none yet)'; parse failure falls back to raw UTC.

history.summary: Server-side kind filter on /api/activity. Pre-v0.9.89 a dashboard or external monitor that wanted a slice of the activity ring (e.g. only supervisor_* events for an audit panel) had to pull the full last-N events and filter client-side, which wastes bandwidth on busy hosts and loses earlier-bucket matches when N is small. v0.9.89 adds `?kind=` query support: `?kind=supervisor_connect` for exact match, `?kind=supervisor_*` for prefix wildcard. The filter is server-side and reads up to 4x the requested max from the ring so the filter doesn't starve the response when most events are not the requested kind. Response gains an echo `kindFilter` field when a filter is active.
entries: feat(routes): /api/activity accepts `?kind=<value>` query param. Exact match by default; trailing '*' becomes a prefix wildcard. Server-side filter so cross-LAN dashboards get only the kinds they care about.

feat(routes): when filtering, the read window expands to min(kCapacity, maxCount*4) so a `?kind=supervisor_* &max=10` query doesn't return an empty array just because the last 10 ring events were all self_test rows.
feat(routes): response gains `kindFilter` echo field (e.g. 'supervisor_*' or 'self_test') so consumers can confirm the server actually applied the filter.
docs(version): bump 0.9.88 -> 0.9.89 (additive query parameter; patch bump per scheme since the route shape is backward-compatible -- queries without `kind=` behave exactly as before).

history.summary: Provider-specific supervisor instructions now include a concrete curl example for the connect/confirm step. The operator handing the saved config off to a LAN client may not know the exact JSON body shape /api/supervisor/connect/confirm expects (configId / providerId / clientId / capabilities array / fingerprint). v0.9.88 emits a copy-paste-ready curl one-liner with the actual configId, providerId, clientId placeholder, full default capability set, and serverFingerprint already filled in. The curl line ends each provider's existing step list, joining the existing 'install into client / set up endpoints / enable heartbeats' sequence with a literal command that the operator can paste into a shell on the LAN client machine to drive the connect/confirm round-trip.

Used by all three provider-specific instruction branches.

history.summary: /api/health/summary now includes a supervisor block + supervisor error state contributes to overall ok flag. Pre-v0.9.87 the summary covered gateway + pools + activity ring + host telemetry but ignored the supervisor surface entirely. An operator polling /api/health/summary for a green light wouldn't see a supervisor in 'error' state even though it indicates a real handshake failure. v0.9.87 adds a `supervisor` block with state, activeProviderId, active flag, and lastErrorMessage, and treats `state == 'error'` as an ok=false contributor. Off, revoked, and disconnected are not errors (operator intent / heartbeat gap respectively) so they don't trip overall ok.

fix(routes): /api/health/summary `ok` field now requires supervisor.state != 'error' alongside the existing gateway/pools/ring/telemetry conditions. Off / config_generated / pending_connection / connected / disconnected / revoked all evaluate as ok=true (they are valid lifecycle states, not error states).

Behavior is byte-identical to v0.9.85 -- this is purely a code-shape clean-up that pays down the deferred item flagged in the v0.9.85 release report.

history.summary: Continuation of the v0.9.84 wildcard-bind URL-leak hunt. v0.9.84 fixed /api/health/summary. v0.9.85's bug-hunt probe found two more routes still leaking 0.0.0.0 in their response bodies: /api/gateway/status and /api/gateway/health. Both return GatewayStatus / GatewayHealth structs that carry mcpUrl + message fields populated by the native HTTP.sys adapter with the raw listenHost (typically 0.0.0.0). Same substitution chain (bindAddress -> activeProfile.preferredBindAddress -> 127.0.0.1) now applied. Status.message also gets the substitution because it embeds the URL in its narrative text ('the in-process HTTP.sys adapter listening on http://0.0.0.0:8080/mcp...'). Both routes also gain an mcpUrlRaw diagnostic field matching the v0.9.84 /api/health/summary shape.
entries: fix(routes): /api/gateway/status now substitutes wildcard hosts in status.mcpUrl + status.message before serializing. New mcpUrlRaw field surfaces the original unsubstituted URL for diagnostic UIs.
fix(routes): /api/gateway/health same substitution + mcpUrlRaw field.
fix(routes): substitution helper also walks narrative text (http://0.0.0.0... embedded in status messages) so the operator-visible message line gets the resolved URL too.

history.summary: Bug found during v0.9.83 bug-hunt probe. The /api/health/summary endpoint surfaced gateway.mcpUrl as 'http://0.0.0.0:8080/mcp' — the raw composeMcpUrl(listenHost) value from the in-process HTTP.sys adapter adapter, with the wildcard bind embedded directly in the URL. External clients (e.g. a remote AI supervisor polling /api/health/summary) couldn't route to 0.0.0.0. The discovery handler already applies a wildcard-to-LAN-IP substitution (v0.9.0+); the health-summary route was missed when /api/health/summary shipped. v0.9.84 wires the same substitution chain into the health-summary route: bindAddress -> activeProfile.preferredBindAddress -> 127.0.0.1 fallback. The raw URL is still surfaced as gateway.mcpUrlRaw for diagnostic purposes (operator-facing UIs can show 'listening on 0.0.0.0, reachable at 192.168.1.7' the way the shell already does for bind addresses). No service restart required for existing clients — the change is purely on the response side.
entries: fix(routes): /api/health/summary gateway.mcpUrl now goes through a wildcard-substitution lambda that mirrors the discovery handler's lanIp resolution chain. Wildcard binds (0.0.0.0 / [::] / [::0]) are substituted with the resolved LAN IP before the URL hits the response.

feat(routes): /api/health/summary adds gateway.mcpUrlRaw which exposes the unsubstituted URL for diagnostic purposes (the operator-facing shell + dashboard already render both 'resolved' and 'listening on' lines via the v0.9.76 bind-address helper; this lets the health-summary surface the same pair).
docs(version): bump 0.9.83 -> 0.9.84 (correctness fix; patch bump per scheme since gateway.mcpUrl was already a string field — the change is value-only).

history.summary: Operator-facing improvement: the generated supervisor config's `instructions` block is now provider-specific. Pre-v0.9.83 every provider got the same four generic 'move this file / import it / connect / confirm' steps which were actionable for none of them. v0.9.83 emits tailored guidance for each provider: Claude gets the ~/.claude.json mcpServers + Claude Desktop streamable-http setup, ChatGPT gets the Custom Connector / Custom MCP App path, Grok gets the MCP-bridge wording. Each provider's steps name the live `server.mcpEndpoint` from the running gateway so the operator copying the steps into the receiving client doesn't have to cross-reference the rest of the config. New backend test `testSupervisorInstructionsAreProviderSpecific` verifies each provider's instructions contain a marker string unique to that path (Claude Code / Custom Connector / Grok MCP bridge).

history.summary: Small correctness fix on the v0.9.79 'Open Browser Dashboard' tray entry. Pre-v0.9.82 the entry's ShellExecuteW called the hardcoded URL http://127.0.0.1:7300/, which silently broke whenever the operator had configured a non-default browser port via Settings or via the bindAddress field. v0.9.82 reads the current snapshot's dashboardUrl field instead, which is already constructed by ShellRuntime with wildcard substitution + LAN-routable IP resolution + the live browser port. Falls back to 127.0.0.1:<live-browser-port> when the snapshot is unpopulated (very early shell startup), and finally to the legacy 127.0.0.1:7300 only when browserPort is also 0 (impossible in practice).
entries: fix(shell-tray): Open Browser Dashboard tray entry now uses self->currentSnapshot_.dashboardUrl (with self->currentSnapshot_.browserPort as the fallback) instead of the hardcoded http://127.0.0.1:7300/. Honors operator-configured ports + wildcard substitution.

history.summary: Security hardening on the supervisor connect/confirm capability validator. Pre-v0.9.81, `confirmConnection` evaluated capability claims with `if (!allowedSet.empty() && allowedSet.count(cap) == 0)`. When the assignment's mode is one whose `capabilitiesForMode()` returns an empty list (notably Disabled and BreakGlassAdmin which aren't pre-populated), the short-circuit accepted any capability that wasn't on the forbidden list. That violates the default-deny security contract specified in MCOS_Supervisor_Agent_Assignment_Wizard_Package/docs/SECURITY_AND_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md ('MCOS must enforce permissions server-side. Do not rely on UI hiding or model self-restraint'). v0.9.81 changes the check to `if (allowedSet.count(cap) == 0)` so an empty allowed list rejects every claim. The Wizard surface still defaults to autonomous_supervisor mode which provides the full 8-capability allowed list, so the operator-driven happy path is unchanged. The change only tightens the fallback path. Added testSupervisorConfirmDefaultDenyOnEmptyAllowedSet regression test that tampers the persisted assignment to mode=disabled with empty allowedCapabilities and verifies even a benign capability claim is rejected.
entries: fix(supervisor): SupervisorAssignmentServiceImpl::confirmConnection rejects every capability claim when the mode's allowed list is empty (default-deny). Pre-v0.9.81 short-circuit acted as default-allow for any non-forbidden capability.
fix(supervisor): error message now names the mode explicitly: `Capability not allowed in current mode (<mode>): <cap>`.

test(supervisor): testSupervisorConfirmDefaultDenyOnEmptyAllowedSet -- tampers a persisted assignment to mode=disabled + allowedCapabilities=[], rehydrates the service, confirms a benign claim is rejected with the not-allowed error path.
test: <fstream> + <sstream> includes added to MasterControlOrchestrationServerTests.cpp for the tamper-and-rehydrate test.

history.summary: Build-system convenience: new DEPLOY_LOCAL_LIVE custom target wrapping scripts/Deploy-LocalLive.ps1. `cmake --build --preset debug --target DEPLOY_LOCAL_LIVE` now builds the active preset's runtime targets (MasterControlServiceHost, MasterControlShell, etc.), then in the same invocation invokes the deploy script with -BuildDir <cmake-binary-dir> -RelaunchShell. The script stops the service, kills any running shell, copies fresh artifacts into the spaces-path live install, restarts the service, waits for /api/version, probes self-tests + supervisor, and relaunches the shell. add_dependencies(DEPLOY_LOCAL_LIVE ${MASTERCONTROL_RUNTIME_TARGETS}) so the target rebuilds anything stale before deploying. Set into the IDEValidationTargets folder so it shows up alongside IDE_PACKAGE / IDE_ACCEPTANCE_* in Visual Studio. The Deploy-LocalLive.ps1 script handles the original install-path-mismatch root cause of the 'server will not start' report; v0.9.80 makes it a one-command developer workflow.
entries: feat(build): new DEPLOY_LOCAL_LIVE custom target in CMakeLists.txt. Depends on the MASTERCONTROL_RUNTIME_TARGETS set so a fresh build precedes every deploy. Invokes scripts/Deploy-LocalLive.ps1 with -BuildDir <CMAKE_BINARY_DIR> -RelaunchShell. Lives in the IDEValidationTargets folder alongside IDE_PACKAGE.

Read-only; the toggle group + Generate / Revoke buttons stay on the Overview surface per the operator's WinUI-is-priority directive. Mirrors the Overview card's six-state lifecycle.

history.summary: Two deferred items from v0.9.76 / v0.9.77 land in v0.9.78: (1) Supervisor heartbeat watchdog. New POST /api/supervisor/heartbeat lets a connected supervisor client check in; the runtime's runInteractive loop now ticks a 5s sweep that asks SupervisorAssignmentService::expireConnectionIfStale(120s) to flip Connected -> Disconnected when no heartbeat lands within the window. The transition emits a supervisor_disconnected ActivityEvent (kind=supervisor_disconnected, statusCode=504) so the Overview Error Reporting card and /api/activity surface the lifecycle change. Two new backend tests (idle-state no-op, stale-heartbeat flip + idempotency). (2) Operator-facing scripts/Deploy-LocalLive.ps1 helper. Resolves the install-path mismatch root cause of the 'the server will not even start' report: the cmake install prefix is the no-spaces path; the live service binary is at the spaces path. The script stops the service, kills any running shell, copies MasterControlServiceHost.exe + MasterControlShell.exe + app.js + styles.css into the spaces-path live install, restarts the service, waits for /api/version, probes self-tests + supervisor, and optionally relaunches the shell. Run with -RelaunchShell after every successful build.
entries: feat(supervisor): ISupervisorAssignmentService::expireConnectionIfStale(threshold). Parses lastHeartbeatUtc (ISO-8601), compares against now via _mkgmtime, transitions Connected -> Disconnected when stale. No-op for any other state. Returns true if a transition occurred so the runtime can audit-log.
feat(routes): POST /api/supervisor/heartbeat records the heartbeat and returns the fresh status snapshot. Route registered in the advertised route table.
feat(runtime): runInteractive() now sweeps the supervisor service every 5s with a 120s heartbeat threshold; emits a supervisor_disconnected ActivityEvent on transition.

test(supervisor): testSupervisorHeartbeatWatchdogIdleStateNoTransition + testSupervisorHeartbeatWatchdogFlipsConnectedToDisconnected. Tests #5 and #6 of the watchdog-specific batch verify no-op for Off/ConfigGenerated, flip for stale Connected, and idempotency on Disconnected.

feat(deploy): scripts/Deploy-LocalLive.ps1. Stops MasterControlProgram + MasterControlShell, copies fresh build artifacts into the spaces-path live install, restarts the service, waits for /api/version, runs self-tests + supervisor smoke. -RelaunchShell optional flag relaunches the shell after restart. Idempotent.

history.summary: Two follow-ups on top of v0.9.76: (1) browser dashboard Supervisor Agent parity with the WinUI Shell card shipped in v0.9.76. New renderSupervisorPanel on the Overview deck reads state.supervisorStatus (added to the parallel refreshAll fetch), renders three single-selection radio rows for ChatGPT / Claude / Grok, a Generate Config & Save button that POSTs /api/supervisor/config/generate then triggers a browser Blob download with the canonical filename, and a Revoke Active button. Status row mirrors the same six-state lifecycle the shell uses (off / config_generated / pending_connection / connected / disconnected / revoked / error). (2) Operator-visible documentation in the v0.9.77 release report on the CMake install-prefix vs live-service-path mismatch: cmake --install writes to C:\Program Files\MasterControlOrchestrationServer\ (no spaces), but the Windows service binary path is C:\Program Files\Master Control Orchestration Server\ (with spaces). The WiX MSI installer (MasterControlOrchestrationServerSetup.exe) bridges the two; for hot-patching during development, copy MasterControlServiceHost.exe + MasterControlShell.exe + the share/ tree from the cmake install root into the spaces-path manually. The pre-session OLD MasterControlShell.exe in the spaces-path caused the crash the operator reported ('the server will not even start'); the v0.9.76 service binary is fine.
