history.summary: Five operator directives in one release: (1) Bind-address consistency on the WinUI Shell. The pre-v0.9.76 shell rendered snapshot.bindAddress raw across four cards (Overview ApiServices, Telemetry EnvironmentProfile, Security BindAddressText, Security narrative); when the operator configured bindAddress=0.0.0.0 the cards showed a literal '0.0.0.0' that no LAN client can connect to. v0.9.76 adds resolveDisplayBindAddress in ShellFormatting.h that substitutes wildcard binds with snapshot.primaryIpAddress, mirroring the v0.9.74 dashboard fix. Editor surfaces (Settings, Setup Wizard) keep raw values so operator edits round-trip. (2) Telemetry MCP-server / sub-agent cards rendered at compact density. EndpointStatCardGrid.h gains a `compact` parameter; TelemetrySectionControl passes true so the two decks render at matching, smaller size with tighter padding (10/8 vs 14/12), smaller header font (13 vs 16), slimmer progress bar (8 vs 10), and the empty-state 'No active clients' line hidden. Runtime + Overview cards keep wide density. (3) WinUI-safe system tray icon with hide-on-close. The v0.9.72 attempt subclassed the WinUI 3 main window's WndProc, which crashed the shell on launch (verified by minidump). v0.9.76 implements the supported pattern: a HWND_MESSAGE-parented stub window owns the Shell_NotifyIcon callback WndProc, and AppWindow.Closing intercepts the close button (Cancel=true + Hide). Right-click menu: Show / Stop Server / Restart Server / Close MCOS Shell. Service runs while the shell is hidden. (4) Supervisor Agent Assignment Wizard end-to-end. New SupervisorAssignmentService backs six routes (/api/supervisor/{assignment,assignment/select,assignment/revoke,config/generate,connect/confirm,status}), persists to <data>/supervisor-assignment.json, mints SUP- + CFG- ids with random hex suffix, defaults to autonomous_supervisor mode + 8 capabilities, rejects forbidden tools (worker.run_shell, admin.*) at connect-confirm, and audit-logs every transition. New WinUI Overview card with three single-selection ToggleSwitches (ChatGPT/Claude/Grok), Generate Config & Save button (calls service then opens IFileSaveDialog), Revoke Active button. (5) Five small visibility additions to keep the operator informed about all of the above on every snapshot tick.
entries: feat(shell-formatting): resolveDisplayBindAddress + isWildcardBindAddress helpers in ShellFormatting.h; formatSecurityNarrative routes through them.
fix(shell-overview): ApplyApisAndServicesCard 'Bind:' line now uses resolveDisplayBindAddress with snapshot.primaryIpAddress; appends '(configured 0.0.0.0)' suffix when wildcard substitution applied.
fix(shell-telemetry): EnvironmentProfile card 'Bind address:' line uses the helper; adds 'Listening on:' suffix line when wildcard substitution applied.
fix(shell-security): BindAddressText uses the helper; surfaces both resolved and configured values when wildcard.

feat(shell-cards): EndpointStatCardGrid::renderEndpointStatCardGrid gains optional `compact` parameter (default false). Compact mode shrinks card padding, header/util fonts, progress-bar height, util-row column widths, and inner spacing; hides 'No active clients' empty-state line.

fix(shell-telemetry): PopulateMcpServerCards / PopulateSubAgentCards pass compact=true so MCP and sub-agent decks render at matching smaller size on the Telemetry surface. Runtime + Overview surfaces keep wide density.

fix(shell-tray): WinUI-safe tray icon. New TrayHostWndProc lives on a dedicated HWND_MESSAGE stub window; tray callback never enters the WinUI 3 main-window WndProc. AppWindow.Closing handler hides the window on close button click; Close MCOS Shell tray command sets exitRequested_ + posts WM_CLOSE for true exit. Restored InstallTrayIcon / RemoveTrayIcon call sites in ConfigureWindow.

history.summary: Self-tests are now VISIBLE. v0.9.69 added the boot self-test framework (37 probes against admin port + gateway state + every supervised pool + activity-ring persistence + telemetry sampler liveness + on-disk worker exe presence) but only surfaced failures through the existing v0.8.7 Error Reporting frame (statusCode>=400 harvest from /api/activity). Operators couldn't see the PASS rows. v0.9.75 adds a dedicated SELF-TESTS card on both the WinUI shell Overview surface and the browser dashboard Overview surface. Both pull from GET /api/self-tests every snapshot tick so the per-probe pass/fail roster updates in real time. A Re-run button on each surface POSTs /api/self-tests/run and replaces the panel with the freshly-computed snapshot immediately, without waiting for the next tick.

feat(shell-ui): OverviewSectionControl gains a SELF-TESTS card with status dot, headline, scrollable per-probe roster (failures float up, alphabetical within bucket), and Re-run button. ApplySelfTestCard renders on every snapshot apply (live tick) so the card stays current.

Future iteration could extend the SSE handler to push 'self_test' events on every sweep so dashboard updates within a beat instead of within ~8s.

history.summary: Bind-address consistency fix on the dashboard. Pre-v0.9.74 the Overview card resolved a wildcard bind (0.0.0.0 / [::]) into the primary LAN IP for display via resolveDisplayUrl, but three other panels (Gateway Status, Onboarding, Discovery) rendered the raw mcpUrl, so with bindAddress=0.0.0.0 and primaryIp=192.168.1.7 the operator saw 'http://192.168.1.7:8080/mcp' on Overview and 'http://0.0.0.0:8080/mcp' on the other three -- same server, three different addresses across cards. v0.9.74 routes every gateway-URL render through resolveDisplayUrl with the same lanIp source so all four cards advertise the address an actual client can connect to. Each card that does the substitution also surfaces the raw 'Listening on / Bound on' line in muted text so the operator sees the configured listen value and the resolved client-facing URL together rather than picking one and hiding the other.
entries: fix(ui): renderGatewayPanel mcpUrl now goes through resolveDisplayUrl with state.dashboard.telemetry.primaryIpAddress (or state.discovery.serverIpAddress) as the lanIp source. Adds 'Listening on <raw>' line below the resolved URL when wildcard substitution applied.
fix(ui): renderOnboardingPanel gatewayUrl now goes through resolveDisplayUrl. The .mcp.json snippet operators copy now contains the resolved IP, not the wildcard.

fix(ui): renderDiscoveryPanel mcpUrl + healthUrl now go through resolveDisplayUrl. Added 'Server IP' row to the discovery list and 'Bound on <raw>' line under the resolved gateway URL when wildcard substitution applied.
docs(version): bump 0.9.73 -> 0.9.74 (UI consistency fix; patch bump per scheme since no public contract changed).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: WinUI shell already shows the bind address consistently across cards (snapshot.bindAddress is a single source feeding Overview's APIs & Services card, Configuration narrative, Security label, Settings textbox, and the Security narrative paragraph). The shell already has the wildcard substitution suffix on the Configuration narrative line. No shell change required for this iteration.

history.summary: CLU now distributes the new Forsetti Agentic Edition governance authority. Per operator directive, the source tree at C:\Users\Flynn\Downloads\forsetti-agentic-edition-main supersedes any previous governance. Phase 1 of the CLU rebuild: vendor the new authority into MCOS at Forsetti-Agentic-Edition/ in the source tree, copy to <install>/share/Forsetti-Agentic-Edition/, and expose it through two new API endpoints. GET /api/governance/agentic-edition/manifest returns a structured catalog of every document, policy registry, agent role spec, contract template, schema, and standard the new authority defines. GET /api/governance/agentic-edition/document/{relative-path} returns the file contents with the appropriate Content-Type (text/markdown for .md, application/json for .json). Path-traversal-safe: paths containing '..' or '\\' are rejected.
entries: feat(governance): vendor forsetti-agentic-edition-main into Forsetti-Agentic-Edition/ in the MCOS source tree and into <install>/share/Forsetti-Agentic-Edition/ for runtime access.

feat(api): GET /api/governance/agentic-edition/manifest catalogs all governance files into structured buckets: documents (every file), policies (policies/ + core/policies/ JSON), agents (agents/ MD), contracts (contracts/ MD), schemas (schemas/ JSON), standards (standards/ MD). Reports {available, root, documents, policies, agents, contracts, schemas, standards}.
feat(api): GET /api/governance/agentic-edition/document/{relative-path} returns the file with the right Content-Type. Path-traversal rejected via '..' / '\\' check.
feat(api): When the vendored directory is not present, the manifest endpoint returns 503 with an actionable hint about where to drop the source tree. Honest unavailable state -- no fake bundle.

docs(version): bump 0.9.72 -> 0.9.73 (additive governance surface; patch bump per scheme since no public contract removed and the existing /api/governance/bundles/{platform} surface is unchanged for v0.9.72 backwards compat).

Phase 3 of the CLU rebuild ships in v0.9.75+: implement the contract-before-action enforceAction outcome (Allow / Block / RequiresOperatorApproval) per CHANGE_CONTROL_POLICY.md against the new policy registries (changelog-rules.json / compliance-rules.json / docs-sync-rules.json / repo-boundaries.json / versioning-rules.json).
WiX installer rules to vendor share/Forsetti-Agentic-Edition/ at install time (currently the directory is provisioned by hand for testing; future iteration adds the install-script line).
WinUI shell Security/Governance section does not yet surface the new authority -- routes are read-only via curl / direct fetch for v0.9.73.

history.summary: WinUI shell minimize-to-tray + system tray right-click menu. Pre-v0.9.72 closing the shell window terminated the shell process; the operator had to relaunch from Start. v0.9.72 subclasses the main window's WindowProc to intercept WM_CLOSE -- close hides the window into the notification area instead of terminating. The tray icon uses the same icon resource as the desktop / start-menu / taskbar (extracted from the shell exe via ExtractIconW). Left-click / double-click restores the window. Right-click shows a popup menu with Show MCOS Shell, Stop Server, Restart Server, Close MCOS Shell. Stop / Restart route through net.exe with runas elevation so the shell itself can run unelevated and still control the MasterControlProgram service. Close MCOS Shell removes the tray icon and posts WM_QUIT.
entries: feat(shell): MainWindow::MainWindowSubclassProc static window-proc intercepts WM_CLOSE (-> ShowWindow SW_HIDE) and the WM_APP+1 tray callback (left-click restore, right-click popup menu).

feat(shell): InstallTrayIcon registers the NOTIFYICONDATAW with NIF_ICON|NIF_MESSAGE|NIF_TIP. Icon comes from ExtractIconW(hInstance, exePath, 0) so the tray icon matches the desktop icon out of the box. Tooltip: 'Master Control Orchestration Server'.
feat(shell): RemoveTrayIcon NIM_DELETEs on WM_DESTROY and on the 'Close MCOS Shell' menu command so leaving the shell properly cleans up the notification area.

feat(shell): mcosRunElevated helper shells out to cmd.exe /c <cmdLine> via ShellExecuteEx with verb=runas. Used by the Stop Server / Restart Server menu items so they can stop+start the service without the shell needing elevation up front.
feat(shell): ConfigureWindow installs the subclass + tray icon after windowHandle_ is captured. SetWindowLongPtrW GWLP_USERDATA = this so the static subclass proc can recover the instance.
feat(shell): #include <shellapi.h> in MainWindow.xaml.cpp; Shell32.lib is already part of the WinUI shell's link surface.
docs(version): bump 0.9.71 -> 0.9.72 (additive shell capability; patch bump per scheme since no public contract changed).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Tray menu Stop/Restart actions prompt for UAC elevation. A future iteration could cache an elevated helper handle so subsequent runs don't re-prompt. For v0.9.72 the prompt is acceptable -- service control is rare and the prompt makes the intent explicit.
Tray icon does not yet show a balloon notification when service state changes. Future iteration could add NIM_MODIFY with NIF_INFO updates.
CLU model implementation against forsetti-agentic-edition governance ships in v0.9.73+.

history.summary: Real-time push channel via Server-Sent Events. Pre-v0.9.71 the dashboard polled /api/dashboard every 2s; every panel was effectively up-to-date but every refresh paid a full snapshot round-trip. v0.9.71 adds a streaming endpoint at GET /api/events that emits 'dashboard' events whenever the snapshot changes (~1s tick, hash-deduped) and 'activity' events as the activity ring appends, so every tab/view reflects new state within a beat without redundant polling. SimpleHttpServer gained a streamMode flag on HttpResponse + a streamHandler callback; handleClient detaches a worker thread for streaming responses so the accept loop is never blocked by an SSE client. Dashboard JS opens an EventSource on load and replaces its 2s polling timer with a slower 8s safety-net poll. A LIVE / POLLING / CONNECTING badge in the header exposes the connection state.
entries: feat(http): HttpResponse gains streamMode + streamHandler. handleClient detects streamMode, sends Content-Type: text/event-stream + Cache-Control: no-cache + Connection: keep-alive headers, then hands the socket to the streamHandler on a detached thread (closesocket on exit).
feat(http): handleClient now closes the client socket explicitly on the non-streaming path too -- pre-v0.9.71 sockets were left to the OS to recycle.

feat(api): GET /api/events SSE endpoint. Initial ': mcos sse stream open' comment fires the EventSource 'open' handler immediately. Per-tick: hash-dedup'd snapshot push as 'dashboard' event; activity-ring tail since the last high-water mark as 'activity' events. ': ping' keepalive every 15 idle ticks so proxies / browsers don't time out.

feat(ui): startRealtimeStream() in resources/web/app.js opens EventSource on /api/events. dashboard handler replaces state.dashboard + renderCurrent(); activity handler appends to state.activity (capped at 500 events) and re-renders activity-bearing decks. Polling timer reduced to 8s safety net.
feat(ui): #realtimeChip in resources/web/index.html shows LIVE (SSE connected) / POLLING (fallback) / CONNECTING (initial state).
docs(version): bump 0.9.70 -> 0.9.71 (additive HTTP capability + new endpoint; patch bump per scheme since no public contract removed).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: WinUI shell minimize-to-tray + right-click stop/restart/close ships in v0.9.72.
WinUI shell does not yet consume /api/events; it continues to poll on its own 2s tick. A future iteration could share an EventSource via the shell's WebView.
CLU model implementation against forsetti-agentic-edition governance ships in v0.9.73+.

history.summary: Telemetry deck now surfaces the MCP server card grid alongside the existing sub-agent card grid. Per operator directive, each MCP server gets its own small card with the same visual structure as a sub-agent card: reachability dot, utilization bar with capacity counts, supervisor pool name, last-probed timestamp, install-state pill + diagnostic block (v0.9.56-58), connected-client list (active leases through the gateway). Reads state.dashboard.mcpServerRuntimeStats which the runtime already populates every snapshot tick.
entries: feat(ui): renderTelemetryClients in resources/web/app.js inserts renderMcpServerUtilizationPanel({deck:'telemetry'}) above the sub-agent panel for both the populated-clients and empty-clients render branches.

feat(ui): MCP card visual is renderMcpServerCard (which delegates to renderSubAgentCard with a synthetic shape) so the cards render identically to the sub-agent grid -- single visual primitive, dual-grid surface.
docs(version): bump 0.9.69 -> 0.9.70 (UI-only addition; patch bump per scheme since no public contract changed).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Real-time / SSE telemetry across all views (v0.9.71): currently the dashboard polls /api/dashboard on an interval. Real-time push via Server-Sent Events ships next.
WinUI shell minimize-to-tray + right-click stop/restart/close ships in v0.9.72.
CLU model implementation against forsetti-agentic-edition governance ships in v0.9.73+.

history.summary: Boot self-test framework. After the HTTP server starts, MCOS spawns a background thread that waits ~3s for pool handshakes to settle, then runs a battery of probes: admin port TCP reachability, gateway port TCP reachability + state==Running, every supervised pool's Ready-instance count vs minInstances, activity-ring persistence health, telemetry sampler liveness, and on-disk worker exe presence. Each probe writes a structured ActivityEvent with kind="self_test" + statusCode 200|500 so the existing v0.8.7 Error Reporting frame on the WinUI Overview deck surfaces failures automatically (it already harvests 4xx/5xx events). A summary row is also emitted with kind="self_test_summary". New endpoints GET /api/self-tests (returns the cached snapshot {startedAtUtc, finishedAtUtc, totalCount, passedCount, failedCount, results[]}; pending=true until the first sweep completes) and POST /api/self-tests/run (re-run on demand).
entries: feat(model): SelfTestResult + SelfTestSnapshot in MasterControlModels.h with NLOHMANN serialization.
feat(runtime): MasterControlApplication::Impl::runBootSelfTestsAsync spawns a background thread that runs the probe battery 3s after boot. Stop hook joins the thread on shutdown.

feat(runtime): runBootSelfTestsNow probes 6 categories: http_endpoint, gateway, pool_handshake (kind=mcp-server pools), sub_agent (kind=SubAgent pools), telemetry, process. Per-probe ActivityEvent emission for the Error Reporting frame; aggregate self_test_summary event for the audit log.
feat(api): GET /api/self-tests returns the cached snapshot. POST /api/self-tests/run re-runs and returns fresh results.

feat(probe): selfTestTcpProbe helper does the same non-blocking connect / 500ms select pattern AdminApiService::probeReachabilityCached uses, but standalone so the self-test machinery doesn't depend on AdminApiService internals.
docs(version): bump 0.9.68 -> 0.9.69 (additive surface; patch bump per scheme since no public contract removed).

history.summary: External-dependency MCPs registered as default supervised pools. docker-control exposes docker.status / docker.list_containers via docker.exe shell-outs (honest unavailable when Docker isn't installed); local-database exposes db.status / db.set_connection_string with the connection string persisted via persistent-context (key 'local-database.connection-string'). The pools are installed_and_supervised because the in-tree worker is the wire; the underlying capability (Docker Desktop / configured DSN) is the operator's concern but the MCP route is live and the tools surface configuration state honestly. Reachable count goes 24/28 -> 26/28; gateway tools/list grows from 92 to 96. Only 2 catalog placeholders remain (playwright + chrome-devtools); both need operator-side `npm install -g <pkg>` followed by POST /api/pools -- the v0.9.58 installCommand + v0.9.60 detection plumbing already surface that path on the dashboard.
entries: feat(worker): Specialization::DockerControl + Specialization::LocalDatabase + CLI parser branches.

feat(worker): docker.status invokes 'docker version --format json' via runProcessCaptured. launchFailed -> dockerReachable=false + actionable hint about installing Docker Desktop. Otherwise returns exitCode + raw stdout/stderr from the daemon.
feat(worker): docker.list_containers shells out to 'docker ps --format {{json .}}' (or '--all' when arguments.all=true), returns raw daemon output.
feat(worker): db.status reads persistent-context['local-database.connection-string']; reports configured=true|false honestly with hint text the operator can act on.
feat(worker): db.set_connection_string persists the connection string via persistentCtxSet so subsequent db.* tools (future iterations) can read it.
feat(runtime): registerWorkerPool calls for docker-control + local-database (minInstances=1, kind=mcp-server).
docs(version): bump 0.9.67 -> 0.9.68 (two additive backends; patch bump per scheme since no public contract removed).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: playwright + chrome-devtools remain catalog placeholders awaiting_pool_registration on hosts without the npm packages. The v0.9.58 install-command + v0.9.60 detection surface already tell operators what to do. A future iteration could auto-register pool templates when the v0.9.60 detector finds the package; for now, the operator runs npm install -g <pkg> and POST /api/pools.

SSE upgrade for Streamable HTTP still deferred.
docker-control db.list_containers and other Docker tools currently shell out to docker.exe; a future iteration could wire WinHttp directly to the Docker named pipe for lower-overhead operation.

history.summary: Resolved the long-standing MCOS sub-agent runner architectural decision by registering all 7 default sub-agents (sentinel, architect, forge, scribe, recon, nexus, watchtower) as supervised stdio MCP servers using the multi-spec worker pattern. Each sub-agent role exposes 3 tools that bridge to admin endpoints + filesystem + structured templates: sentinel.list_rules / .recent_activity / .health_summary; architect.list_phases / .discovery_doc / .draft_adr; forge.list_pools / .suggest_pool_template / .list_specializations; scribe.list_release_reports / .draft_release_report / .version_state; recon.dashboard / .diagnostics / .gateway_tools; nexus.health_summary / .discovery / .list_clients; watchtower.health_summary / .gateway_status / .activity_tail. Pools registered with kind=SubAgent so the dashboard renders them in the sub-agent grid. Sub-agent reachable count goes 0/7 -> 7/7; total reachable goes from 24/28 + 0/7 to 24/28 + 7/7; gateway tools/list grows from 71 to 92.
entries: feat(worker): Specialization::AgentSentinel / AgentArchitect / AgentForge / AgentScribe / AgentRecon / AgentNexus / AgentWatchtower + CLI parser branches.
feat(worker): Each sub-agent's catalog exposes 3 tools: 2-3 HTTP bridges to admin endpoints + 0-1 filesystem-backed or template-emitting tools.

feat(worker): forge.list_specializations is reflective -- it walks every catalog function in this binary and returns {specialization, tools[]} so an operator can see at runtime what the worker can do.
feat(worker): scribe.list_release_reports reuses indexListFiles pointed at the handoff/realignment dir with glob 'v*-release-report.md' so 'list current release notes' is one tool call.
feat(worker): architect.draft_adr / scribe.draft_release_report return structured markdown templates the LAN AI client fills in.
feat(runtime): registerSubAgentPool lambda mirrors registerWorkerPool but with kind=SubAgent. 7 pools registered: sentinel / architect / forge / scribe / recon / nexus / watchtower.
docs(version): bump 0.9.66 -> 0.9.67 (seven additive role-named pools; patch bump per scheme since the sub-agents were previously catalog placeholders with no backing pool).

history.summary: Win32-input + screen-capture + desktop-control batch. Four new specializations: keyboard-mouse-control (input.keyboard via SendInput KEYEVENTF_UNICODE; input.click + input.move via SetCursorPos + MOUSEEVENTF_*; input.scroll via MOUSEEVENTF_WHEEL/HWHEEL); screen-capture-vision (screen.capture via GetDC + CreateCompatibleBitmap + BitBlt + GetDIBits + BMP file-header assembly + base64 encode -> MCP image content shape; screen.size via GetSystemMetrics); desktop-control (desktop.list_windows via EnumWindows callback that captures hwnd/title/visible/foreground/bounds; desktop.focus via case-insensitive substring match + SetForegroundWindow; desktop.launch via ShellExecuteW; desktop.foreground via GetForegroundWindow + GetWindowTextW + GetWindowThreadProcessId); computer-use umbrella that re-exports computer.screenshot / computer.click / computer.type / computer.move_mouse / computer.window_list under the Anthropic-style names. Reachable count goes 20/28 -> 24/28; gateway tools/list grows from 57 to 71.
entries: feat(worker): Specialization::KeyboardMouseControl / ScreenCaptureVision / DesktopControl / ComputerUse + CLI parser branches.
feat(worker): base64Encode helper (standard alphabet, no line breaks) used for screen-capture image payloads.

feat(worker): inputKeyboard / inputClick / inputMove / inputScroll wrappers around SendInput. Layout-independent KEYEVENTF_UNICODE + special-cased \n / \t / \b -> VK_RETURN / VK_TAB / VK_BACK so common control characters work.

feat(worker): screenCapture builds a 24-bit DIB via GetDIBits, prepends BITMAPFILEHEADER + INFO header so the byte stream is a valid .bmp, base64-encodes, returns under MCP image content shape with width/height metadata.
feat(worker): desktopListWindows / desktopFocus / desktopLaunch / desktopForeground via EnumWindows + SetForegroundWindow + ShellExecuteW + GetForegroundWindow.

feat(worker): ComputerUse specialization re-exports the most common input/screen/desktop tools as computer.* aliases so clients written against the Anthropic computer-use spec can use MCOS as a drop-in.
feat(runtime): registerWorkerPool calls for keyboard-mouse-control (4 leases), screen-capture-vision (2 -- captures are heavier), desktop-control (4), computer-use (4).
docs(version): bump 0.9.65 -> 0.9.66 (four additive backends; patch bump per scheme since no public contract removed).

7 sub-agents still 0/7 reachable; v0.9.67 ships them next as MCP tool servers exposing role-specific helper tools.
screen.capture returns BMP, not PNG. Larger payloads but no WIC dependency. Future iteration could add PNG via Windows Imaging Component.

history.summary: Three more in-tree backends: persistent-context (JSON-file-backed K/V at %ProgramData%\MCOS\persistent-context.json with ctx.set/get/list/delete tools), knowledge-graph (JSON-file-backed entities+relations store at %ProgramData%\MCOS\knowledge-graph.json with kg.entity.upsert / kg.relation.create / kg.search / kg.read_graph / kg.delete_entity), file-watcher (polling-based snapshot diff using FindFirstFileW with watch.add / watch.poll / watch.list / watch.remove tools, process-scoped watch table). entries: feat(worker): Specialization::PersistentContext / KnowledgeGraph / FileWatcher + CLI parser branches.
feat(worker): mcosDataDirectory() resolves %ProgramData%\MCOS via GetEnvironmentVariableW; create_directories on demand.
feat(worker): persistentCtx{Set,Get,List,Delete} -- read/modify/write JSON store under a process-wide mutex.

feat(worker): kgEntityUpsert / kgRelationCreate / kgSearch (case-insensitive substring) / kgDeleteEntity / loadKnowledgeGraph -- JSON store, observation merging by exact-string dedup, related-relation surfacing in search.

feat(worker): fileWatch{Add,Poll,List,Remove} + takeSnapshot() -- iterative-stack FindFirstFileW walk that captures sizeBytes + lastWriteTime per file. watch.poll re-walks and computes added/modified/removed against the previous snapshot, then atomically replaces it. Watch table is process-scoped so Job Object kill clears all watches.

history.summary: Five more in-tree backends following the multi-spec worker pattern: code-execution-repl (repl.exec for python/node/powershell), local-test-runner (test.run for ctest/pytest/jest/npm/dotnet/cargo + a 'shell' escape hatch), local-build-tool (build.run for cmake/msbuild/cargo/npm/dotnet/gradle/make), local-linter (lint.run for eslint/pylint/ruff/clang-tidy/dotnet-format/rustfmt), local-indexer (index.list_files via FindFirstFileW recursive walk with PathMatchSpecW glob filter, reparse-point loop guard, configurable maxFiles cap). Reachable count goes 12/28 -> 17/28; gateway tools/list grows from 39 to 44.
entries: feat(worker): Specialization::CodeExecutionRepl, LocalTestRunner, LocalBuildTool, LocalLinter, LocalIndexer + CLI parser branches.

feat(worker): runCommandLineTool() shared helper -- builds a quoted command line from (exe, args[]) and defers to runProcessCaptured. Returns the same JSON envelope shape as shellExec / gitRun for uniform parsing.

feat(worker): httpBridgeGet(url, timeoutMs) helper. Shells out to curl.exe (built into Windows 10+) with -s -m <sec> + a sentinel format string '__MCOS_HTTP_STATUS__:%{http_code}' so the worker can split body from status code without depending on libcurl. Tries to parse the body as JSON; on failure passes through as bodyText. Captures bridgeFailed/bridgeError honestly when curl can't be launched or the call times out.

history.summary: Boot-time npm package detection. history.summary: Honest-unavailable diagnostic surface for runtime stats. Pre-v0.9.56 the dashboard reported reachable=false with empty status text for every catalog placeholder (21 of 28 MCP servers + 7 of 7 sub-agents on this host) and operators had no way to tell apart a healthy stdio-supervised pool, an admin-port-served helper, and a TCP placeholder waiting for a pool. v0.9.56 adds five fields to SubAgentRuntimeStat / McpServerRuntimeStat: unavailableReason (categorized: connection_refused / timeout / dns_failed / no_listener / supervised_pool_not_ready / no_endpoint_registered), lastErrorMessage (human-readable line including host:port), lastErrorAtUtc (timestamp of the failure), installState (installed_and_supervised / online_via_admin_port / online_via_external_listener / supervised_pool_not_ready / awaiting_pool_registration / unknown), and installHint (actionable next step like 'POST /api/pools with poolId=...'). The reachability cache also stores per-entry probe error categories so the snapshot can mirror them. New endpoint GET /api/diagnostics/runtime-stats bundles the per-entry detail with an aggregate count by installState. The response answers 'how many of my N catalog entries are actually live, supervised, or just placeholders?' in one request.

history.summary: POST /api/setup/dependencies/{id}/{unknown-verb} returns 400 with the valid action list instead of a misleading 405 'Method Not Allowed'. The route handler only matches endsWith('/install'); other verbs (preflight, uninstall, detect, anything else) fell through to the v0.9.28 supportedMethodsForPath fallback which saw the prefix /api/setup/dependencies/ as POST-allowed and returned 405 with Allow: POST, OPTIONS. POST IS the right method -- it's the URL action segment that's unknown. v0.9.54 adds a sibling fallback handler that catches POST to any /api/setup/dependencies/{id}/{not-install} path and returns 400 with 'Unknown dependency action <verb>. Supported actions: install.' A bare /api/setup/dependencies/{id} (no action) also returns 400 with the route shape.

history.summary: Companion to v0.9.52: same URL-segment-id validation extended to /api/clients, /api/runtime/mcp-servers, /api/runtime/subagents, /api/runtime/subagent-groups. The v0.9.53 bug-hunt followed the v0.9.52 pool injection across the four sister registration endpoints and found the same bug: clientId='foo/bar' / mcpServerId='foo/bar' / subAgentId='foo/bar' all registered successfully, then the entity was unreachable for direct GET / DELETE because the slash split the URL path into a sub-resource segment. Sub-agent groups were already partially protected (the slash-id collided with an existing target) but lacked the explicit gate. v0.9.53 introduces a shared isSafeUrlSegmentId() helper (ASCII letters, digits, '-', '_', '.') and adds it to all four upsert paths. The v0.9.52 inline pool validation refactored to use the same helper. LanClientAccessService gains a constructor-time scrub of previously-persisted invalid entries so the v0.9.52 bug-hunt's stuck 'foo/bar' client is purged on next boot.

history.summary: Pool registration input validation. The v0.9.52 bug-hunt sent POST /api/pools with poolId='foo/bar' and got back 200 'Pool registered.' -- a pool that was instantly UNREACHABLE because /api/pools/{poolId} splits the slash into a sub-resource segment. Same probe with poolId='' returned 200 (with succeeded:false) but should be 400. Same probe with minInstances > maxInstances or negative minInstances was accepted unconditionally; ensureMinInstances would then loop trying to spawn workers up to an unreachable target. v0.9.52 adds three validation gates in WorkerSupervisor::upsertPool: (1) poolId must be ASCII letters/digits/'-'/'_'/'.' only -- no slashes, whitespace, control bytes, or other URL-unsafe characters; (2) scalePolicy.minInstances >= 0; (3) scalePolicy.maxInstances >= 1; (4) minInstances <= maxInstances. Failure returns 400 from the route. The character-set restriction is what every existing operator pool already uses, so no migration impact.
entries: fix(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::upsertPool() rejects poolId containing slashes / whitespace / control bytes / other URL-unsafe characters with a clear validation message.
fix(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::upsertPool() rejects scalePolicy with minInstances < 0, maxInstances < 1, or minInstances > maxInstances.
fix(http): POST /api/pools maps validation failures to HTTP 400 (was 200/false).

history.summary: JSON-RPC batch requests now return a proper -32600 Invalid Request envelope instead of HTTP 500. Pre-v0.9.51 a batch body (an array of request objects per JSON-RPC 2.0 §6) reached `req.value("method", ...)` which threw type_error.306 ('cannot use value() with array'); the v0.9.48 serveLoop catch picked it up and returned HTTP 500 with the nlohmann exception text in the body. The right answer is a single Invalid Request envelope at the JSON-RPC layer, since this gateway doesn't support batch -- the spec allows that as long as the server explicitly says so. v0.9.51 also rejects non-object top-level JSON (e.g. `"hello"` or `42`) with the same -32600.
entries: fix(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::handleMcpRequest rejects JSON-RPC batch arrays with -32600 ('JSON-RPC batches are not supported by this gateway') instead of throwing past the type_error.306.
fix(gateway): non-object top-level JSON (string, number, null) returns -32600 ('top-level JSON must be a request object').

history.summary: Query strings broke route matching. Pre-v0.9.50 a probe like 'GET /api/dashboard?cache=bust' returned 405 Method Not Allowed. SimpleHttpServer::parseRequest stored the full request target verbatim in request.path ('/api/dashboard?cache=bust'), every route handler used strict equality (request.path == '/api/dashboard') for matching, so any GET with a query string failed to match. The v0.9.28 supportedMethodsForPath() table did strip the query for its lookup, so the path appeared 'known' but the request appeared 'method not allowed' -- the worst possible response: a generic GET with a cache-bust parameter (standard browser behavior, dashboard polling, retry-with-jitter clients) got 405. Verified live: GET /api/dashboard, /api/version, every other GET-only route returned 405 the moment any query string was attached. Fix: parseRequest now splits at '?' -- request.path holds the URI path, new request.query holds the raw query (no leading '?'). The two query-aware routes (/api/activity and /api/telemetry/events) read request.query directly; their match patterns simplified from rfind(prefix, 0) == 0 to strict equality. Every other route's strict equality now works as intended for query-string variants.
entries: fix(http): SimpleHttpServer::parseRequest splits request target at '?' so request.path is the URI path only and request.query holds the query string. Every GET endpoint now matches under arbitrary query strings.
feat(http): HttpRequest gained a `query` member (raw query string, no leading '?').
refactor(http): /api/activity and /api/telemetry/events route matchers simplified to strict path equality; query parsing reads request.query.

history.summary: Companion to v0.9.48: admin port (7300) had the same UTF-8 issue but was masked by the outer 500 handler. POST /api/clients with mojibake displayName returned 500 'invalid UTF-8 byte at index N' from nlohmann::json::dump(). The service didn't crash because SimpleHttpServer's request handler has an outer try/catch returning 500 -- but that's the wrong status (RFC 7231 §6.5.1: client-supplied bad bytes are 400). The MasterControlApplication::Impl::jsonResponse template helper now serializes with error_handler_t::replace so invalid UTF-8 becomes U+FFFD (`\ufffd`) in the output. This is the same fix the v0.9.48 gateway crash needed and applies to the entire admin API surface in one place. RFC 8259 §8.1 endorses this lenient behavior for tolerant JSON serializers.
entries: fix(admin): jsonResponse() helper uses dump() with error_handler_t::replace so admin API responses no longer fail with HTTP 500 when client bodies contain invalid UTF-8.
docs(version): bump 0.9.48 -> 0.9.49 (admin port mirror of v0.9.48 fix; patch bump per scheme).

history.summary: **CRITICAL**: invalid UTF-8 bytes in any /mcp request body crashed the MCOS service. The v0.9.48 bug-hunt sent a stray Latin-1 0xE9 byte (the `é` char in CP1252) embedded in a tools/list params field and watched the entire MasterControlServiceHost process exit with code 1067 (ERROR_PROCESS_ABORTED). Root cause: nlohmann::json::dump() throws type_error.316 on invalid UTF-8 bytes in any string field. Pre-v0.9.48 the buildJsonRpcError helper called `envelope.dump()` (default error_handler_t::strict). The throw escaped buildJsonRpcError, escaped handleMcpRequest, and escaped serveLoop -- which is the entry point of an std::thread, so an uncaught exception there tripped std::terminate and took the process down. Operator-visible: any LAN client with a CP1252 / Latin-1 / mojibake body byte (very common when bridging from non-UTF-8 terminals or older Windows clients) could crash MCOS at will. Two-layer fix: (1) buildJsonRpcError uses dump() with error_handler_t::replace (invalid bytes -> U+FFFD) wrapped in try/catch with a hand-rolled fallback envelope. (2) serveLoop wraps handleMcpRequest in try/catch returning HTTP 500 + a generic JSON-RPC -32603 envelope, so any future uncaught exception out of the dispatcher logs and continues instead of crashing the gateway thread.
entries: fix(gateway): buildJsonRpcError serializes with error_handler_t::replace and a try/catch fallback so invalid UTF-8 bytes in a parse-error message no longer crash dump().

fix(gateway): serveLoop wraps handleMcpRequest in try/catch at the dispatcher boundary; any uncaught exception now produces HTTP 500 with a -32603 JSON-RPC error instead of std::terminate-ing the service.
docs(version): bump 0.9.47 -> 0.9.48 (CRITICAL crash fix; patch bump per scheme since it's a defect not an architecture change).

history.summary: Four more remove-route holdouts that returned 500 on bad bodies and 400 (instead of 404) on missing targets. Pre-v0.9.44: (1) /api/platform-services/apple-hosts/remove, (2) /api/runtime/mcp-servers/remove, (3) /api/runtime/subagents/remove, (4) /api/runtime/subagent-groups/remove all called adminApiService_->remove*Json(request.body) which runs nlohmann::json::parse(...).get<RemovalRequest>() inline; the parse_error throws bubbled past their local 200/400 branches and hit the outer 500 handler. Routes (2) and (3) had their v0.9.38 404-vs-400 message-branch logic inside the same scope as the throwing call, so the 404 path never fired on a malformed body either -- the throw landed in the outer handler before the message branch ran. Route (4) had been touched by v0.9.42 only on the upsert side. v0.9.44 wraps each in try/catch returning 400 + diagnostic body, and adds the 404-on-missing-target branch to (4) for parity with the v0.9.30/v0.9.33/v0.9.37/v0.9.38/v0.9.43 sweep.

history.summary: POST /api/install/{package,repo,zip} return 400 instead of 500 on bad bodies. Pre-v0.9.41 the three install routes called adminApiService_->install*Json helpers which run nlohmann::json::parse(requestBody).get<Spec>() inline -- no try/catch -- so an empty body or any body that doesn't deserialize into the matching Spec (InstallerPackageSpec / BootstrapRepoSpec / ZipBundleSpec) threw and the throw bubbled to the outer 500 handler. history.summary: /api/pools/{id} now surfaces quarantinedUntilUtc directly. The v0.9.40 bug-hunt killed the baseline-tools worker 4 times in rapid succession to verify the v0.9.8 crash circuit breaker (5 worker deaths within 60s -> pool quarantined for 60s with auto-respawn paused). The supervisor handled it correctly -- emitted pool_worker_death + pool_worker_respawn events for the first 4, then pool_quarantine on the 5th implicit attempt -- but the only API surfaces of the quarantine state were /api/health/summary's darkOrQuarantined counter and the activity ring's pool_quarantine event. /api/pools/{poolId} returned 'instances:[]' with no quarantine field, so a generic operator dashboard rendering per-pool status had to infer 'pool is quarantined' from the empty instance list. v0.9.40 adds a ManagedEndpointPool.quarantinedUntilUtc field (empty when not quarantined; ISO-8601 UTC expiry when quarantined). The supervisor's listPools/findPool populate it from poolQuarantineUntil_, converting the in-memory steady_clock deadline into a wall-clock UTC string by adding the remaining duration to system_clock::now().
entries: feat(api): ManagedEndpointPool gains quarantinedUntilUtc field. /api/pools and /api/pools/{poolId} surface the crash-circuit-breaker quarantine deadline directly.
feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::quarantineExpiryUtcLocked() converts the steady_clock cool-down deadline into wall-clock ISO-8601 UTC for serialization.

history.summary: 503 Service Unavailable for the six 'service is not running' responses that were still returning 200. /api/governance/decisions when CLU is null, /api/pools POST when supervisor is null (2 sites: upsert + scale/drain/leases action), /api/leases acquire when lease router is null (2 sites: pool action + standalone), and /api/telemetry/heartbeat when aggregator is null all returned 200 with succeeded:false. Per RFC 7231 §6.6.4 the named runtime service being uninitialized is exactly what 503 communicates: 'unable to handle the request due to a temporary overload or maintenance.' These are partial-init / shutdown windows in normal operation -- never seen during steady-state because all six services boot before the HTTP listener accepts requests -- but the right response if a probe ever catches the window. Closes the deferred-from-v0.9.30 sweep.

history.summary: DNS-SD service advertisement now works. Pre-v0.9.36 both DNS_SERVICE_REGISTER_REQUEST sites (PlatformServiceCatalogService and BeaconService) set pRegisterCompletionCallback = nullptr, which DnsServiceRegister rejects with ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER (Win32 87 / 0x57). All three platform-gateway lanes (windows / macos / iOS) and the underlying _mcos._tcp.local LAN beacon were stuck in status='registration_failed' with last_register_error=0x00000057 -- visible in /api/beacon. The mDNS service-type advertisements (_mastercontrol-windows._tcp.local etc.) never reached the LAN, so a client that browsed by service type to discover MCOS got nothing. The native gateway on port 8080 worked because it doesn't depend on DNS-SD; clients with the URL hard-coded or learned via the UDP beacon could still connect. v0.9.36 adds a non-null no-op completion callback (the synchronous DnsServiceRegister return value carries the 'registration accepted' decision; the eventual async completion is informational and we don't need to gate on it) and accepts DNS_REQUEST_PENDING (= 0x2522) as success alongside ERROR_SUCCESS, since the async-with-callback path returns DNS_REQUEST_PENDING by spec.
entries: fix(dns-sd): PlatformServiceCatalogService::registerGatewayLocked sets pRegisterCompletionCallback = &dnsRegisterCallback (no-op) instead of nullptr. Treats DNS_REQUEST_PENDING as success in addition to ERROR_SUCCESS.
fix(dns-sd): BeaconService::registerInstanceLocked applies the same fix (callback + DNS_REQUEST_PENDING acceptance).

history.summary: /api/governance/bundles/{unknown} now returns 404 instead of the windows bundle. GovernanceBundleService::normalizePlatform defaulted to 'windows' on any unrecognized input, so a probe at /api/governance/bundles/freebsd (or any nonsense suffix) silently returned the 10 417-byte windows bundle. Misled clients would happily download and apply the wrong governance rules. v0.9.35 validates the platform at the route layer with platformFromKey() (which already returns PlatformTarget::Unknown for unknown values) and returns 404 + { succeeded: false, message: 'Unknown governance bundle platform '<name>'. Supported: windows, macos, ios.' }. The underlying normalizePlatform fallback is left untouched -- it's defense-in-depth for any internal caller that bypasses the HTTP route.

history.summary: Lease leak in tools/call. NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::ProcessJsonRpc acquired a lease per tools/call but never released it. The pre-v0.9.34 source had a comment block that explicitly said 'Always release the lease... LeaseRouter::releaseLease is idempotent on unknown ids... Comment kept for clarity -- the call site is below.' but no releaseLease() call followed. Every tools/call request leaked one lease. The v0.9.34 bug-hunt probed it: 10 parallel tools/call requests left activeLeaseCount=10 forever, all 10 leases in state='active' with releasedAtUtc='', acquiredAtUtc clustered at the same second. Operator-visible symptoms: the dashboard's per-pool 'active leases' bar grows monotonically; saturation calculations and scale-out triggers fire on phantom leases; with maxActiveLeasesPerInstance=64 a long-running gateway eventually rejects calls with 'could not acquire instance lease.' v0.9.34 calls router->releaseLease(lease.leaseId, 'tools/call complete') unconditionally after the bridge round-trip returns, before any of the success/error JSON-RPC return paths.
entries: fix(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::ProcessJsonRpc tools/call now calls router->releaseLease() after the stdio bridge response returns, regardless of success/failure path. Stale comment block citing 'the call site is below' replaced with a v0.9.34 note documenting the bug.

history.summary: /api/activity now returns the LATEST events when called with ?max=N (no since=), not the oldest. Pre-v0.9.32 ActivityEventRing::read() iterated the ring forward and stopped at maxCount, surfacing events 1..N -- which on a 459-event ring with max=10 returned events from 06:46:11Z (boot-time supervisor respawns) instead of the live tail. Operator dashboards rendering 'recent activity' widgets showed hours-old data, exactly the opposite of what the widget meant. This was a hidden consequence of v0.9.31's max= fix: with max=ignored, the response had everything; with max=honored but oldest-first, the response had everything wrong. v0.9.32 introduces split semantics in read(): sinceId empty -> tail mode (latest maxCount, oldest-first within the slice); sinceId non-empty -> forward-iterate and return oldest events newer than sinceId, capped at maxCount, so incremental polling still renders chronologically without skipping intermediate events.
entries: fix(activity): ActivityEventRing::read() with empty sinceId returns the LATEST maxCount events (oldest-first within the slice) instead of the OLDEST. Incremental-poll path (since=N) unchanged.

history.summary: /api/activity now honors ?max=N. Pre-v0.9.31 the route parsed only ?since= and silently dropped ?max=, so /api/activity?max=5 returned the entire 512-event ring -- often 128 KB+ on a busy host. Operator dashboards polling for a small 'recent activity' widget pulled every event on every tick (we observed an actual probe returning 128986 bytes for max=5). The ActivityEventRing::read() helper has supported a maxCount parameter since the original PHASE-09 ring; the route handler just wasn't wiring it through. The /api/telemetry/events route -- structurally adjacent in the same handler block -- already honored max= correctly, which is how the bug-hunt caught the asymmetry.
entries: fix(http): /api/activity parses ?max=N and clamps to ActivityEventRing::kCapacity. Default behavior (no max=) unchanged.

history.summary: Hostname truncation fix and three more 200-with-succeeded:false routes corrected. (1) WindowsHostTelemetryService::readComputerName() called GetComputerNameA which returns the NetBIOS name capped at 15 chars by protocol. Hosts with longer DNS names ('PC-GAMING-R7-5800X' chopped to 'PC-GAMING-R7-58') had their hostName silently truncated in /api/beacon, /api/dashboard, and every dependent client surface -- visibly inconsistent with the worker's tools/call host_info which reports the full hostname via a different Win32 API. v0.9.30 switches to GetComputerNameExA(ComputerNameDnsHostname, ...) which has no 15-char cap (RFC 1035 caps DNS names at 255 octets, well within the new 256-byte buffer) with a fall-back to GetComputerNameA on hard failure. (2) GET /api/pools/{poolId} for an unknown pool returned 200; now returns 404 (RFC 7231 §6.5.4). (3) GET /api/pools/{poolId}/{unknown-subresource} returned 200; now 400 (route-shape error). (4) POST /api/leases/{leaseId} without /release suffix returned 200; now 400. (5) POST /api/pools/{poolId}/{unknown-action} returned 200; now 400.
entries: fix(host): WindowsHostTelemetryService::readComputerName() uses GetComputerNameExA(ComputerNameDnsHostname) instead of GetComputerNameA, returning the full DNS hostname instead of the NetBIOS-truncated 15-char name. NetBIOS-API fallback retained for hard failures.

history.summary: Malformed-JSON and missing-required-field POST responses now return 400, not 200. Pre-v0.9.29 the catch arms in /api/governance/decisions, /api/pools, and /api/telemetry/heartbeat all returned 200 OK with a body of { succeeded: false, message: 'Invalid X JSON: ...' }. HTTP middleware that keys on status (nginx upstream metrics, Prometheus blackbox exporter, fetch.ok in browser, retry libraries that retry on 4xx/5xx but not 2xx) saw '200 OK' and treated the response as a successful operation -- the operator-visible symptom was that a misconfigured client could spam parse errors at MCOS forever and no monitoring would alert. RFC 7231 §6.5.1: a syntactically-malformed request body is a 400 Bad Request. v0.9.29 passes the explicit 400 status code on the three parse-catch sites and on the ClientHeartbeat.clientId precondition (valid JSON but missing required field is still a 400).
entries: fix(http): /api/governance/decisions parse error returns 400, not 200.

history.summary: 405 Method Not Allowed with Allow header for unsupported verbs on known operator API routes. Pre-v0.9.28, hitting any registered route with the wrong verb (DELETE/PUT/PATCH on /api/version, GET on /api/install/zip, POST on /api/health, etc.) returned an opaque 404 indistinguishable from 'endpoint does not exist'. RFC 7231 §6.5.5 says a known URI with an unsupported method should return 405 with an Allow header listing the supported verbs so clients can correct themselves; 404 is for resources that genuinely don't exist. v0.9.28 introduces a path->methods table that mirrors handleHttpRequest's route registrations, a buildAllowHeader() helper that always surfaces HEAD when GET is supported (because the v0.9.26 HEAD->GET rewrite makes that universally true) and always surfaces OPTIONS (because the v0.9.27 preflight short-circuit handles it), and a 405 fall-through that fires before staticFileResponse when the path is in the table but the verb isn't. HttpResponse gained an extraHeaders field so the Allow header can ride alongside the standard CORS block.
entries: feat(http): supportedMethodsForPath() table enumerates every registered operator API route and its supported verbs.
feat(http): buildAllowHeader() canonicalizes the Allow value, adding HEAD where GET is supported and OPTIONS unconditionally.

feat(http): 405 Method Not Allowed fires before staticFileResponse fall-through when the path is known but the verb isn't, with body { succeeded: false, message: 'Method Not Allowed', path, method, allow } and an Allow response header per RFC 7231 §6.5.5.
feat(http): HttpResponse.extraHeaders carries arbitrary response-specific headers; SimpleHttpServer::sendResponse emits them after the standard CORS block.
docs(version): bump 0.9.27 -> 0.9.28 (RFC 7231 §6.5.5 compliance; patch bump per scheme).

history.summary: CORS preflight + headers on the operator API. Pre-v0.9.27 OPTIONS preflight requests returned 404 and no response carried Access-Control-Allow-* headers. Browser-based dashboards from a different origin (e.g. a debug UI on http://localhost:9999, a dev server on a different port, or a static-file viewer) refused to send the actual cross-origin request because preflight failed. v0.9.27: SimpleHttpServer short-circuits OPTIONS with HTTP 204 + permissive CORS headers, and every response (including GET, POST, HEAD) now carries Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, Access-Control-Allow-Methods, Access-Control-Allow-Headers, and Access-Control-Max-Age: 86400. The wildcard origin is correct under the MCOS LAN-trust model (ADR-001: no app-layer auth; any LAN client that can reach the port is by definition authorized) -- the wildcard doesn't grant access that wasn't already implicit. The two stale test pools (test, x) accidentally registered during the v0.9.27 bug-hunt's Content-Type and Content-Length probes were also cleaned up via /api/pools/{id}/remove.
entries: feat(http): SimpleHttpServer::handleClient short-circuits OPTIONS with HTTP 204 + suppressBody=true, returning headers only.

feat(http): SimpleHttpServer::sendResponse adds Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS, Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, X-MCOS-Client-Id, X-MCOS-Client-Type, Access-Control-Max-Age: 86400 on every response.

history.summary: HEAD requests on operator endpoints now return GET-equivalent headers (RFC 7231 §4.3.2). Pre-v0.9.26 every HEAD on /api/* fell through the SimpleHttpServer's GET-only matching to the 404 fallthrough -- common monitoring tools (Pingdom, Uptime Robot, healthchecks.io, curl -I) saw every operator route as dead. Fix: SimpleHttpServer rewrites HEAD->GET for handler dispatch, then the new HttpResponse::suppressBody flag tells sendResponse to emit headers + Content-Length matching the body size but skip the body itself. Every existing GET route handles HEAD transparently with no per-route changes.
entries: fix(http): SimpleHttpServer treats HEAD as GET for handler dispatch and suppresses body in sendResponse. Status, headers, Content-Type, and Content-Length all match the GET equivalent; only the response body bytes are skipped. Per RFC 7231 §4.3.2.
feat(http): HttpResponse gained suppressBody:bool. Defaults to false; SimpleHttpServer sets it to true on the HEAD path.

history.summary: Dashboard reachability now reflects supervised-pool truth, not legacy TCP probe. v0.9.25 bug-hunt found: an mcp-server (or sub-agent) entry with a healthy supervised pool was reported as reach=false on the dashboard, because the reachability probe was hitting the inventory's legacy host:port (e.g. 192.168.1.7:7101 for filesystem) -- which has nothing listening, since the supervised pool's worker runs as an MCOS-spawned stdio child, not a TCP listener. Operator-visible bug: filesystem MCP serves 14 tools through the gateway end-to-end, but the dashboard shows it as red. v0.9.25 fix: when an mcp-server / sub-agent has a supervised pool with at least one Ready instance, override reachable=true and endpointHostPort='stdio (supervised pool: N ready instance(s))'. The legacy TCP probe still runs and populates the cache for entries WITHOUT a supervised pool (so the inventory's red dots for unimplemented servers stay accurate); the truth wins only when a real pool is alive.
entries: fix(admin-api): SubAgentRuntimeStat + McpServerRuntimeStat get reachable=true and endpointHostPort='stdio (supervised pool: N ready instance(s))' when their poolId resolves to a supervised pool with readyCount>0. Pre-v0.9.25 the reachability dot reflected only the legacy TCP probe to the inventory's host:port, which is meaningless for stdio-supervised pools. Live impact: the 5 currently-supervised pools (baseline-tools, memory, sequential-thinking, filesystem, sqlite) flip from red to green on the dashboard. Inventory entries WITHOUT a supervised pool (the dormant 23-server catalog stubs) keep their TCP-probe-based reachability so the red dots there remain honest.

history.summary: POST /api/pools/{poolId}/restart -- hot-rotate a pool's workers atomically. Pre-v0.9.23 operators rotating a pool had to send the existing /remove + (manual re-register) + /scale sequence, which is racy if monitoring polls between the calls. v0.9.23 wires those three steps as a single restart operation: snapshot the current pool definition, removePool, upsertPool with the same definition, ensureMinInstances. Use cases: a misbehaving worker that hasn't crossed the quarantine threshold, a config-file change to the pool's environment that needs a respawn to take effect, or a precautionary rotation after a memory-leak suspicion. No new behavior in the supervisor; pure convenience composition.
entries: feat(api): POST /api/pools/{poolId}/restart accepts an empty body, snapshots the current pool definition (so the executable + args + env + scale policy survive the round-trip), calls removePool then upsertPool then ensureMinInstances, and returns OperationResult { succeeded:true, message:'Pool restarted (removed + re-registered + scaled to minInstances): ...' }. Persistence + tools/list cache invalidation + Telemetry::Worker::Info event handled inline matching the existing remove/upsert/scale handlers.

history.summary: GET /api/health/summary -- single-URL operational health view. Stitches version + gateway state + tool count + pool roster (healthy/quarantined) + activity-ring persistence health + host-telemetry sampler liveness into one JSON object so monitoring tools and deploy gates can poll one endpoint instead of fanning out across /api/health, /api/gateway/status, /api/gateway/tools, /api/pools, /api/activity/health, and /api/host/telemetry. Top-level 'ok' boolean: true iff gateway is running AND every pool with minInstances>0 has a healthy instance AND zero pools dark or quarantined AND activity-ring persistence has no errors AND the telemetry sampler has produced at least one reading (capturedAtUtc non-empty). Also moves the iteration-stop-gate hook from settings.local.json into settings.json so it's actually loaded into the running session, addressing the third 'are you stalled?' interruption.
entries: feat(api): GET /api/health/summary returns { version, time, gateway:{state,toolCount,mcpUrl,adapterType}, pools:{total,withHealthyInstance,totalHealthyInstances,darkOrQuarantined}, activityRing:{enabled,bytesOnDisk,totalAppends,totalAppendErrors}, hostTelemetry:{sampleAvailable,capturedAtUtc,cpuPercent,memoryPercent}, ok }. The 'ok' flag is the conjunction of every health signal so monitoring tools can use a single boolean.

fix(hooks): the iteration-stop-gate hook moved from .claude/settings.local.json into .claude/settings.json. settings.local.json wasn't being loaded into the active session, so the gate was inert. settings.json is the canonical loaded config; the gate runs alongside the existing echo-style stop reminder.

history.summary: Activity-ring persistence health surface. Closes the v0.9.13 deferred item: pre-v0.9.17 the ActivityEventRing's appendToDiskLocked silently swallowed disk-write failures (catch (...) {}), so operators had no signal that persistence was failing. v0.9.17 adds counters (persistedAppendCount_, persistedAppendErrorCount_), a captured-most-recent error message, and a public PersistenceHealth getter exposed at GET /api/activity/health. The endpoint returns persistenceEnabled, filePath, bytesOnDisk (file_size lookup), totalAppends, totalAppendErrors, lastErrorMessage, lastErrorSecondsAgo, inMemoryRingSize, highWaterMarkId, and a top-level ok flag (persistenceEnabled AND zero errors). Also bumps the catch blocks in appendToDiskLocked from catch (...) {} to specific catch (const std::exception&) + catch (...) so we can capture the exception text. Use case: deploy retro after a crash, operator hits /api/activity/health and immediately sees if disk persistence was healthy.
entries: feat(activity-health): ActivityEventRing gained persistedAppendCount_ + persistedAppendErrorCount_ + lastPersistErrorMessage_ + lastPersistErrorAt_. appendToDiskLocked increments the counters and captures the error message on each open / write failure (was: silently swallowed).

feat(activity-health): public ActivityEventRing::persistenceHealth() returns a snapshot struct PersistenceHealth { persistenceEnabled, filePath, bytesOnDisk, totalAppends, totalAppendErrors, lastErrorMessage, lastErrorSecondsAgo, inMemoryRingSize, highWaterMarkId } under mutex_.

history.summary: Dashboard latency: from 6.6s spike-prone (v0.9.13) to 34ms median, zero spikes. Three coordinated fixes in v0.9.16: (1) reachability prober walks listEndpoints() + listGateways() so synthetic platform-gateway endpoints are no longer cache-miss-on-every-snapshot; (2) probeReachabilityCached gained a default-false allowSynchronousProbe flag -- snapshot calls take the new non-blocking path (return cached value or false; never block on probe), prober calls explicitly opt into synchronous probing to populate the cache; (3) AdminApiService::snapshot() now calls inventoryService_->refreshAsync() instead of refresh() -- the contract comment explicitly says refreshAsync exists 'so the admin API doesn't block for 10+ seconds while N endpoints are TCP-probed sequentially', but it was only used in mutating handlers, not the snapshot. The 10+ second blocking refresh inside the snapshot was the dominant slow path; switching to refreshAsync eliminates it. **Measured: 12-call sweep median 34ms, max 370ms, 0 of 12 calls over 1 second.** Compare v0.9.13 baseline of 6946ms cold + 6.6s warm and v0.9.15's 4-of-12 calls at 5-19s.
entries: feat(admin-api): reachability prober walks the union {inventoryService_->listEndpoints(), platformServiceCatalogService_->listGateways()} so it covers the synthetic platform-gateway endpoints the snapshot adds inline.

feat(admin-api): probeReachabilityCached gained allowSynchronousProbe parameter (default false). Snapshot callers never block: cache miss returns last-known-good or false, prober refreshes asynchronously. Prober explicitly passes true to opt into synchronous-probe behavior.

fix(admin-api): snapshot() calls inventoryService_->refreshAsync() instead of refresh(). The contract comment on refreshAsync was explicit about the 10+ second blocking-refresh problem; this had been the dominant slow path on every dashboard read.

history.summary: Background async reachability prober. v0.9.14 cut steady-state /api/dashboard latency from 6946ms to 36ms via a 30s TTL cache, but kept a periodic 6-20s spike every 30s when the TTL expired and the next snapshot had to re-sweep all probes synchronously. v0.9.15 eliminates the spike entirely. AdminApiService now starts a background thread on construction (StartReachabilityProber) that walks the inventory every kReachabilityProberIntervalMs (10s) and re-probes every endpoint, refreshing the cache before its TTL expires. Snapshot calls always read from a warm cache. Same interruptible-cv shutdown pattern as BeaconService and the supervisor watchdog so destructor stops the prober promptly. Net effect: dashboard latency is consistently <100ms regardless of how recently a TTL cycle expired.
entries: feat(admin-api): AdminApiService gained reachabilityProberThread_ + reachabilityProberRunning_ + reachabilityProberSleepMutex_ + reachabilityProberSleepCv_. New methods StartReachabilityProber (started from runtime composition root after AttachWorkerLayer) and StopReachabilityProber (called from the new ~AdminApiService destructor). Thread settles 2s then loops every 10s, walking inventoryService_->listEndpoints() and calling probeReachabilityCached on each.

feat(admin-api): kReachabilityProberIntervalMs=10000, kReachabilityProberSettleMs=2000. Interval is comfortably under the 30s cache TTL so even a slow cold sweep fits within one validity window. Settle prevents premature probing while inventory hydrates at boot.

feat(admin-api): ~AdminApiService override + StopReachabilityProber call ensure the background thread joins before the service object dies. Symmetric with v0.9.6 supervisor watchdog and v0.9.11 BeaconService shutdown patterns.

history.summary: Dashboard latency fix: cached reachability probes. v0.9.14 bug-hunt found that /api/dashboard took 6.6s warm and 20s cold because the snapshot path did a serial 200ms TCP-connect probe for every sub-agent and every mcp-server endpoint -- with ~30 endpoints in a typical install that's 6+ seconds of pure probe time on every refresh, blocking both the WinUI shell's snapshot loop and the browser dashboard's auto-refresh. v0.9.14 adds a per-host:port reachability cache with kReachabilityCacheTtlSeconds=30 (initially 5s but the first measurement showed that with full-sweep cold probes taking ~6s, a 5s TTL was already expired by the time the sweep finished, so every subsequent snapshot re-probed everything; bumped to 30s after live measurement). Refreshes within the TTL serve from an O(1) map lookup instead of re-probing. The probe code itself (non-blocking TCP connect with 200ms select timeout, getaddrinfo for both IPv4 + IPv6) is unchanged and lives in a single helper probeReachabilityCached() shared by the sub-agent and mcp-server snapshot paths -- previously the same ~60 lines of WinSock were duplicated inline. **Measured live: dashboard latency dropped from 6946ms cold to 32-41ms cache-hit -- a ~180x speedup.** Periodic spike every 30s when the TTL expires (re-probes take 6-20s); that's a v0.9.15 candidate (background warm-up thread).
entries: feat(admin-api): AdminApiService gained a reachabilityCache_ map keyed by 'host:port' string + a 5s TTL. probeReachabilityCached(host, port, outHostPort) checks the cache, probes only on miss, updates the cache on probe completion. The host:port formatting (RFC 3986 IPv6 brackets per v0.8.2) is shared with the cache key.

refactor(admin-api): both probe sites in DashboardSnapshot snapshot() (sub-agent stats around line 10285, mcp-server stats around line 10429) now call probeReachabilityCached. Removes ~120 lines of duplicated WinSock code; same probe semantics; same cache.

history.summary: Persistent activity ring. Operator directive: continue iteration. v0.9.13 closes the activity-ring-loses-on-restart issue documented in v0.9.10's risk list. Pre-v0.9.13 the global ActivityEventRing was in-memory only; a wedge / crash / SCM force-stop lost all forensic data, including the v0.9.10 supervisor_shutdown_wedge events that were specifically designed for post-mortem analysis. v0.9.13 persists every append to a JSON-lines file at AppPaths::dataDirectory/activity.jsonl. On runtime construction the ring loads up to kPersistedTailMax (= kCapacity = 512) most-recent events from the file before the in-memory ring starts accepting new appends; nextSequence_ is bumped to the highest persisted id so new events get fresh ids. The file is truncated and rewritten when it crosses kFileBytesSoftMax (8MB) to bound on-disk size, matching the in-memory ring's semantics. Persistence is best-effort: I/O failures are swallowed so they cannot disrupt the in-memory append path. Operators reading /api/activity after a restart now see the prior process's tail events alongside the new run's events, which is exactly what deploy retros and post-mortem analysis need.
entries: feat(activity): ActivityEventRing gained setPersistencePath, appendToDiskLocked, rewriteFromRingLocked, loadFromDiskLocked. Construction-time hook in MasterControlApplication::Impl::initialize wires it to AppPaths::dataDirectory/activity.jsonl FIRST, before any other service constructs, so subsequent gateway-boot / configuration-load / pool-registration appends get persisted from the very first event.

feat(activity): file-size cap. When activity.jsonl crosses 8 MB the ring rewrites the file from its in-memory contents (truncating older history). 8 MB ~= weeks of typical traffic at the current event rate.

feat(activity): load-on-construct repopulates the ring with up to kPersistedTailMax events from the persisted file. Malformed lines (corrupt file, partial writes, prior-version schema) are skipped silently so a bad file can't poison the new run.
docs(version): bump 0.9.12 -> 0.9.13 (persistent activity ring; patch bump per scheme).

history.summary: Watchdog uses condition_variable wait_for (symmetric with v0.9.11 BeaconService change). Pre-v0.9.12 the WorkerSupervisor watchdog used a 100ms-step polled sleep that observed watchdogRunning_=false within at most 100ms; functional but inconsistent with BeaconService's v0.9.11 cv-based interruptible sleep. v0.9.12 replaces it with std::condition_variable::wait_for guarded by watchdogSleepMutex_ + watchdogSleepCv_. The destructor now notify_all()s the cv before falling into the bounded-join path, so the watchdog wakes within microseconds of watchdogRunning_=false instead of waiting for the next 100ms polling tick. Two small benefits: (a) symmetry with BeaconService makes the codebase easier to reason about; (b) the worst-case shutdown is now bounded by one in-flight reap+drain cycle (a few ms) instead of '100ms sleep + reap'. Persistent activity ring deferred to v0.9.13.
entries: feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor gained watchdogSleepMutex_ + watchdogSleepCv_. Watchdog loop uses wait_for with !watchdogRunning_ predicate. Destructor notify_all()s before bounded join.

history.summary: Interruptible BeaconService::stop(). Operator directive: continue iteration. v0.9.11 closes the BeaconService stop blocking observed in the v0.9.10 deploy: service stop measured 14.8s because BeaconService::stop() called worker_.join() and the worker was sleeping for the full beaconBroadcastIntervalSeconds (15s default) between broadcasts; the running_=false flag wasn't observed until the sleep ended. v0.9.11 replaces std::this_thread::sleep_for with std::condition_variable::wait_for guarded by a sleepMutex_ + sleepCv_; stop() flips running_, notify_all()s the cv under the lock, and the worker wakes immediately. Both the broadcast-interval sleep and the disabled-state poll use the same interruptible pattern. Service stop now completes in well under one second once the beacon worker is the only thing holding it up.
entries: feat(beacon): BeaconService gained sleepMutex_ + sleepCv_. start() worker uses std::condition_variable::wait_for for both the broadcast-interval sleep and the beacon-disabled poll, with a predicate of !running_.load(). stop() acquires the mutex, calls notify_all(), then joins; the worker wakes immediately on notification and exits within ms.
