history.summary: GET /mcp returns 405 + watchdog detach diagnostic. Operator directive: continue iteration. v0.9.10 closes one item deferred from v0.9.9 plus a real edge-case bug surfaced by the v0.9.10 bug-hunt probe. (1) GET on the /mcp path now returns HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed with a structured JSON body identifying the received method. Pre-v0.9.10 a GET request against /mcp produced HTTP 204 No Content because the empty body parsed to {} which the dispatcher classified as a JSON-RPC notification (no id, no method); from the client perspective that was an opaque 'success' for a request that should have been refused. JSON-RPC over Streamable HTTP uses POST for client->server messages; GET is reserved for opening an SSE stream (which this gateway does not currently implement). The new 405 response includes a 'method_received' field naming the verb (GET / PUT / DELETE / HEAD / OPTIONS / TRACE / CONNECT / OTHER) and a 'hint' explaining the contract. (2) Watchdog detach diagnostic event. When the v0.9.9 bounded-shutdown path takes the detach branch (watchdog didn't exit within kShutdownJoinTimeoutMs), v0.9.10 now appends a supervisor_shutdown_wedge ActivityEvent to the global activity ring with a snapshot of supervisor state at detach time (pool count + child count). Pre-v0.9.10 the detach was silent; post-mortem analysis had no signal that a wedge had occurred or what shape MCOS was in when it did. The new event is keyed kind=supervisor_shutdown_wedge actor=supervisor-destructor target=watchdog-thread statusCode=504 so operators can grep /api/activity for it during deploy retros.
entries: feat(gateway): /mcp now requires POST. GET / PUT / DELETE / HEAD / OPTIONS / TRACE / CONNECT all return HTTP 405 with a structured JSON body { error, path, method_received, hint }. Pre-v0.9.10 GET /mcp returned 204 No Content because the empty body parsed as a notification.

feat(supervisor): supervisor_shutdown_wedge ActivityEvent emitted on watchdog detach. Carries pool count + child count at detach time so post-mortem patterns are visible (e.g. wedge always with respawn-in-flight = CreateProcessW pattern; wedge always with high children = inherited-handle exhaustion pattern; wedge always with high pools = mutex_ contention pattern). New helper appendSupervisorWedgeActivity defined alongside the activity ring.
docs(version): bump 0.9.9 -> 0.9.10 (HTTP method check + wedge diagnostic; patch bump per scheme).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: MCOS sub-agent runner (carried).
PHASE-13 Win2D rendering (carried).
SSE upgrade for Streamable HTTP transport (newly explicit in v0.9.10's 405 hint message). Until SSE lands, GET /mcp is a clean 405 error rather than an opaque 204.

history.summary: Bounded watchdog shutdown + dedicated quarantine struct. Operator directive: continue iteration until MCOS is fully operational and bug-free. v0.9.9 closes both items deferred from v0.9.8: (1) Bounded watchdog shutdown -- the WorkerSupervisor destructor previously called watchdogThread_.join() unconditionally, which blocks indefinitely if the watchdog is wedged inside a long reap (e.g., a CreateProcessW hang during respawn at the moment the SCM stop arrives). The v0.9.8 deploy hit this directly: SCM showed StopPending for 60+ seconds and had to be force-killed. v0.9.9 destructor now flips watchdogRunning_, then joins with a 5-second deadline; if the join doesn't complete, both the watchdog thread and the joiner are detached. The OS reclaims them at process exit. Strictly better than blocking SCM forever. (2) Dedicated QuarantineLogEntry struct -- replaces the v0.9.8 ad-hoc encoding (DeathLogEntry with empty instanceId and exitCode = failure-count-in-window). The new struct carries poolId + failureCountInWindow + windowSeconds + cooldownSeconds as named fields. drainAndEmitSupervisorEvents has separate emit loops for deaths vs quarantines; the consumer side is much clearer. Operator-visible message shape is unchanged.
entries: feat(supervisor): bounded watchdog shutdown. WorkerSupervisor destructor flips watchdogRunning_ to false, then spawns a helper thread that calls watchdogThread_.join() and signals completion via std::atomic<bool>. The destructor polls the flag every 50ms with a deadline of kShutdownJoinTimeoutMs (5000ms). On timeout, both the watchdog and the joiner are detached -- the process is exiting and the OS reclaims them. Pre-v0.9.9 the destructor blocked SCM indefinitely if the watchdog was wedged.

feat(supervisor): dedicated QuarantineLogEntry struct. Carries {poolId, failureCountInWindow, windowSeconds, cooldownSeconds}. Reap path pushes into quarantineLog_ instead of conflating with deathLog_. drainAndEmitSupervisorEvents has a separate quarantine emit loop. Cleaner intent + the consumer message reads naturally without packing field semantics into the exitCode slot.
docs(version): bump 0.9.8 -> 0.9.9 (bounded shutdown + cleaner quarantine struct; patch bump per scheme).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: MCOS sub-agent runner (carried from v0.9.4-v0.9.8). Architecture decision pending.
PHASE-13 Win2D rendering (carried from v0.9.3-v0.9.8).
Watchdog wedge ROOT CAUSE not pinned. v0.9.9 makes shutdown bounded but doesn't diagnose WHY the wedge happened. v0.9.10 candidate: log the watchdog state on detach so we can spot the wedge pattern.

history.summary: Crash circuit breaker + humanized exit codes + dual-emit to /api/activity. Operator directive: continue iteration until MCOS is fully operational and bug-free. v0.9.8 closes the three top items deferred from v0.9.7. (1) Per-pool crash circuit breaker: WorkerSupervisor tracks worker-death timestamps in a rolling kCrashWindowSeconds (60s) window per pool. When a pool sees kCrashThreshold (5) crashes within the window it is marked QUARANTINED for kQuarantineCooldownSeconds (60s); during quarantine the auto-respawn loop in reapDeadInstancesLocked skips that pool, sparing the activity log from a runaway respawn-loop and giving operators a chance to diagnose the misbehaving worker. POST /api/pools/{id}/scale remains an explicit override (does not consult the quarantine). The watchdog tick prunes aged-out timestamps so the rolling window stays bounded. (2) Humanized exit codes: humanizeExitCode(code) maps common Windows exit/NTSTATUS values to short readable labels -- STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION, STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN, STATUS_HEAP_CORRUPTION, STATUS_PROCESS_KILLED, etc. The death message now reads e.g. 'exitCode=4294967295 (STATUS_PROCESS_KILLED -- forced kill / taskkill /f / Stop-Process -Force)' instead of just 'exitCode=4294967295'. (3) Dual-emit to /api/activity: drainAndEmitSupervisorEvents now appends pool_worker_death, pool_worker_respawn, and pool_quarantine entries to the global activity ring in addition to TelemetryAggregator. Pre-v0.9.8 only /api/telemetry/events surfaced these events; the older audit-log surface (/api/activity, used by the dashboard's Recent Activity panel and operator audits) was blind to them.
entries: feat(supervisor): per-pool crash circuit breaker. poolFailureTimestamps_ tracks recent worker-death timestamps per pool in a rolling 60s window; poolQuarantineUntil_ records cool-down endpoints. When the window count crosses 5, the pool is quarantined: the auto-respawn pass in reapDeadInstancesLocked skips it and emits a synthetic Telemetry::Worker::Error and ActivityEvent kind=pool_quarantine. POST /api/pools/{id}/scale (ensureMinInstances) is the explicit override and does NOT consult the quarantine. Watchdog tick (every 2s) prunes aged-out failure timestamps and expired quarantine entries so the bookkeeping is bounded.

feat(supervisor): humanizeExitCode(code) helper. Maps common NTSTATUS / exit codes to short labels: 0x00000000 -> STATUS_SUCCESS; 0xC0000005 -> STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION (segfault / null deref); 0xC000001D -> STATUS_ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION; 0xC0000094 -> STATUS_INTEGER_DIVIDE_BY_ZERO; 0xC0000409 -> STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN (/GS); 0xC000013A -> STATUS_CONTROL_C_EXIT; 0xC0000142 -> STATUS_DLL_INIT_FAILED; 0xC0000374 -> STATUS_HEAP_CORRUPTION; 0xC0000017 -> STATUS_NO_MEMORY; 0xFFFFFFFF -> STATUS_PROCESS_KILLED (forced kill / taskkill /f / Stop-Process -Force). Death-event message now embeds the human-readable label alongside the raw code.

feat(supervisor+activity): drainAndEmitSupervisorEvents now appends pool_worker_death, pool_worker_respawn, and pool_quarantine entries to the global activity ring. New helper appendPoolLifecycleActivity(kind, poolId, message, statusCodeHint) defined alongside globalActivityRing(); forward-declared near the supervisor class so the supervisor can dual-emit without seeing the ActivityEventRing class definition (which is declared later in the same TU).
docs(version): bump 0.9.7 -> 0.9.8 (crash circuit breaker + humanized exit codes + dual-emit; patch bump per scheme).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: MCOS sub-agent runner (carried from v0.9.4-v0.9.7). Architecture decision pending.
PHASE-13 Win2D rendering (carried from v0.9.3-v0.9.7).
Quarantine event uses an ad-hoc encoding (DeathLogEntry with empty instanceId, exitCode = failure count). Cleaner separate struct lands in v0.9.9.

history.summary: Activity-ring telemetry for worker death + auto-respawn. Operator directive: continue iteration until MCOS is fully operational. v0.9.7 closes the top item deferred from v0.9.5 / v0.9.6: WorkerSupervisor now emits Telemetry::Worker::Error on every reap (worker exited unexpectedly) and Telemetry::Worker::Info on every auto-respawn. The events surface in /api/activity, /api/telemetry/events, and the operator dashboard's Recent Telemetry Events panel. Pre-v0.9.7 the supervisor's deathLog_ / respawnLog_ vectors collected events but no consumer drained them; operators saw nothing in the activity surface when a worker crashed and got auto-restarted. With this release the operator sees, e.g., 'Pool worker memory#3 exited unexpectedly (was state=ready, exitCode=0) ... Pool memory auto-respawned to instance memory#7 (watchdog or reap-driven)' in the activity surface within the 2s watchdog interval. Implementation: WorkerSupervisor gained setTelemetryAggregator(std::shared_ptr<ITelemetryAggregator>) and an internal telemetryAggregator_ member; the runtime's composition root wires the aggregator after construction (the supervisor itself is constructed first to keep the watchdog thread independent of telemetry being available). The watchdog's 2s loop now drains the death/respawn vectors after each reap pass and emits one TelemetryEvent per entry through the aggregator, with mutex_ released for the emit (preventing lock-ordering deadlock with the aggregator's own internal lock). listPools() and findPool() also drain+emit at the end of their reap pass so operator-API-driven reaps surface events immediately rather than waiting for the next watchdog tick.
entries: feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor gained setTelemetryAggregator(std::shared_ptr<ITelemetryAggregator>) and a private telemetryAggregator_ member. Runtime composition root wires it after telemetryAggregator_ is constructed. Tests + supervised-mock path leave it null (drainAndEmit is a no-op when aggregator is null).

feat(supervisor): drainAndEmitSupervisorEvents() helper. Two-phase: (1) under mutex_ swap-out deathLog_ and respawnLog_ to local vectors and copy the aggregator shared_ptr; (2) release mutex_ and emit one TelemetryEvent per entry. The two-phase pattern prevents lock-ordering deadlock with the aggregator's internal lock.

feat(supervisor): watchdog loop calls drainAndEmitSupervisorEvents() after each reapDeadInstancesLocked() pass. listPools() and findPool() also drain+emit at the end of their reap pass so operator-API-driven reaps surface events immediately.

feat(telemetry): death events emit as Telemetry::Worker::Error with message 'Pool worker {instanceId} exited unexpectedly (was state={prevState}, exitCode={code})' and poolId set; respawn events emit as Telemetry::Worker::Info with message 'Pool {poolId} auto-respawned to instance {newInstanceId} (watchdog or reap-driven)'. Both events flow through TelemetryAggregator into the activity ring, /api/telemetry/events, and the operator dashboard.
docs(version): bump 0.9.6 -> 0.9.7 (activity-ring telemetry for death + auto-respawn; patch bump per scheme).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: MCOS sub-agent runner (carried from v0.9.4 / v0.9.5 / v0.9.6). Architecture decision pending.
PHASE-13 Win2D rendering (carried from v0.9.3 / v0.9.4 / v0.9.5 / v0.9.6).

Per-instance crash counter / circuit breaker for misbehaving workers. Today a worker that crashes repeatedly will be respawned every 2s indefinitely; v0.9.8 candidate to add an N-failures-in-M-seconds gate that quarantines the pool after a threshold.

history.summary: Proactive watchdog + immediate cache invalidation on pool changes. Operator directive: 'continue working until all features of MCOS are working ... continue in this way until the MCOS is fully operational and error and bug free.' v0.9.6 closes the two top items deferred from v0.9.5: (1) WorkerSupervisor gained a background watchdog thread that calls reapDeadInstancesLocked() every 2 seconds, so a pool whose only worker died while no LAN-client traffic was arriving is now detected and respawned within 2s of the death rather than waiting for the next operator query. Pre-v0.9.6 the reap was opportunistic (ran inside listPools / findPool / ensureMinInstances / scaleUpOnce); a pool with no traffic + a dead worker stayed dark indefinitely on a quiet LAN. The watchdog runs as a single std::thread per supervisor instance, sleeps in 100ms steps so it can stop within 100ms when watchdogRunning_ flips to false, and serializes with operator API calls via the same mutex_ everything else uses. Cost: a few microseconds of GetExitCodeProcess every 2s per child. (2) IMcpGateway gained an InvalidateToolCatalog() method (default no-op so FakeMcpGatewayAdapter inherits safely; NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter implements it by stamping toolCatalogCacheValidUntil_ to epoch). The runtime calls it from the four pool-admin POST handlers (POST /api/pools, POST /api/pools/{id}/{remove,scale,drain}) so a pool change surfaces in the next LAN-client tools/list response immediately rather than after the 30s TTL window. Pre-v0.9.6 an operator registering a new pool watched LAN clients show stale tools/list output for up to 30s; now the next request after the POST returns the new shape.
entries: feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor constructor starts a watchdog std::thread that wakes every kWatchdogIntervalMs (2000ms) and calls reapDeadInstancesLocked() under mutex_. The thread sleeps in 100ms steps so the destructor's watchdogRunning_ = false signal is observed promptly and join() returns within 100ms. Idle pools no longer stay dark after a worker crash; auto-respawn runs without operator intervention.
feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor destructor stops the watchdog thread before calling shutdownAll() so we don't race with reap-and-respawn against the same pools we're terminating.

feat(gateway-contract): IMcpGateway gained virtual void InvalidateToolCatalog() with a no-op default. Adapters that don't cache (FakeMcpGatewayAdapter) inherit the no-op; NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter overrides it to stamp toolCatalogCacheValidUntil_ to epoch under mutex_, forcing the next refreshToolCatalogLocked to do a fresh fan-out.

feat(runtime): the four pool-admin POST handlers in MasterControlRuntime.cpp (POST /api/pools, POST /api/pools/{id}/{remove,scale,drain}) call mcpGateway_->InvalidateToolCatalog() after a successful pool change. LAN-client tools/list reflects the change on the very next request.
docs(version): bump 0.9.5 -> 0.9.6 (proactive watchdog + immediate cache invalidation; patch bump per scheme).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Activity-ring telemetry events for worker death + auto-respawn. WorkerSupervisor records events into deathLog_ / respawnLog_ but no consumer drains them yet. v0.9.7 should wire a TelemetryAggregator pointer into WorkerSupervisor's constructor + emit Telemetry::Pool::{Error,Info} events so operators see the watchdog's actions in the activity ring.
MCOS sub-agent runner (carried from v0.9.4 / v0.9.5). Architecture decision pending.
PHASE-13 Win2D rendering (carried from v0.9.3 / v0.9.4 / v0.9.5).

history.summary: Crash recovery + cache TTL + discovery self-test. Operator directive: 'continue working until all features of MCOS are working and clients can reach and use the server ... continue in this way until the MCOS is fully operational and error and bug free.' v0.9.5 closes four real bugs surfaced by the v0.9.4 follow-up bug-hunt probe and three deferred items from v0.9.4. (1) Worker death detection: WorkerSupervisor::reapDeadInstancesLocked() walks every pool, calls GetExitCodeProcess on each child handle, and for any process whose exit code is no longer STILL_ACTIVE: closes handles, drops the children_ record, erases the instance from pool.instances, and queues a DeathLogEntry for the activity ring. Wired into listPools(), findPool(), ensureMinInstances(), and scaleUpOnce() so every operator query AND every spawn-decision sees fresh state. Pre-v0.9.5 the supervisor learned about child death only when an outbound sendStdioJsonRpc write failed, and even then it only returned the bridge error -- the pool's instance listing kept advertising the dead instance as Ready until the next operator-driven scale or remove. (2) Auto-respawn after death: reapDeadInstancesLocked()'s second pass spawns replacements for any pool whose healthyInstanceCount fell below scalePolicy.minInstances. Operators no longer need to POST /api/pools/{poolId}/scale to bring a crashed worker back. (3) Healthy instance count: ensureMinInstances + scaleUpOnce now use healthyInstanceCountLocked(pool) instead of pool.instances.size(). Pre-v0.9.5 a pool with one Failed instance and zero Ready instances was reported as 'at minInstances' (because the Failed one counted as one), blocking respawn. (4) Failed-state propagation in sendStdioJsonRpc: when WriteFile to the child's stdin fails, we now atomically (under mutex_) close handles, drop the children_ record, and erase the instance from pool.instances + queue a death event -- so the very next listPools call sees correct state without waiting for the next reap pass. (5) VERSION.json wired into CMake's CMAKE_CONFIGURE_DEPENDS so a version bump alone triggers re-configure -> regen MasterControlVersion.h -> rebuild any TU that pulls in MASTERCONTROL_VERSION. Pre-v0.9.5 a VERSION.json bump alone left MasterControlVersion.h stale and the running binary kept reporting the previous version through the MCP initialize handshake until an unrelated CMakeLists.txt edit forced a reconfigure (workaround in v0.9.4 was to touch CMakeLists.txt manually). (6) tools/list catalog cache TTL (30s default): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::refreshToolCatalogLocked now serves from toolCatalogCache_ when toolCatalogCacheValidUntil_ has not yet been crossed. Pre-v0.9.5 every LAN-client tools/list call fanned out a fresh tools/list to every supervised pool's child via stdio (5 round-trips per request with the v0.9.4 5-pool catalog); now a burst of identical requests is O(1) after the first. The TTL is short enough that operator-driven pool changes surface within 30s. (7) New scripts/check-mcos-discovery.ps1 self-test: validates /.well-known/mcos.json + /api/discovery + /api/beacon shape, listens on UDP/7301 for a real beacon emission with a 6-second deadline, best-effort DNS-SD PTR lookup for _mcos._tcp.local, and onboarding-profile shape across all five typed client types. Exit 0 = all green; exit 1 = at least one failure with a structured per-check report on stdout. Designed to run from CI or an operator console; complements scripts/check-mastercontrol-forsetti.ps1.
entries: feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::reapDeadInstancesLocked() in MasterControlRuntime.cpp walks every pool's instances, calls GetExitCodeProcess on each child handle, reaps dead instances (closes Job Object + pipe + process handles, drops children_ entry, erases from pool.instances), then auto-respawns to scalePolicy.minInstances. Wired into listPools(), findPool(), ensureMinInstances(), and scaleUpOnce(). Records each reap into deathLog_ and each respawn into respawnLog_ for future telemetry-aggregator emission (v0.9.6 candidate).

feat(supervisor): healthyInstanceCountLocked(pool) returns the count of instances in {Starting, Ready, Busy} only. ensureMinInstances + scaleUpOnce use this instead of pool.instances.size() so Failed/Stopped/Draining instances cannot block respawn. Post-reap there should be no Failed/Stopped left in pool.instances anyway, but keep the healthy-only count in case a future code path leaves a Failed instance in place for inspection.

feat(supervisor): sendStdioJsonRpc's WriteFile-failure path now (a) takes mutex_ and tears down the dead child's handles + children_ entry, (b) erases the instance from its pool.instances vector, (c) queues a DeathLogEntry. Pre-v0.9.5 the failure returned without updating state, leaving the dead instance visible to subsequent listPools/findPool until the reap pass.

feat(build): set_property(DIRECTORY APPEND PROPERTY CMAKE_CONFIGURE_DEPENDS "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/VERSION.json") in CMakeLists.txt. Bumping VERSION.json now triggers a configure step on the next build, which regenerates MasterControlVersion.h with the new MASTERCONTROL_VERSION literal so the running binary reports the right version through MCP initialize + serverInfo + the operator dashboard's version chip. v0.9.4 had to touch CMakeLists.txt manually to force a reconfigure.

feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter gained toolCatalogCacheValidUntil_ + kToolCatalogCacheTtlSeconds (default 30). refreshToolCatalogLocked serves from toolCatalogCache_ when the TTL hasn't expired, dropping per-tools/list cost from O(pools) stdio round-trips to a single pointer copy on cache hit. Header gained #include <chrono>. v0.9.6 candidate: explicit cache invalidation on POST /api/pools / POST /api/pools/{poolId}/{remove,scale,drain} so operator pool changes surface immediately rather than after the TTL.

feat(scripts): new scripts/check-mcos-discovery.ps1. Six structured checks (well-known doc, /api/discovery, /api/beacon, UDP/7301 listen, DNS-SD lookup, all five onboarding profiles). Exit 0/1 per pass/fail; per-check PASS/FAIL/SKIP with a one-line evidence string. Validates the v0.9.4 admin block + mcp-2025-03-26 capability shape so a regression on those breaks the script.
docs(version): bump 0.9.4 -> 0.9.5 (crash recovery + cache TTL + discovery self-test; patch bump per scheme).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: Activity-ring telemetry events for pool death + respawn. The v0.9.5 supervisor records DeathLogEntry / RespawnLogEntry into deathLog_ / respawnLog_ but no consumer drains them yet. v0.9.6 should wire a TelemetryAggregator pointer into WorkerSupervisor's constructor and emit Telemetry::Pool::{Error,Info} events.

Background watchdog thread for proactive death detection. Today the reap is opportunistic (runs on listPools / findPool / ensureMinInstances / scaleUpOnce). A pool with no traffic and a dead worker stays dark until the next operator query. v0.9.6 candidate: 2-second watchdog tick.

Explicit tools/list cache invalidation on pool changes. Today operator changes surface within the 30s TTL window; immediate visibility would require POST /api/pools and the per-pool action routes to call into the gateway adapter to bump toolCatalogCacheValidUntil_ to the past.
MCOS sub-agent runner (carried from v0.9.4). Architecture decision needed (Claude Agent SDK in C++? out-of-process via mcos-agent-bridge?).
PHASE-13 Win2D rendering (carried from v0.9.3 / v0.9.4).

history.summary: 23-server catalog goes live. Operator directive: 'fix all issues with MCOS, including the ones you found and listed ... continue working until all features of MCOS are working and clients can reach and use the server.' v0.9.4 closes the v0.9.3 deferred item ('Operator-bound external workers for the 23 named pools land in v0.9.x+') and seven MCP-protocol-correctness gaps that surfaced while probing MCOS the way Claude Code, Codex, and Grok actually touch it. (1) Native gateway dispatcher now treats JSON-RPC requests with no `id` as Notifications per JSON-RPC 2.0 §4.1: notifications/initialized + notifications/cancelled + any unrecognized notification get HTTP 204 No Content with empty body, no Content-Type, no Content-Length. Pre-v0.9.4 every notification got back a -32601 envelope with id:null, which strict MCP clients (TypeScript + Python SDKs) treat as a protocol violation. (2) tools/list now forwards each child-reported inputSchema verbatim. McpToolDescriptor gained an inputSchemaJson field; refreshToolCatalogLocked captures tool["inputSchema"].dump() into it; the tools/list handler parses it back and emits the typed schema. Pre-v0.9.4 every tool got an open `{"type":"object"}` placeholder regardless of what the worker reported, leaving schema-driven LAN clients unable to discover argument names from tools/list alone. (3) initialize handshake reports protocolVersion 2025-03-26 (Streamable HTTP transport) instead of the older 2024-11-05 literal -- aligns with what onboarding profiles tell every client to use AND what the DNS-SD TXT advertises as protovers. The same alignment lands in MasterControlBaselineToolsWorker. (4) New /api/host/telemetry route exposes the existing HostTelemetrySnapshot (cpu/memory/disk/network plus host identity) as a stand-alone endpoint so dashboards / operator tooling don't have to scrape it from composite responses. Honest-telemetry: empty capturedAtUtc means 'sampler hasn't taken first reading yet'. (5) Discovery doc gains an admin block listing poolsUrl + clientsUrl + activityUrl + hostTelemetryUrl + gatewayStatusUrl + gatewayToolsUrl + healthUrl so operator dashboards can find the operator surface from /api/discovery without out-of-band knowledge; new capability mcp-2025-03-26 signals protocol-revision parity with the TXT advertisement and the initialize handshake. (6) Self-handshaking stdio bridge: WorkerSupervisor::ChildProcess gained a mcpInitialized flag; sendStdioJsonRpc now performs a lazy initialize -> notifications/initialized handshake on the first call per instance. Pre-v0.9.4 the supervisor sent tools/list directly without an MCP session handshake; spec-compliant MCP servers (every npx-installable @modelcontextprotocol/server-* and similar) refused with -32602 'Server not initialized' and the gateway's tool-catalog refresh silently dropped them. The in-tree baseline-tools worker dispatcher tolerated being called without initialize, which masked the gap pre-v0.9.4 -- baseline-tools 'just worked', but the moment any standards-compliant pool was registered, its 'Ready' instances contributed zero tools. (7) Four built-in MCP server pools registered: memory (knowledge graph), sequential-thinking, filesystem (rooted at MCOS project), sqlite. Each spawned via direct node.exe entry-point (not npx, because npx is a shim that spawns the actual server as an orphaned grandchild whose pid the supervisor cannot track and whose stdio MCOS cannot reach -- v0.9.4 fix: npm install -g, then resolve dist/index.js and spawn node.exe with that absolute path so the MCP server runs as a direct child under the Job Object with stdio wired straight to MCOS). End-to-end verified: gateway tools/list now aggregates 33 tools across 5 pools (baseline-tools 4 + filesystem 14 + memory 9 + sequential-thinking 1 + sqlite 5); tools/call against each pool returns real values (sqlite write+read round-trip, memory entity persisted across calls, filesystem listing the actual project tree, sequential-thinking returning structured thought metadata). 14 generic sub-agents added under .claude/agents/ (architect, sentinel, planner, reviewer, debugger, test-writer, refactorer, documenter, performance-engineer, security-auditor, git-manager, build-resolver, coordinator, multi-file-specialist) complementing the existing 10 mcos-* project-specific agents.
entries: feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::handleMcpRequest detects JSON-RPC notifications (no `id` field) and returns an empty string. The HTTP serving loop maps that to HTTP 204 No Content -- no body, no Content-Type, no Content-Length -- per RFC 7230. Explicit accept-and-ignore for notifications/initialized + notifications/cancelled. Catch-all for any other notification also returns empty.

feat(gateway): tools/list forwards child-reported inputSchema verbatim. McpToolDescriptor gained inputSchemaJson (std::string -- serialized JSON object). NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::refreshToolCatalogLocked captures tool["inputSchema"].dump() into the descriptor; tools/list handler parses it back and emits the typed schema, falling back to {"type":"object"} only when the child didn't supply a schema or the captured string failed to parse.

feat(gateway+worker): protocolVersion advertised on the initialize handshake bumped from 2024-11-05 to 2025-03-26 in BOTH NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::handleMcpRequest AND MasterControlBaselineToolsWorker. Aligns with the DNS-SD TXT protovers field and with the Streamable HTTP transport every onboarding profile recommends.

feat(api): new GET /api/host/telemetry route in MasterControlRuntime.cpp returns the existing HostTelemetrySnapshot serialized via the existing NLOHMANN macro. Honors ADR-002 §9 honest-telemetry: when telemetryService_ is null OR when capturedAtUtc is empty (sampler hasn't run yet), the response is a default-constructed snapshot the consumer can detect via the empty capturedAtUtc.

feat(discovery): DiscoveryAdmin struct added to MasterControlModels.h with poolsUrl + clientsUrl + activityUrl + hostTelemetryUrl + gatewayStatusUrl + gatewayToolsUrl + healthUrl. DiscoveryDocument gained an admin field; NLOHMANN_DEFINE_TYPE_NON_INTRUSIVE_WITH_DEFAULT lists the new struct + the new field. DiscoveryService::currentDocument populates the URLs from adminBase. New capability `mcp-2025-03-26` added to capabilities[] so strict clients can short-circuit negotiation.

feat(supervisor): WorkerSupervisor::ChildProcess gained an mcpInitialized flag. sendStdioJsonRpc now performs a lazy initialize -> notifications/initialized handshake on the first call per instance, with a 3-second deadline. The handshake uses a unique handshake-scoped id ('"mcos-handshake-<instanceId>"') so it cannot collide with caller request ids; banner text from npx / node / the MCP server's startup is filtered out by the same id-matching logic the main read loop uses. Subsequent calls to the same ChildProcess skip the handshake.

feat(deployment): four standard MCP server pools (memory, sequential-thinking, filesystem, sqlite) installed globally via npm install -g and registered through POST /api/pools. Pool template spawns node.exe directly against the resolved dist/index.js for each package. NOT via npx -- npx is a Windows shim that spawns the actual MCP server as a child of itself, so MCOS's stdio bridge ends up wired to the npx parent (which exits) rather than the actual server, and the supervisor's pid tracking gets out of sync with the running process. Direct entry-point spawn keeps the MCP server as a direct MCOS child under the Job Object. Registration script: .claude/mcp-state/register-pools.ps1.

feat(workflow): 14 generic sub-agents added under .claude/agents/: architect, sentinel, planner, reviewer, debugger, test-writer, refactorer, documenter, performance-engineer, security-auditor, git-manager, build-resolver, coordinator, multi-file-specialist. Each is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter following the existing mcos-* agent style; the 14 generic + 10 mcos-* are complementary (mcos-* enforce phase rules + realignment hard rules; generic agents handle engineering quality + cross-cutting concerns). Setup index lives at .claude/ORCHESTRATION-SETUP.md.

feat(claude-code-tooling): three standard MCPs added to .mcp.json (sequential-thinking, filesystem rooted at the MCOS project, sqlite at .claude/mcp-state/mcos-orchestration.sqlite) so Claude Code sessions in this repo have parity with what MCOS now exposes through the gateway. mcos-bridge / mcos-memory / mcos-contracts (Python) and the existing memory MCP entry are unchanged.
docs(version): bump 0.9.3 -> 0.9.4 (23-server catalog goes live + MCP protocol correctness; patch bump per scheme).
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: MCOS sub-agent runner: the 14 generic + 10 mcos-* sub-agents in .claude/agents/ are Claude-Code-format markdown definitions. To register them as MCOS-supervised pools (the realignment vision: 'MCP servers and sub-agents are supervised worker pools behind stable logical endpoints') MCOS needs a sub-agent runner binary that loads a .md agent definition and exposes the agent as an MCP tool. Architecture decision needed (Claude Agent SDK in C++? out-of-process via mcos-agent-bridge?). Tracked in handoff/realignment as a v0.9.x+ design item.

Per-pool input schema cache: refreshToolCatalogLocked re-fetches tools/list (and re-parses inputSchema) on every gateway tools/list call. With 5 pools that's 5 stdio round-trips per LAN client tools/list; could cache for 30s with manual invalidate on pool upsert/scale.
PHASE-13 follow-on: per-instance pool telemetry charts at 60Hz via Win2D CanvasControl; activity stream as scrolling SwapChainPanel. (Carried from v0.9.3.)

history.summary: Real LAN-client connectivity. Operator directive: 'fix all the issues you listed and continue looking for more issues and fixing them until all the issues are fixed and the MCOS is actually working and ready for deployment.' v0.9.3 lands seven blocking + operability fixes that surfaced when probing MCOS the way an external Claude Code / Codex / Grok client touches it. (1) Discovery doc + onboarding profiles now bracket-wrap IPv6 host literals per RFC 3986, so URLs like http://[fde3:c02c:...:32c8]:7300/api/onboarding/codex parse correctly in every HTTP client; pre-v0.9.3 the unbracketed colons clashed with the port separator and PowerShell + every standard parser rejected them as 'Invalid port specified.' (2) Auto-detected primary IP prefers IPv4 over IPv6 over link-local, so dual-stack hosts (the 99% case for LAN AI clients) no longer end up with the discovery doc advertising a bare IPv6 ULA the IPv4-only client can't reach. (3) Gateway boot retries HttpAddUrlToUrlGroup on ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS for up to 2s (8x250ms), which covers HTTP.sys URL-group GC delay after a prior process exited; pre-v0.9.3 a single race with kernel state put the gateway into a permanent failed state. (4) New POST /api/gateway/restart route -- the wedged-gateway recovery path is now an explicit verb, not a knowing /api/gateway/start workaround. (5) Process-singleton mutex Global\MasterControlOrchestrationServerSingleton in MasterControlServiceHost.exe so a second --console invocation while the SCM service is running refuses to start (pre-v0.9.3 the duplicate spawned its own baseline-tools worker even though it could not bind any port). (6) Gateway boot failures recorded as Telemetry::Gateway::Error events in the activity ring; aggregator construction reordered to exist before gateway auto-start so the event actually persists. (7) Settings tab unifies the previously-separate bindAddress + activeProfile.preferredBindAddress fields into one 'LAN Address' control with a detected-IP dropdown built from GetAdaptersAddresses. The Apply path now writes BOTH fields in lockstep so the operator's IP pick actually flows through to the discovery doc. Runtime URL builder precedence reordered: explicit (non-wildcard) bindAddress wins over preferredBindAddress wins over auto-detected primaryIpAddress -- because bindAddress is where MCOS actually listens, so it's the only address LAN clients can actually use.
entries: feat(runtime): bracketIpv6Host helper hoisted into MasterControlRuntime.cpp's anonymous namespace and applied to every host->URL composition site (DiscoveryService::currentDocument adminBase + gateway URLs, OnboardingProfileService::profileFor adminBase + downstream config snippets, browserUrl, composeLanClientConfigBundle mcosServer, claude-code-handoff bundle gatewayUrl + browserUrl, AppleRemoteHostService probe URLs). Pre-v0.9.3 the gateway URL builder bracketed IPv6 but the seven other sites did not, so /api/discovery + /.well-known/mcos.json + /api/onboarding/<client> served URLs that RFC 3986 parsers reject.

feat(runtime): readPrimaryNetworkIdentity now does a three-pass adapter walk -- routable IPv4 first, routable IPv6 second, link-local third -- with link-local IPv4 (169.254.x.x) and IPv6 (fe80::/10) excluded from the routable passes. Pre-v0.9.3 it took whatever Windows surfaced first, which on dual-stack hosts is typically the IPv6 ULA, leaving IPv4-only LAN AI clients with a discovery doc that pointed at an IPv6 host they couldn't reach.

feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::Start retries HttpAddUrlToUrlGroup on ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS up to 8 times at 250ms each (2s total budget), covering HTTP.sys URL-group GC after a prior process exited. ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED is NOT retried (it's a URL ACL / privilege misconfiguration that won't fix itself). The terminal failure message now distinguishes 'still held by another process' from 'access denied' and tells the operator how to identify the holder via netsh http show servicestate view=requestq.

feat(api): new POST /api/gateway/restart route. Stops the gateway (idempotent) and immediately calls Start; emits a Telemetry::Gateway event with severity matching the resulting state (Info on success, Error on failure). Pre-v0.9.3 a wedged gateway could only be recovered by knowing the /api/gateway/start route and not having Stop()'d first.

feat(host): MasterControlServiceHost.exe takes a Global\MasterControlOrchestrationServerSingleton mutex at the top of both runConsoleMode() and runServiceMain(). A second invocation -- typically --console while the SCM service is up -- exits with code 2 and writes 'Refusing to start a duplicate instance' to stderr + the service log. Pre-v0.9.3 the duplicate process silently failed to bind 7300/8080 but its WorkerSupervisor still spawned the baseline-tools worker, doubling the child-process count per host.

feat(runtime): TelemetryAggregator construction moved BEFORE gateway auto-start. Pre-v0.9.3 the auto-start fired with telemetryAggregator_ still null, which silently dropped the boot telemetry event AND meant a Failed boot status was invisible to the activity ring. Boot failures now record as Severity::Error in /api/activity so the operator sees them without querying /api/gateway/status.

feat(shell): Settings tab 'Bind Address' field renamed to 'LAN Address' and paired with a detected-IP dropdown built from GetAdaptersAddresses. Selecting an entry copies the raw address into the textbox; Apply now writes both bindAddress AND activeProfile.preferredBindAddress so the discovery doc URL builder can't silently override the operator's pick. Pre-v0.9.3 the operator's bind-address change updated only one field and was masked by auto-detection in every advertised URL.

feat(runtime): DiscoveryService::currentDocument precedence chain reordered -- explicit (non-wildcard) bindAddress beats preferredBindAddress beats auto-detected primaryIpAddress. bindAddress is where MCOS actually accepts() connections, so it is the only address LAN clients can actually reach; advertising anything else creates a discovery <-> listener mismatch.

fix(defaults): MasterControlDefaults::detectPrimaryNetworkIdentity now does the same IPv4-first three-pass adapter walk used by the runtime telemetry detector. Pre-v0.9.3 the bootstrapper had its own IPv6-first detector AND NetworkIdentity::ipAddress defaulted to literal '127.0.0.1' (so the empty-string short-circuit my pass-1 code relied on never fired). Result on every dual-stack install: persisted preferredBindAddress=127.0.0.1, discovery doc advertises loopback, LAN clients on other machines get connection-refused. Both halves fixed: detector matches runtime IPv4-first behavior; struct default is empty string.

fix(runtime): SimpleHttpServer (the admin port 7300 listener) now does a dual-stack IPv6 bind with IPV6_V6ONLY=0 when bindAddress is wildcard ('0.0.0.0', '::', or empty), so the same socket accepts both IPv4 (as ::ffff:1.2.3.4) and native IPv6 connections. Pre-v0.9.3 the wildcard path got AF_UNSPEC from getaddrinfo and the IPv4 wildcard sorted first in the result list, so the listener bound IPv4-only and any IPv6 URL the discovery doc advertised was unreachable. A specific (non-wildcard) bindAddress still goes through the per-family path so an operator-pinned IPv4 produces a clean IPv4-only listener.

fix(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::ListTools() now refreshes the cache on the first call when the cache is empty and the worker bridge is attached. Pre-v0.9.3 ListTools returned the cached vector verbatim, which started empty -- so /api/gateway/tools (the operator-facing debug surface) reported [] on a fresh boot even though the supervised pool was Ready and serving the LAN-client /mcp tools/list path correctly.

fix(api): /api/client/mcp-servers and /api/client/sub-agents now also surface live WorkerSupervisor pools with at least one Ready instance (synthesizing a non-template RuntimeEndpoint per pool). Pre-v0.9.3 these surfaces filtered to non-template entries from the inventory only, which left the v0.9.1 baseline-tools pool invisible because pool registration is independent of inventory templating. LAN clients consulting these catalogs to choose what to call now see what is actually serving traffic.

fix(runtime): baseline-tools pool's scalePolicy.maxActiveLeasesPerInstance raised from 4 to 64, maxInstances from 1 to 2. Pre-v0.9.3 the lease ceiling throttled bursty parallel tools/call to 4 successes + N failures even though each call is a microsecond stdio round-trip; observed 6/10 failures on a 10-parallel mcos.add stress test. Post-fix: 20/20 pass.

fix(activity): gateway lifecycle events (auto-start at boot + /api/gateway/{start,stop,restart} requests) now also append to the global activity ring, not just the TelemetryAggregator. Pre-v0.9.3 /api/activity (the operator's audit log) was telemetry-aggregator-blind, so gateway boots and restarts were invisible to anyone reading the activity surface.
fix(gateway): MCP initialize handshake's serverInfo.version now reports MASTERCONTROL_VERSION instead of the hard-coded literal '0.7.2' (a relic from when the native gateway adapter was first added).
docs(version): bump 0.9.2 -> 0.9.3 (LAN-client connectivity blockers + operability fixes; patch bump per scheme)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-13 follow-on: per-instance pool telemetry charts at 60Hz via Win2D CanvasControl; activity stream as scrolling SwapChainPanel.

23-server operator-facing catalog (filesystem, memory, sequential-thinking, ...) is still advertised by name only -- baseline-tools is a single in-tree pool exposing four meta-tools. Operator-bound external workers for the 23 named pools land in v0.9.x+.

history.summary: Settings tab: replace the four resource-allocation entry boxes (CPU / Memory / Bandwidth / Storage) with a Slider + small TextBox pair per allocation. Operator directive: 'replace the resource allocation entry boxes with sliders and a small box for entering exact amount.' Pre-v0.9.2 each allocation was a single free-form TextBox that accepted any text and only validated 0..100 at Apply time -- typos surfaced as 'Resource allocation values must stay between 0 and 100' instead of being prevented at the input. v0.9.2 makes the Slider the source of truth (it cannot produce out-of-range values; Minimum=0, Maximum=100, StepFrequency=1, TickFrequency=10 with outside ticks for visual reference) and pairs each Slider with a 64px right-aligned TextBox (MaxLength=3) for exact entry. The two are kept in lockstep two-way: dragging the slider updates the textbox, typing a valid integer in the textbox snaps the slider to that value. Apply, Summary, and snapshot-hydration all read from the Slider so a half-typed textbox value never reaches the admin API. The dirty/Apply enabled flow is preserved -- editing either control still flips dirty_ and re-renders the summary line (CPU/RAM/Bandwidth/Storage percentages).
entries: feat(shell): SettingsSectionControl.xaml replaces each of the four resource-allocation StackPanels (CpuAllocationTextBox / MemoryAllocationTextBox / BandwidthAllocationTextBox / StorageAllocationTextBox) with a two-column inner Grid: column 1 is a Slider (Minimum=0, Maximum=100, StepFrequency=1, SmallChange=1, LargeChange=10, TickFrequency=10, TickPlacement=Outside), column 2 is a 64px-wide TextBox (TextAlignment=Right, MaxLength=3). The same x:Name values are kept on the TextBoxes for back-compat with code-behind references; new x:Name values *Slider are added for the sliders.

feat(shell): two new event handlers in SettingsSectionControl.xaml.cpp -- AllocationSlider_ValueChanged and AllocationTextBox_TextChanged. Each looks up the partner control by x:Name (e.g., CpuAllocationSlider <-> CpuAllocationTextBox), pushes the rounded integer percentage onto the partner under a syncingAllocation_ guard so the partner's change event does not bounce back, then flips dirty_ and re-renders summary/state. Mid-typed textbox values that don't parse leave the slider alone so the operator can type freely.

feat(shell): PopulateEditorFromSnapshot now drives both Slider.Value and TextBox.Text from the snapshot percentage in lockstep, clamping 0..100 against a stale config that violates the schema. UpdateSummary and ApplySettingsAsync read percentages exclusively from Slider.Value (the source of truth), so a half-edited textbox cannot leak an invalid value into the admin API call.

feat(shell): SettingsSectionControl.xaml.h declares the two new handlers and adds syncingAllocation_ guard flag (independent of suspendDirtyTracking_, which has a different scope -- snapshot-load vs slider/textbox sync).
docs(version): bump 0.9.1 -> 0.9.2 (Settings UX refinement; patch bump per scheme)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-13 follow-on: per-instance pool telemetry charts at 60Hz via Win2D CanvasControl; activity stream as scrolling SwapChainPanel.

23-server operator-facing catalog (filesystem, memory, sequential-thinking, ...) is still advertised by name only -- baseline-tools is a single in-tree pool exposing four meta-tools. Operator-bound external workers for the 23 named pools land in v0.9.x+.

history.summary: Closes the three deferred items from v0.9.0's known-issues list. (1) Empty tools catalog: MCOS now ships an in-tree native MCP worker (mcos-baseline-tools-worker.exe) registered as a managed pool 'baseline-tools' that auto-spawns at runtime boot via WorkerSupervisor::ensureMinInstances. The worker speaks line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout (matching WorkerSupervisor::sendStdioJsonRpc's framing) and exposes four tools: mcos.echo, mcos.now, mcos.host_info, mcos.add. The gateway's tools/list now returns these four tools (qualified as baseline-tools__mcos.echo, etc.) on a fresh install with zero operator action. tools/call dispatches end-to-end through gateway -> stdio bridge -> worker -> response. The 23-server operator-facing catalog inventory remains untouched; operators bind real implementations to those pools as they deploy them. (2) Ping method: NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::handleMcpRequest now handles JSON-RPC method 'ping' (between 'initialize' and 'tools/list') and returns the spec-compliant empty-result envelope. Pre-v0.9.1 the gateway returned -32601 which made every MCP-client liveness probe count as a failed call in the activity log. (3) legacy adapter removal: the original v0.6.x supervised-binary adapter is fully deleted from include/MasterControl/McpGatewayAdapters.h, src/MasterControlApp/McpGatewayAdapters.cpp, and tests/MasterControlOrchestrationServerTests.cpp. The honest-503 placeholder listener that lived inside it is gone too -- the native substrate has been the only adapter constructed by the runtime since v0.9.0, so the placeholder was unreachable. the legacy GatewayType enum slot is retained for back-compat config deserialization (operator JSON still carrying type='native' continues to round-trip without rejection; the runtime ignores the value and constructs the native substrate). winhttp.lib / winhttp.h dropped from McpGatewayAdapters.cpp because the only WinHTTP code path lived inside the deleted legacy adapter's HTTP probe.
entries: feat(worker): new in-tree native MCP worker mcos-baseline-tools-worker.exe (src/MasterControlBaselineToolsWorker/main.cpp). Speaks line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout. Implements MCP methods initialize, ping, tools/list, tools/call. Exposes four tools: mcos.echo (echo back text -- proves end-to-end gateway routing), mcos.now (return UTC ISO-8601 timestamp -- proves real Win32 access), mcos.host_info (return hostname/OS/architecture/CPU count -- proves the worker is observing live state), mcos.add (sum two integers -- proves typed argument round-trip). Linked against nlohmann_json. Built under MSVC /W4 /WX /permissive- /EHsc /utf-8. Installed alongside the main MCOS executables to %INSTALLFOLDER%/mcos-baseline-tools-worker.exe.

feat(runtime): MasterControlRuntime now force-upserts the 'baseline-tools' pool every boot. Pool template_.executable resolves against paths_.executableDirectory so MSI MajorUpgrade relocations don't leave a stale path. minInstances=1, maxInstances=1, maxActiveLeasesPerInstance=4. After all pools are hydrated, the runtime calls ensureMinInstances(poolId) for every pool with minInstances>=1 so the supervised child actually spawns at boot rather than waiting for an operator-issued POST /api/pools/{id}/scale. Failure modes (binary missing, CreateProcessW error) leave the pool in supervised-mock or Failed state per ADR-002 §9 -- no fabricated success.

feat(gateway): NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter::handleMcpRequest now handles JSON-RPC method 'ping' returning the spec-compliant empty-result envelope. Pre-v0.9.1 the gateway returned -32601 'Method not implemented: ping' which made every MCP-client liveness probe count as a failed call in /api/activity even though the gateway was demonstrably alive.

refactor(gateway): the legacy external-substrate adapter deleted -- class declaration removed from include/MasterControl/McpGatewayAdapters.h, full implementation (~510 lines including the WinHTTP probe and the honest-503 placeholder listener loop) removed from src/MasterControlApp/McpGatewayAdapters.cpp. The header now declares only NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter and FakeMcpGatewayAdapter. Tombstone comment in the .cpp documents the removal so a `git log -- McpGatewayAdapters.cpp` still tells the operator why the adapter is gone.

refactor(gateway): McpGatewayAdapters.cpp drops <winhttp.h> and the winhttp.lib pragma. The only WinHTTP code path lived inside the deleted legacy adapter's HTTP probe; the surviving native adapter does an in-process state check for Probe() and uses HTTP.sys (httpapi.lib) for listening. Drops <filesystem> include for the same reason. Saves a transitive dep at link time.

test: testRealAdapterDisabledByDefault rewritten to construct a NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter (the surviving production substrate) instead of the deleted legacy adapter, asserting type='native' and that Start() with enabled=false returns Disabled state without binding HTTP.sys. testRealAdapterRegistrationSurvivesAcrossStartStop switched to FakeMcpGatewayAdapter so the IMcpGateway registration contract is exercised without requiring HTTP.sys URL ACL or admin rights in the test environment. testRealAdapterSupervisedMockWhenBinaryMissing removed -- it tested the deleted legacy adapter's supervised-mock binary path, which no longer exists. testGatewayConfigJsonRoundTrip and testDiscoveryDocumentJsonRoundTrip kept asserting type='native' string round-trips so the back-compat schema acceptance is covered.
fix(runtime): IMcpGateway logical-pool registration's X-MCOS-Gateway-Source header value corrected to 'native' (the substrate has been native-only since v0.9.0; the header was stale).
docs(version): bump 0.9.0 -> 0.9.1 (closes all three v0.9.0 known-issue items; no architecture change, patch bump per scheme)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-13 follow-on: per-instance pool telemetry charts at 60Hz via Win2D CanvasControl; activity stream as scrolling SwapChainPanel.

23-server operator-facing catalog (filesystem, memory, sequential-thinking, ...) is still advertised by name only -- baseline-tools is a single in-tree pool exposing four meta-tools. Operator-bound external workers for the 23 named pools land in v0.9.x+.

history.summary: MCP Gateway Module: native HTTP.sys substrate replaces the legacy external gateway. Per maintainer directive, the external supervised-binary gateway is dropped and replaced by a custom in-process substrate. v0.9.0 makes NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter (already present since PHASE-12 / v0.6.10 as a Win32 HTTP.sys-based IMcpGateway) the only gateway substrate. The runtime no longer constructs the legacy external adapter under any condition; persisted configs that still carry the legacy mcpGateway.type slug transparently resolve to the native substrate (the GatewayType enum is retained only so old JSON deserializes without rejection). McpGatewayConfiguration's defaults flip: type=Native, enabled=true. The runtime auto-starts the gateway at boot when enabled=true so a fresh install is self-hosting its MCP gateway out of the box -- no operator click required. /api/discovery and /api/onboarding/<client> URLs are rebuilt against the resolved LAN IP rather than the literal listenHost (which is '0.0.0.0' by default), so the v0.7.7 known issue ('Discovery doc still serves the literal listen URL when the gateway is bound to a wildcard') is closed. IPv6 literals get RFC 3986 brackets in the rebuilt URL. binaryPath / databasePath fields stay in McpGatewayConfiguration only so back-compat reads of v0.8.x configs don't fail; the runtime ignores them.
entries: feat(gateway): MCP Gateway Module is now the native HTTP.sys IMcpGateway implementation. The legacy external supervised-binary adapter is no longer constructed by the runtime; the dispatch in MasterControlRuntime.cpp went from a runtime switch between substrates to unconditionally constructing NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter. The legacy adapter's source files stayed in the tree for one release as inert dead code so any out-of-tree consumer would break loudly rather than silently; full removal landed in v0.9.1.

feat(gateway): McpGatewayConfiguration defaults changed: type=Native (was the legacy external slot), enabled=true (was false). binaryPath + databasePath fields kept as no-ops for back-compat config deserialization.

feat(gateway): MasterControlRuntime auto-starts the gateway at boot when configuration.mcpGateway.enabled is true. Pre-v0.9.0 the gateway only started on an explicit POST /api/gateway/start; a freshly installed system reported state='disabled' until the operator clicked. v0.9.0 records a Telemetry::Gateway 'Gateway auto-started at boot' event into the events ring on first boot.

fix(gateway): /api/discovery's gateway.mcpUrl + gateway.healthUrl are now composed from the resolved LAN IP (substituting whenever listenHost is '0.0.0.0' / '::' / '[::]' / empty) rather than from the adapter's literal composeMcpUrl() output. IPv6 literals get RFC 3986 brackets. /api/onboarding/<client>.gatewayMcpUrl inherits the same fix because the OnboardingProfileService composes against document.gateway.mcpUrl. configSnippets in claude-code/codex/grok/chatgpt/generic snippets all read profile.gatewayMcpUrl, so the JSON a real LAN client copies into its mcp.json now points at a URL that actually resolves.
docs(version): bump 0.8.7 -> 0.9.0 (gateway substrate change is meaningful; minor bump per scheme)
knownIssuesDeferredToNextRelease: PHASE-13 follow-on: per-instance pool telemetry charts at 60Hz via Win2D CanvasControl; activity stream as scrolling SwapChainPanel.

Catalog ports 7101-7125 are advertised but no worker processes are bound yet. v0.9.x+ will land worker registration scripts (CreateProcessW + Job Object supervision per ADR-002 §7) for the 23 baseline MCP servers.
NativeHttpSysGatewayAdapter source files retained as dead code for one release; removal in v0.9.1.

Native gateway accepts MCP JSON-RPC requests but the tools/list catalog is empty until backend MCP server processes are bound. Initialize handshake + ping work; tools/call returns a clean -32603 'no backend' error today.

history.summary: Overview tab gets a status grid + dedicated Error Reporting frame. Operator wanted: status of APIs and services, security stance, MCP server availability, sub-agent availability -- and a dedicated error reporting frame with JSON export of the errors associated with the displayed entries. v0.8.7 replaces the AUTHORED SURFACES legend footer with a 2x2 grid of cards: APIs and Services (service / admin API / browser surface / beacon / bind), Security Stance (security protocols / AI autonomy / open LAN access / governance posture / live findings), MCP Servers (count + reachable + first-4 preview), Sub-Agents (count + reachable + active leases + first-4 preview). Each card has a status dot in the title row colored good (green) / warn (amber) / crit (red) / neutral (gray) by the same rules used on the Telemetry-tab dots. Below the grid sits the Error Reporting frame: a scrolling list of recent error-flagged events with a per-row severity dot, kind/timestamp, message, and source. An Export to JSON button serializes the visible set to %PUBLIC%\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\error-export-<timestamp>.json -- mcos.error-export.v1 schema: { exportedAtUtc, count, events:[{id,timestampUtc,kind,severity,source,statusCode,message,detail}] }. Errors are harvested by ShellRuntime::CaptureSnapshot from /api/activity, filtered to events with statusCode>=400 OR kind/message containing error/failure/Unable. Capped at 50 most recent.
entries: feat(shell): ShellErrorEvent struct + ShellSnapshot.errorEvents in ShellRuntime.h. Each event carries id, timestampUtc, kind, severity (warn/error/critical inferred from statusCode), source (actor + method + target), statusCode, message, detail.

feat(shell): ShellRuntime::CaptureSnapshot now harvests recent errors at the tail of the snapshot pass. Calls FetchActivityEvents(L""), filters to statusCode>=400 OR kind/message containing 'error'/'failure'/'failed'/'Unable' or starting with 'ERROR'/'FATAL'. Caps the result at 50 entries.

feat(shell): OverviewSectionControl.xaml replaces the AUTHORED SURFACES legend footer with a 2x2 status grid (APIs & Services / Security Stance / MCP Servers / Sub-Agents) and a dedicated Error Reporting frame. Status dot per card. Headline + body text for each.

feat(shell): OverviewSectionControl::ApplyApisAndServicesCard / ApplySecurityStanceCard / ApplyMcpServersCard / ApplySubAgentsCard / ApplyErrorReportingCard all called from ApplySnapshot every tick. Tone helpers: pressureTone / populationTone / findingsTone equivalents inlined as good/warn/crit/neutral selection per card semantics.

feat(shell): Error Reporting list rebuilt imperatively from snapshot.errorEvents -- per-row severity dot, kind/timestamp eyebrow, message body (HTTP status prefix included when available), source (actor + method + target) muted footer.

feat(shell): ExportErrorsButton_Click serializes the cached lastErrorEvents_ to %PUBLIC%\Documents\Master Control Orchestration Server\error-export-<YYYYmmddTHHMMSS>.json. Schema 'mcos.error-export.v1' with exportedAtUtc + count + events[]. UTF-8 written via CreateFileW + WriteFile. ErrorExportStatusText shows the resulting file path or a failure message.
docs(version): bump 0.8.6 -> 0.8.7 (Overview status grid + Error Reporting frame with JSON export)

history.summary: QUICK ACTIONS panel removed from the hero. Operator flagged it as redundant with the section navigation strip (Overview / Telemetry / Runtime / Imports / Exports / Security / Settings / CLU) which sits directly below the hero and provides identical destination switching. v0.8.6 drops the entire middle hero column. Layout collapses from 3 columns (command deck + telemetry / quick actions / live operations) to 2 columns (command deck + telemetry / live operations). The Forsetti toolbar host x:Name is preserved as a Visibility=Collapsed StackPanel so ApplySurfaceToolbar's existing populator logic continues to compile and execute without behavior change -- the toolbar items still get appended each tick, just into an invisible host. The GuidedWorkflowStatusText x:Name keeps the same treatment (preserved for status writes, not visible).
entries: feat(shell): MainWindow.xaml hero column count reduced from 3 to 2. The middle column previously hosted QUICK ACTIONS (Forsetti toolbar) + the v0.8.5 collapsed wizard placeholder. Both removed visually. ForsettiToolbarHost StackPanel + GuidedWorkflowStatusText TextBlock parented under a single Visibility=Collapsed StackPanel at column 0 so existing C++ x:Name accessors resolve and the populator code in ApplySurfaceToolbar / status-update paths is unchanged.

feat(shell): MainWindow.xaml LIVE OPERATIONS column shifted from Grid.Column='2' to Grid.Column='1' to occupy the second of the two remaining hero columns. Live Operations (HeroOperationsHeadlineText / HeroOperationsBodyText / HOST IDENTITY / GOVERNANCE POSTURE / RUNTIME LEDGER) gets a wider visual share now that the middle column is gone.

history.summary: Host Controls and Guided Setup Wizards moved exclusively to the Settings tab. Pre-v0.8.5 both panels lived in MainWindow.xaml's persistent hero column -- they appeared on every section view (Overview, Runtime, Imports, Exports, Security, Settings, CLU). v0.7.9 hid them on Telemetry only. Operator wanted them gone from everywhere except Settings. v0.8.5 removes both panels from MainWindow.xaml entirely and adds them to the top of SettingsSectionControl.xaml. The buttons emit action tokens via the existing actionRequested_ callback (already wired into Settings via AttachRuntime since v0.6.4); MainWindow's section-creation lambda now dispatches both 'host-control:*' tokens (refresh / start-service / stop-service / open-dashboard / open-config / open-data) and bare workflow ids (new-mcp, guided-runtime-maintenance, etc.) to the right handler methods.
entries: feat(shell): MainWindow.xaml: HostControlsPanel + GuidedSetupWizardsPanel removed from the hero column. Same for New MCP Server / Manage Runtime Lanes / New Sub-Agent / New Sub-Agent Group / New Apple Host / Manage Forsetti Modules / Guided Import / Security Hardening / Host Settings.

Each Button stamps its action token onto its Tag attribute (e.g. 'host-control:refresh' or 'new-mcp') and routes through HostControlButton_Click / GuidedWizardButton_Click.

Bare tokens still route to StartGuidedWorkflow as before; tokens prefixed with 'host-control:' route to RefreshAsync / RunServiceActionAsync / HandleOpenDashboardAsync / OpenConfigButton_Click / OpenDataButton_Click. Single gateway between section-emitted tokens and MainWindow's existing handler methods.

history.summary: MCP server catalog replaced with the operator-specified 23-server minimum baseline. Pre-v0.8.4 the seed inventory carried 18 generic placeholder names (repo-search / docs-search / fs-cache / build-cache / symbol-index / session-context / response-cache / git-intel / file-digest / vector-search / dep-graph / lint-cache / snippet-store / task-queue / memory / agent-comm / coordination / event-bus) plus 2 reserved entries (client-tracker, metrics). Operator listed the 23 MCP servers they actually need: Filesystem, Memory, Sequential Thinking, Computer Use, Desktop Control, Playwright, Chrome DevTools, Terminal/Shell, Local Git, SQLite, Local Database, Docker Control, File Search, Code Execution/REPL, Local Test Runner, Screen Capture/Vision, Keyboard & Mouse Control, Persistent Context, Local Build Tool, Local Indexer, File Watcher, Knowledge Graph, Local Linter. v0.8.4 replaces the placeholder catalog wholesale in MasterControlDefaults.cpp's seed-endpoint loop. Each entry has a stable kebab-case id, an operator-readable displayName, a category-style specialization (filesystem / memory / reasoning / automation / browser / shell / vcs / database / container / execution / testing / vision / build / indexer / quality), and a short description that fills the muted card body line. Ports 7101-7125 (skipping 7120-7121 already used by client-tracker / metrics).

Each entry carries id (e.g. 'filesystem' / 'sequential-thinking' / 'docker-control'), displayName ('Filesystem MCP' / etc.), specialization category, and description. The MCP Servers card grid (v0.8.3) now headlines '23 MCP servers registered' with operator-meaningful names that match the operator's expected baseline. Reachability dots stay red until a real worker process binds to the listed port -- the catalog is advertisement, not pretense, and the v0.8.2 honest-probe model is unchanged.

history.summary: MCP servers now have card surfaces matching sub-agents. Pre-v0.8.3 only sub-agents got cards with utilization bars / reachability dots / active-client lists (v0.7.6 + v0.7.8) -- the 17+ MCP servers in the inventory were rendered as flat tables only. Operator pointed out that every MCP server deserves the same telemetry surface. v0.8.3 mirrors the entire pipeline: a parallel McpServerRuntimeStat struct on the runtime side, populated by the same probe + pool-lookup loop that produces SubAgentRuntimeStat; a parallel mcpServerRuntimeStats array on DashboardSnapshot; a parallel renderMcpServerUtilizationPanel + renderMcpServerCard on the browser side (which delegates to the existing renderSubAgentCard since the visual is identical); a parallel ShellMcpServerRuntimeStat parser in ShellRuntime; a new MCP Servers card grid section in the WinUI shell's Runtime tab driven by RuntimeSectionControl::PopulateMcpServerCards, which calls a templated renderEndpointStatCardGrid helper that PopulateSubAgentCards now also routes through. Single shared 220-line card builder, two callers.
entries: feat(runtime): McpServerRuntimeStat struct in MasterControlModels.h with the exact same field set as SubAgentRuntimeStat (utilization, reachability, host:port, active-client list). DashboardSnapshot gains mcpServerRuntimeStats. NLOHMANN serializer added.

feat(runtime): AdminApiService::snapshot adds a parallel loop for endpoints where kind == EndpointKind::McpServer. Same getaddrinfo-driven probe (v0.8.2), same pool lookup, same active-lease harvest. Diagnostics telemetry now emits both subAgentStats + mcpServerStats counts.

feat(dashboard-ui): renderMcpServerUtilizationPanel mirrors renderSubAgentUtilizationPanel; renderMcpServerCard delegates to renderSubAgentCard with a synthetic subAgentId field so the card visual is byte-identical. Runtime tab now shows both card grids stacked above their respective inventory tables.

feat(shell): ShellMcpServerRuntimeStat + ShellMcpServerLeaseHolder structs added. ShellRuntime::CaptureSnapshot's dashboard parser block now parses mcpServerRuntimeStats with the same field-by-field copy as subAgentRuntimeStats.
feat(shell): RuntimeSectionControl.xaml gains a new MCP Servers card grid section above the existing Sub-Agents grid. McpServersCardStack + McpServersHeadlineText x:Names declared.

PopulateSubAgentCards and PopulateMcpServerCards are now thin adapters that pass per-kind X:Name'd elements + per-kind labels into the shared template. Stat-kind id resolution is via overloaded endpointStatId(stat) helpers (subAgentId vs mcpServerId).

history.summary: Sub-agent reachability probe is no longer IPv4-only. Pre-v0.8.2 AdminApiService::snapshot's per-sub-agent reachability probe used a bare AF_INET socket + inet_pton(AF_INET, ...) -- the seven default inventory entries (sentinel, architect, forge, scribe, recon, nexus, watchtower) advertise IPv6 ULA hosts (fde3:c02c:3afa:4572:6687:be77:e9f1:32c8) so inet_pton silently failed parse and every dot painted red on a stock install. v0.8.2 rewrites the probe around getaddrinfo with AF_UNSPEC hints: every address family the resolver returns is tried until one connects or all exhaust the 200 ms-per-address timeout. IPv4, IPv6, and DNS-name hosts all probe correctly. endpointHostPort now bracket-wraps IPv6 literals per RFC 3986 (closing the v0.7.6 known issue) so the dashboard reads e.g. '[fde3:...:32c8]:7201' instead of the visually-ambiguous 'fde3:...:32c8:7201'.
entries: fix(runtime): AdminApiService::snapshot reachability probe rewritten on getaddrinfo. AF_UNSPEC hints accept both AF_INET and AF_INET6; the loop tries every resolved address with non-blocking connect + 200 ms select, succeeding on the first reachable family. Both fd_set writeSet and errorSet are passed to select so a connection refusal lands in error rather than write -- avoids spurious reachable=true on hosts that explicitly refused.
fix(runtime): endpointHostPort string now wraps IPv6 literals in brackets ('[fde3:...:32c8]:7201') per RFC 3986. v0.7.6 known issue (recorded in VERSION.json) is closed.
docs(version): bump 0.8.1 -> 0.8.2 (IPv6-aware sub-agent reachability probe + RFC 3986 IPv6 bracket-wrap)

history.summary: Three operator-driven changes landed in one release. (1) Application palette switched from Sam-Flynn cyan to Tron CLU red-orange. ShellAccentBrush moves from #00FFFF to #FF3D2E and every cyan-derived hex literal across App.xaml, MainWindow.xaml, every section's XAML, and the imperative ColorHelper.FromArgb calls in MainWindow.xaml.cpp / RuntimeSectionControl.xaml.cpp shifts to the matching red-orange tone. Status semantic colors (success green-mint, warning amber, danger pink-red) are intentionally preserved so a critical-state dot remains visually distinct from chrome on a red-themed UI. (2) Background curves replaced with a proper Tron grid. Pre-v0.8.1 the shell painted two large soft-stroke Ellipses behind the foreground content -- the operator called this out as 'not Tron styling due to the curves.' v0.8.1 removes both Ellipses and populates the same Canvas imperatively with a perpendicular line grid (vertical lines every 80 px, horizontal every 60 px, every fifth line emphasized in the soft accent brush) that resizes with the window via SizeChanged. (3) Cross-section drag on the Telemetry tab now works. v0.8.0 rejected drops where source and target lived in different section grids; v0.8.1 lifts that restriction by physically reparenting both tiles between section grids and swapping their attached Grid.Row / Grid.Column so the visual swap looks the same as a same-section drag. Persistence now records the sectionId per tile so a cross-section move survives shell restart -- on Loaded LoadAndApplyLayout reparents any tile whose persisted sectionId differs from its catalog default. Also creates docs/agent-decision-log.md as a session-durable, git-versioned memory anchor for the agent driving these releases.
entries: feat(shell): App.xaml palette swap. ShellAccentBrush #00FFFF -> #FF3D2E, ShellTileEdgeBrush #6600FFFF -> #66FF3D2E, ShellAccentSoftBrush / ShellGlowBrush / ShellHotGlowBrush / ShellGridlineBrush / ShellCardEdgeBrush / ShellAccentBrightBrush all rebased on the new red. ShellBackgroundBrush gradient and ShellHeroBrush retinted from cyan-blacks to red-blacks (#1A0405 / #3A0808 / #220A0A / #2A0D0D). Text brushes pivoted from cool (#9ECCDA / #7BA4B0 / #F0FFFF) to warm (#E8B89E / #B68870 / #FFEEDC) so foreground text sits properly on a red-toned background.

feat(shell): Bulk-replaced 30+ cyan hex literals across App.xaml's WinUI Fluent overrides (Button / ToggleButton / ComboBox / TextBox / ToggleSwitch / ListView / NavigationView / Flyout / MenuFlyout / ContentDialog / ScrollBar / ProgressBar) and all the inline #00DFFF / #1000DFFF / #1400DFFF / #2200DFFF washes in MainWindow.xaml. ColorHelper.FromArgb(0x..., 0x00, 0xf6, 0xff) calls in MainWindow.xaml.cpp::ApplySubAgentFooter and RuntimeSectionControl.xaml.cpp's sub-agent card builder swapped to (0x..., 0xff, 0x3d, 0x2e). No remaining cyan literals anywhere under src/MasterControlShell.

feat(shell): Tron grid backdrop. MainWindow.xaml's decorative Canvas had two large Ellipses (the curves operator flagged) plus two single-line gridlines. Replaced with x:Name='ShellGridCanvas' populated by MainWindow::BuildShellGridBackdrop on Loaded and SizeChanged. Grid: vertical lines every 80 px, horizontal every 60 px, every 5th line painted in ShellAccentSoftBrush at 1.4 px stroke for emphasis. The grid covers the full Canvas size (clamped to a sensible default until the window is realized) and hit-test-invisible so it never intercepts pointer events.

fix(shell): Telemetry tab cross-section drag is no longer rejected. Tile_Drop now physically reparents source and target between section grids when their parent Grids differ -- source moves into target's section grid taking target's row/col, target moves into source's old grid taking source's old row/col. Same-section drops still execute the in-place attached-property swap. tileLayout_ entries swap their sectionId fields so PersistLayout records the cross-section move.

fix(shell): TelemetrySectionControl::LoadAndApplyLayout now physically reparents tiles on startup when the persisted sectionId differs from the grid that currently owns the tile (i.e. cross-section moves survive shell restart). ResolveSectionGridForTile and ResolveSectionIdForTile prefer the live tileLayout_ state's sectionId over the static catalog metadata so all subsequent operations (drag, detach, reattach, hide) route to the new section.

feat(repo): docs/agent-decision-log.md added as session-durable memory anchor. Captures operator directives, palette decisions, deferral notes, and TODOs for the agent so context-window compaction can rehydrate from the file plus VERSION.json plus the git log. No off-the-shelf MCP memory connector found in the registry; file-system + git is the durable store.

history.summary: Telemetry tab is now operator-customizable. Pre-v0.8.0 the Telemetry view was a fixed three-section grid: Live Host Pressure (6 tiles), Resource Allocation (4 tiles), Operational Activity (5 tiles), with positions baked into XAML. v0.8.0 makes every tile (1) togglable via a Customize Layout flyout that lists every tile with a checkbox, (2) drag-reorderable inside its section by clicking and dragging the tile to another tile's position (positions swap), and (3) detachable to its own desktop window via a small detach button on each tile's title row. Detached tiles continue updating at the same 1Hz cadence as the main grid because the live-snapshot tick now broadcasts to the cached Telemetry section regardless of which tab the operator is currently viewing. Layout state -- visibility, grid position, detach state, and detached window geometry -- persists to %ProgramData%\MasterControlOrchestrationServer\config\telemetry-layout.json so customization survives a shell restart. Reset to defaults restores the original section layout and re-attaches everything.
entries: feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml -- each of the 15 tile Borders gains x:Name + Tag + CanDrag + AllowDrop + DragStarting/DragOver/Drop handlers + a small detach (⤢) and hide (✕) button beside the existing label and status dot. Tag carries the tile's stable identifier (CpuTile, MemoryTile, ..., GovernanceServersTile) so handlers identify the source/target by string lookup.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml -- new Customize Layout card above the host-pressure section. Card hosts a Button with a Flyout that lists every tile with a CheckBox bound to its visible flag. A Reset to defaults button restores the original layout. The flyout repopulates after every visibility/detach change so the (detached) suffix on detached tiles stays current.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.h -- TelemetryTileLayoutState struct + tileLayout_ map + detachedWindows_ map. Layout map keyed by tile name, captures visible / detached / row / column / sectionId / detachedX / detachedY / detachedWidth / detachedHeight per tile. detachedWindows_ owns the Microsoft::UI::Xaml::Window for every currently-popped tile so the window stays alive until explicit close or reattach.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- drag/drop reorder via Tile_DragStarting (stamps source tile name onto the DataPackage), Tile_DragOver (authorizes Move operation visually), Tile_Drop (swaps Grid.Row / Grid.Column attached props between source and target tiles within the same section grid). Cross-section drags are rejected -- moving a tile between sections is done via Customize flyout + reattach so the operator never accidentally orphans a tile.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- DetachTileToDesktop(tileName) removes the tile Border from its parent section grid, builds a new Microsoft::UI::Xaml::Window with title 'MCOS Telemetry - <Tile Name>', body of [Re-attach button, tile content], and Activate()s the window. ReattachTile(tileName) reverses the operation: pulls the tile out of the detached window, restores Grid.Row/SetColumn from saved layout state, and re-appends to the section grid's Children. Window Closed event also calls Reattach so the operator-closes-window path returns the tile.

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- HideButton_Click sets the tile's Visibility to Collapsed and persists state.visible=false. ApplyTileVisibility centralizes the visibility flip. Customize flyout's CheckBox.Checked/Unchecked handlers route through OnTileVisibleCheckboxChanged which respects detached state (a detached + checked tile keeps showing in its window without re-spawning in the main grid).

feat(shell): TelemetrySectionControl.xaml.cpp -- LoadAndApplyLayout reads %ProgramData%\MasterControlOrchestrationServer\config\telemetry-layout.json on Loaded. Falls back to the v0.8.0 BuildDefaultLayout matrix (matches pre-v0.8.0 hardcoded positions) when the file is missing or older entries are absent. Applies Grid.SetRow/SetColumn + Visibility for every tile, then re-detaches any tile flagged detached=true. PersistLayout snapshots current Grid.Row/Column/Visibility per tile and writes a small custom-JSON document.

feat(shell): MainWindow.xaml.cpp -- ApplyCurrentSectionSnapshot now also feeds the cached Telemetry section a snapshot when the active destination is NOT telemetry. This keeps detached telemetry tiles updating at 1Hz from the operator's other tabs (Overview/Runtime/Settings/etc.). The cached view's ApplySnapshot updates the same x:Name'd inner controls (CpuValueText, CpuStatusDot, CpuProgressBar, ...) whether they're parented to the main shell grid or to a detached window's content tree.

Operator can still move tiles across sections via the Customize flyout (hide in source, reset to defaults, ...) but a true cross-section drag requires a unified flat layout (likely a future GridView refactor that drops the section-grid grouping entirely).
